"Because you can merge with beasts," Su Mei said. "Regular tamers can only guide their partners. They can't share their senses, their instincts, their power. But you can. You're the only one who might be able to sense the corruption's origin point and survive the journey there."

*"She's not wrong,"* the lightning hawk's voice chimed in. *"The corruption has a unique signature. I can taste it on the wind, even now. It comes from somewhere upwind, deeper in the swamp."*

Lin closed his eyes, extending his senses through his bonded beasts. The lightning hawk's perception merged with his own, and he felt it—a distant thrum, like a discordant note in an otherwise silent symphony. The corruption's heartbeat, echoing through the swamp.

"I can feel it," he said, opening his eyes. "But it's faint. I'd need to be closer to pinpoint the source."

Su Mei's face lit up with desperate hope. "Then you'll help us?"

Lin considered. The logical part of his mind screamed at him to refuse. He had three beasts now, but he was still only at the third level of the Beast Melding Realm. The Festering Heart would be crawling with corrupted beasts far stronger than the one he'd just killed.

But another part of him—the part that had driven him to bond with a crippled snake, to enter a temple of shadows, to push past every limit imposed on him—that part whispered something different.

*This is how you grow. This is how you become strong enough to protect everyone.*

"I'll help," Lin said. "But not for your sect. Not for the valley." He looked at his three beasts, feeling their presence in his soul like three distinct flames. "I'm doing this for them. And for myself."

Su Mei nodded, understanding in her eyes. "That's enough."

---

The journey to the Festering Heart took three days.

They moved through the swamp in careful formation, Su Mei's disciples taking point while Lin's beasts scouted ahead. The corruption grew thicker the deeper they went—twisted trees that wept black sap, pools of stagnant water that glowed with sickly light, beasts whose eyes burned with malevolent red fire.

Lin's triple merge proved invaluable. He could sense the corruption's presence before it became visible, redirecting the group around the worst concentrations. His shadow panther eliminated threats before they could raise alarms. His lightning hawk cleared the skies of corrupted birds. His wind-snake purged any corruption that touched their skin.

But on the third night, they reached the edge of something terrible.

The swamp ended abruptly, giving way to a vast clearing. In its center stood a structure that shouldn't exist—a temple of black stone, its surface covered in writhing symbols that seemed to move when Lin looked directly at them. The corruption emanated from it in visible waves, distorting the air like heat haze.

"The Festering Heart," Su Mei whispered, her face pale even in the dim light.

*"There's something inside,"* the shadow panther said, its mental voice tight with unease. *"Something old. Something that knows we're here."*

As if summoned by the words, the temple's doors swung open. Not outward, but inward—as if inviting them inside.

A figure emerged from the darkness within.

It was human-shaped, but wrong. Its skin was pale as bone, stretched tight over a frame that seemed too tall, too thin. Its eyes were empty sockets filled with swirling black mist. And when it spoke, its voice came from everywhere at once—from the ground, from the air, from inside Lin's own mind.

*"Finally,"* it said, its mouth stretching into a smile that split its face. *"I've been waiting for someone interesting."*

Lin's beasts snarled, pressing close to him. His hands balled into fists, power thrumming through his channels.

"Who are you?" he demanded. "What do you want?"

The figure tilted its head, as if amused by the question. *"I am what remains of the last Beast Resonance master,"* it said. *"And what I want... is you."*

Before Lin could respond, the world went black.

title: The Beast That Wears a Face
content:
Lin Xiao moved through the corrupted beast like a ghost made of razor wire.

The triple merge had transformed him into something that existed between moments. He saw the world in fragments—the beast's twisted spine, the black qi pulsing from its wounds, the terrified faces of the sect disciples huddled against the rock formation. All of it came to him through three pairs of eyes at once, filtered through three distinct minds.

The panther thought in shadows and patience.

The hawk thought in speed and precision.

The snake thought in stillness and sudden death.

And Lin thought in all three at once.

He struck. His hand, wreathed in shadow and crackling with lightning, punched through the corrupted beast's ribcage. The creature howled, black blood spraying across the swamp. It swung a massive claw at his head, but Lin was already gone, reforming twenty feet away with the panther's grace.

*"You move like a beast,"* the panther observed, its voice a cold whisper at the edge of his consciousness.

*"I am a beast,"* Lin replied. *"I just happen to look human."*

The corrupted beast staggered, its chest wound already knitting closed. Black qi swirled around the injury, accelerating the regeneration. Lin's eyes narrowed. Normal beasts didn't heal that fast. Normal beasts didn't have red eyes that burned like furnaces.

"What is this thing?" he called to the pinned disciples.

A young woman in blue silk robes, her face streaked with mud and tears, shouted back: "It was one of us! A senior disciple! He went into the Deep Marsh three weeks ago and came back—came back wrong!"

Lin's blood turned cold.

The corrupted beast turned toward the sound of her voice, and for the first time, Lin saw it clearly. Beneath the twisted flesh and broken bone, beneath the black veins and weeping sores, there was a face. A human face. Stretched and distorted, eyes bleeding crimson light, but human nonetheless.

*They're turning people into beasts.*

The thought hit him like a physical blow. The corruption wasn't just affecting spirit beasts. It was affecting beast tamers. And if it could transform a senior disciple into this monstrosity—

"Where did you find him?" Lin demanded, dodging another swipe. The beast was faster now, its regeneration complete. It was learning. Adapting.

The female disciple's face went pale. "The Whispering Stones. At the heart of the Deep Marsh. He said—he said he heard a voice calling to him from beneath the stones."

The shadow panther's presence sharpened in Lin's mind. *"I know that place. The Whispering Stones are older than this valley. Older than the sects. The beasts who go there do not return."*

*"And tamers who go there become this?"* Lin gestured at the corrupted creature.

*"It seems so."*

The beast lunged. Lin flowed around it, but he felt the strain now. The triple merge was powerful, but it demanded constant focus. The panther's shadow essence pulled him toward stealth, the hawk's lightning essence pushed him toward speed, the snake's venom essence whispered for him to strike and retreat. Holding all three in balance was like juggling fire, water, and lightning.

He needed to end this quickly.

*[Beast Resonance: Shadow Lightning Strike]*

Lin's body dissolved into a blur of darkness and electricity. He crossed the distance in less than a heartbeat, appearing directly in front of the corrupted beast. His hand, now more claw than human, drove into its chest. Not to wound this time—to find the core.

All beasts had cores. Spirit beasts, corrupted beasts, even human cultivators. The core was the source of power. Destroy it, and the creature died.

His fingers brushed against something hot and pulsing. The beast's core—but it felt wrong. Twisted. Like a bell that had been cracked and beaten back into shape.

*"What in the nine hells..."*

The beast's core wasn't a beast core. It was a human dantian—corrupted, mutated, but unmistakably human. This disciple's cultivation base had been forcibly converted into a beast core. He hadn't been turned *into* a beast. He had been merged *with* one.

Against their will.

Lin ripped his hand free, stumbling backward. The beast shrieked, but didn't pursue. Instead, it stood trembling, its massive body convulsing. The red light in its eyes flickered.

"Kill... me..."

The words came from the beast's throat, but they were human. Hoarse. Broken.

"Please..."

Lin's heart clenched. "Who did this to you?"

The beast's body contorted, flesh rippling like water. For a single moment, the human face beneath the corruption became clear—a young man, maybe twenty years old, his eyes full of pain and terror.

"The voice... underneath the stones... it's not a beast... it's a *cage*..."

The beast's body seized, the corruption surging back. The red light in its eyes flared brighter than before. Whatever humanity had been left in that shell was drowning.

"Kill me!" the beast howled, the voice breaking into a guttural roar. "Before—before it takes—AAAAARGH!"

The transformation completed. The last trace of the human face vanished beneath a mask of bone and black qi. The beast grew larger, its muscles bulging, its claws lengthening. The red eyes fixed on Lin with predatory intelligence.

*"The human is gone,"* the panther said. *"What remains is the corruption given flesh."*

*"Then I'll put it down."*

Lin raised his hand, calling on all three beasts. The shadow panther materialized behind the creature, claws extended. The lightning hawk circled overhead, electricity building between its wings. The wind-snake coiled at Lin's feet, ready to strike.

But before he could attack, something changed.

The corrupted beast stopped moving.

It stood perfectly still, its head tilted as if listening to something. Then, slowly, impossibly, it turned and *bowed*.

Lin's blood froze.

"What the—"

The beast spoke, but the voice that emerged was not human. It was ancient. Resonant. A voice that had been buried beneath the Whispering Stones for centuries.

*"Found you."*

The ground erupted.

Black tendrils of corruption burst from the swamp, wrapping around Lin's ankles, his wrists, his throat. They pulsed with malevolent energy, draining his strength. The triple merge shattered as the shadow panther and lightning hawk were violently disconnected from his consciousness.

Lin hit the ground hard, gasping.

*"A beast tamer who can merge with his beasts,"* the voice crooned, echoing from everywhere at once. *"I have not tasted such potential in a thousand years. You will make a fine vessel."*

The corrupted beast began to dissolve, its flesh sloughing off in ribbons of black qi. The tendrils dragged Lin toward the center of the swamp, toward the Whispering Stones.

Toward whatever had been waiting for him all along.

"No!" The female disciple lunged, drawing a talisman from her sleeve. She slapped it against the nearest tendril, and the paper burst into golden flame. The tendril shrieked, releasing Lin's arm.

But more tendrils rose to replace it.

"Run!" Lin shouted at her. "Get back to the sect! Warn them!"

"I won't leave you!"

"You'll die if you stay!"

The disciple's face hardened. She was young—maybe sixteen—but there was steel in her eyes. "My name is Song Mei. And I don't leave people behind."

She bit her thumb, drawing blood, and pressed it against her forehead. A seal flared to life, and her cultivation erupted—fifth level of the Beast Melding Realm. A golden crane materialized beside her, its feathers gleaming with spiritual light.

"Senior, I don't know who you are, but you saved us. Let me return the favor."

The golden crane launched itself at the tendrils, its beak slicing through the corruption like a blade. Song Mei grabbed Lin's arm and pulled him upright.

"Can you fight?"

Lin's three beasts were recovering, their connections re-forming. The shadow panther appeared beside him, its fur bristling with anger. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, sparks dancing along its feathers. The wind-snake coiled around his leg, its tongue flickering.

"I can always fight."

Song Mei nodded. "Good. Because we're not alone."

She pointed toward the Whispering Stones, and Lin's heart sank.

Figures were emerging from the mist. Dozens of them. All human, all wearing the robes of various sects. And all of their eyes burned with red light.

The voice from beneath the stones laughed.

*"You did not think I would let my congregation wander, did you? Every beast, every tamer, every soul that hears my voice—they become part of me. Part of the great unity."*

The corrupted humans advanced, their movements synchronized. They were no longer individuals. They were puppets. Tools.

And at the center of them all stood a figure that made Lin's blood run cold.

A boy, maybe twelve years old, wearing tattered robes. His eyes were closed, his face peaceful. But the corruption pouring from his body was denser than anything Lin had ever seen.

*"This one was special,"* the voice said, almost fondly. *"He heard my call before any other. He came to me willingly. And in return, I made him my vessel. My voice. My hands in this world."*

The boy opened his eyes.

They were not eyes. They were voids. Empty sockets filled with swirling darkness that seemed to pull at Lin's very soul.

"Lin Xiao," the boy said, and the voice was ancient and terrible. "You have something I want."

The boy raised his hand, and Lin felt his three beasts scream in protest as something tried to tear them from his soul.

"Your [Beast Resonance]," the boy continued. "Give it to me. Or I will take it from your corpse."

The corrupted disciples closed in, their red eyes fixed on Lin with hungry anticipation.

Song Mei's golden crane screeched a challenge.

The shadow panther growled, low and dangerous.

The lightning hawk's electricity crackled like thunder.

The wind-snake hissed, venom dripping.

And Lin Xiao, standing at the center of an army of the corrupted, felt something he had never felt before.

Fear.

But not the kind that broke him.

The kind that made him *dangerous*.

"Come and take it," he said, and his voice was calm. "I dare you."

The boy smiled, and it was the smile of something that had not been human for a very long time.

*"Finally,"* it whispered. *"A worthy opponent."*

The corrupted army attacked.

And the battle for Thousand Beast Valley began.

title: Chapter 10
content:
CHAPTER 10: The Price of Power

Three days. That's how long Lin had been running.

The corrupted beasts came in waves, each one stronger than the last. He'd killed twelve of them now, their twisted bodies littering the swamp behind him. But more kept coming, drawn by something he couldn't identify.

*"They're hunting you,"* the shadow panther's voice echoed in his mind. *"Not the sect disciples. You specifically."*

Lin ducked under a rotting claw, rolled, and drove his blade—reinforced with shadow essence—into the creature's skull. Black blood splattered across his face. He didn't bother wiping it off anymore.

"Why?" he gasped, already moving again. The lightning hawk circled above, scanning for the next threat. The wind-snake had grown quiet, coiled tightly around his arm in a protective stance.

*"You carry the corruption within you now."*

Lin froze mid-stride. "What?"

*"Beast Resonance. It's not just power you merge with, little tamer. It's everything. Their wounds. Their sickness. Their death."* The panther's voice was cold, clinical. *"The corrupted beasts sense the taint growing inside you. They see you as kin. And they want to bring you home."*

Lin looked at his hands. They were trembling. Not from exhaustion—he'd pushed past that hours ago. No, the tremors were deeper. His veins were darkening, the same sickly black he'd seen in the corrupted beast's blood.

No. No, no, no.

He ripped his sleeve back. His arm was a roadmap of corruption, black lines spreading from his wrist toward his shoulder like roots claiming soil.

"When did this start?"

*"The moment you merged with all three of us during that first corruption beast fight. The taint entered through the bond. You were too focused on survival to notice."*

Lin fell to his knees. The swamp water soaked through his pants, cold and indifferent.

Xiao Mei's face flashed in his mind. Her smile. Her faith in him. "You're special, Lin Xiao. I know it."

What would she say if she saw him now? A walking plague. A monster in the making.

A roar split the air. Something massive was approaching—the ground shook with each step, and the trees bent away from the pressure of its qi.

*"Rank 5,"* the lightning hawk shrieked, terror bleeding through the bond. *"Corrupted Thunder Ape. It's coming fast."*

Lin forced himself to stand. His legs felt like paper, but he stood.

"Can I still merge with you?" he asked. "Without making this worse?"

Silence.

*"The corruption doesn't care about your intentions,"* the shadow panther finally said. *"Each merge will accelerate the process. You might have a week before the taint reaches your core. Maybe less."*

"A week." Lin laughed—a hollow, broken sound. "That's more than I had yesterday."

He reached out with his will, activating [Beast Resonance]. The three beasts responded, their power flooding into him. But this time, he felt it. The corruption wasn't just in his body anymore. It was in the bond itself, twisting the connection, whispering promises of eternal power.

*Join us,* the voices hissed. *Become one with the corruption. No more pain. No more fear. Only strength.*

Lin gritted his teeth. "Shut up."

The Thunder Ape crashed through the trees, thirty feet of muscle and rage. Its fur was matted with black ichor, and lightning—corrupted lightning, black and purple—crackled around its fists. One eye was gone, replaced by a pulsing orb of corruption that seemed to stare directly into Lin's soul.

"Come on then," Lin said, shadow essence pooling around his feet, lightning sparking along his arms. "Let's see who breaks first."

---

The fight lasted seven seconds.

Lin moved like he'd never moved before. The shadow panther's stealth merged with the lightning hawk's speed, making him a blur of darkness and electricity. He appeared behind the ape, driving a shadow-claw into its spine. The creature roared, spinning, but Lin was already gone, reappearing above it, driving a thunderous kick into its skull.

The ape stumbled. Lin pressed the attack, his wind-snake's venom coating his blade as he slashed at the creature's exposed throat.

For a moment, he thought he'd won.

Then the corruption erupted.

Black tendrils shot out from the ape's wounds, wrapping around Lin's ankle before he could dodge. He hit the ground hard, the air leaving his lungs. The tendrils tightened, dragging him toward the ape's gaping maw.

*"Break them!"* the shadow panther snarled. *"Use our power!"*

Lin tried. He really did. But the corruption was inside him now, pulsing in rhythm with his heartbeat. It recognized the tendrils. It welcomed them.

*Let go,* the voices whispered. *Stop fighting. Accept what you're becoming.*

"No," Lin growled. "I won't."

He reached deep into himself, past the corruption, past the fear, past the exhaustion. He found the core of his [Beast Resonance] ability—the pure, untainted spark that had first bonded him to the wind-snake.

And he pushed.

The world shattered into white light.

---

Lin woke to silence.

He was lying in a crater, the Thunder Ape's body reduced to ash around him. His beasts were unconscious, their presence dim in his mind. His body felt like it had been torn apart and put back together wrong.

But he was alive.

"What... was that?"

*"You burned the corruption out,"* a new voice said. Not one of his beasts. This voice was ancient, resonant, like stone grinding against stone. *"Most would have died. You're either very lucky or very stubborn."*

Lin forced his eyes open. Standing at the edge of the crater was a figure—humanoid, but wrong. Its body was made of interlocking stone plates, and its eyes glowed with amber light. An earth elemental. But not a beast. Something older.

"Who are you?"

"I am the Warden." The elemental stepped closer, its weight causing the ground to tremble. "I guard the boundary between the corruption's domain and the living world. And you, little tamer, have been marked."

"Marked? By what?"

"The corruption has chosen you as its vessel. Every beast you bond with, every merge you perform, brings it closer to claiming your body as its avatar." The Warden's eyes flickered with something that might have been pity. "You have three choices. One: sever your bonds and live as a mortal. The corruption will leave you, but your cultivation will be destroyed."

Lin's hand went to his chest, where the wind-snake's bond pulsed. "No."

"I thought you'd say that. Two: complete your transformation. Embrace the corruption. Become its champion. You would be more powerful than any beast tamer in history."

"And lose myself in the process."

"Yes."

"What's the third option?"

The Warden was silent for a long moment. When it spoke, its voice was heavy with warning.

"Three: find the source. The Heart of Corruption lies deep within the Thousand Beast Valley, at the place where the first corruption beast was born. Destroy it, and the taint will die. But the journey will kill you. The corruption will fight you every step of the way, growing stronger the closer you get. And even if you reach it, destroying the Heart will require a sacrifice."

"What kind of sacrifice?"

The Warden leaned closer, its amber eyes boring into Lin's.

"Everything you are. Every bond you've formed. Every beast you've tamed. To destroy the Heart, you must burn your own soul as fuel. There will be nothing left. No reincarnation. No afterlife. Just emptiness."

Lin's hands were shaking again. The corruption lines on his arms had spread to his neck.

He thought of Xiao Mei. Of the lightning hawk's first joyful shriek. Of the shadow panther's grudging respect. Of the wind-snake, who had never given up on him even when its core was broken.

"Where is the Heart?"

The Warden's expression didn't change, but something shifted in its posture. Respect. Or maybe resignation.

"Follow the corruption. It will lead you home."

Lin climbed to his feet. His body screamed in protest. His beasts stirred weakly in his mind, sending pulses of concern.

"I'll do it," he said. "I'll destroy the Heart."

The Warden nodded slowly. "Then ready yourself, little tamer. The corruption knows you're coming now."

Behind the elemental, the swamp began to churn. Hundreds of red eyes glowed in the darkness, accompanied by the sound of wet, ragged breathing.

"You have company," the Warden said, and then it was gone, dissolving into the earth like it had never been there.

Lin looked at the approaching horde. Corrupted beasts of every shape and size, their bodies twisted and wrong, their minds consumed by the same taint that was eating him alive.

He smiled. It wasn't a happy smile.

"One week," he muttered, raising his hand. The shadow panther materialized beside him, groggy but ready. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, its feathers still smoking. The wind-snake uncoiled, its eyes gleaming with cold resolve.

[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge]

The corruption screamed inside him as the power flooded through. But this time, Lin didn't try to suppress it. He let it flow, let it become part of the merge.

If he was going to die anyway, he might as well use every tool at his disposal.

The horde charged.

Lin charged back.

And in the distance, deep beneath the Thousand Beast Valley, something ancient and malevolent opened its eyes for the first time in a thousand years.

title: The Elder's Betrayal
content:
Lin Xiao's body hummed with the combined power of three beasts as he faced the corrupted creature. The thing was massive—easily fifteen feet at the shoulder, its body a nightmarish patchwork of scales, fur, and exposed muscle. Black qi oozed from its wounds like blood from a rotting corpse.

*"This is no ordinary corruption,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"I have seen this before. In the old wars."*

"What do you mean?" Lin asked, circling the beast.

The creature swung a clawed arm the size of a tree trunk. Lin blurred, shadows carrying him beneath the strike. He came up on the beast's other side, lightning crackling along his forearm.

*"The corruption was seeded,"* the panther said. *"Deliberately. By a beast tamer."*

The words hit Lin like a physical blow. He stumbled, and the corrupted beast nearly caught him with a sweep of its tail. The wind-snake's instincts took over, coiling his body into an impossible contortion that saved him from being crushed.

"A beast tamer did this?"

*"Feel the corruption, little tamer. It has structure. Intent. This is not random decay—it is a weapon."*

The hunting party screamed as the beast turned its attention back to them. Lin moved without thinking, merging his three beasts into a single strike. Shadow wrapped around his fist. Lightning coated his knuckles. Venom dripped from his fingers.

He punched.

The impact sent shockwaves through the swamp. Water exploded upward. Trees cracked and fell. The corrupted beast's chest caved inward, black blood spraying across the clearing.

But the creature didn't fall.

It turned, and Lin saw something that made his blood run cold.

The corruption was *healing*. Black tendrils of qi were pulling the wound closed, knitting flesh and bone back together faster than any natural regeneration should allow.

*"The tamer who created this is nearby,"* the panther said. *"They are controlling it. Directing it."*

Lin's eyes swept the treeline. "Who's there? Show yourself!"

The corrupted beast roared and charged again. Lin dodged, but this time the creature was faster. Its claw caught him across the ribs, sending him tumbling through the mud. Pain exploded through his chest. He felt blood—his blood—warm and wet against his torn robes.

The disciples screamed again. One of them—a young woman with a jade bracelet—was trying to summon her own beast, but the creature kept breaking her concentration with its roars.

"Get back!" Lin shouted, forcing himself to his feet. "All of you, get behind me!"

They didn't need to be told twice. They scrambled toward the rock formation, huddling together like frightened children.

The corrupted beast turned, its red eyes locking onto Lin. Black qi swirled around its body, forming patterns that looked almost like script. Ancient script. The kind Lin had only seen in the temple ruins.

*"It is a script-beast,"* the shadow panther said, its voice heavy with dread. *"A creature created specifically to carry corruption. The tamer who made this is no mere disciple."*

"Then who?"

The answer came not from the panther, but from the treeline.

A figure stepped out of the shadows, moving with the casual grace of someone who had nothing to fear. He was old—ancient, even—with white hair that fell past his shoulders and a beard that brushed his chest. His robes were black, embroidered with silver patterns that writhed and moved like living serpents.

"Impressive," the old man said, his voice like grinding stones. "A cripple with a broken snake, and yet you've bonded three beasts. The Resonance talent. I had heard rumors, but I did not believe them."

Lin's blood went cold. "You're the one controlling this thing."

"I am." The old man smiled, and there was nothing kind in it. "I have been watching you, Lin Xiao. Ever since you entered the Shadow Swamp. I wanted to see if the rumors were true. If you truly possessed the power of Beast Resonance."

"What do you want?"

"I want what every beast tamer wants. Power." The old man stepped closer, and Lin saw the beast at his side—a creature of pure shadow, its body flickering between forms like smoke in the wind. A Rank 5 Shadow Wraith. One of the rarest and most dangerous beasts in the valley. "But I want it on a scale that you cannot imagine. The Resonance talent allows merging with beasts. But what if it could be used to merge with *humans*? To create a fusion of beast tamers themselves?"

Lin's stomach churned. "That's insane."

"Is it?" The old man laughed. "Think about it, boy. A single beast tamer can bond with multiple beasts. But what if an entire sect could bond together? What if a hundred tamers could merge their power into a single being? We would be unstoppable. The Thousand Beast Valley would bow to us. The entire cultivation world would tremble."

"You're talking about destroying everything that makes us human."

"Humanity is a weakness. I intend to transcend it."

The corrupted beast roared, and Lin realized the old man had been stalling. While they talked, the creature had been healing. Now it stood at full strength, its red eyes blazing with renewed fury.

"Kill him," the old man said. "And bring me his core. I want to study the Resonance talent firsthand."

The corrupted beast charged.

Lin moved on instinct, activating Beast Resonance at full power. Shadow, lightning, and venom merged into a single torrent of energy that erupted from his body. He became a blur of motion, striking the creature again and again, each blow landing with the force of a falling mountain.

But the beast kept coming. It was too big, too strong, too *fast*. And behind it, the old man watched with calculating eyes, studying every move Lin made.

*"He is learning your techniques,"* the wind-snake warned. *"He will adapt."*

*"Then we need to end this quickly,"* Lin replied.

He changed tactics. Instead of trying to overpower the creature, he used the shadow panther's stealth to vanish into the darkness. The corrupted beast roared in confusion, swinging blindly at empty air.

Lin appeared behind it, lightning blade extended. The strike severed the creature's spine, sending it crashing to the ground.

But before he could finish it, the old man moved.

He appeared in front of Lin with impossible speed, his hand outstretched. Black qi erupted from his palm, slamming into Lin's chest and sending him flying backward. He crashed through three trees before finally stopping, his vision swimming.

"You have potential," the old man said, walking toward him. "But potential means nothing without control. Let me show you what true power looks like."

He raised his hand, and the black qi gathered into a spear. The weapon hummed with malevolent energy, its tip pointed directly at Lin's heart.

"Any last words, boy?"

Lin coughed blood. "Yeah. You talk too much."

He activated Beast Resonance again, but this time he didn't merge with his beasts. Instead, he pushed the Resonance outward—into the corrupted beast.

The effect was immediate.

The creature screamed, its body convulsing as the corruption fought against Lin's power. The black qi that had been controlling it began to unravel, dissolving like smoke in the wind.

"What are you doing?" the old man demanded, his composure cracking for the first time.

"Cleaning up your mess," Lin said through gritted teeth.

The corrupted beast's body began to change. The twisted flesh smoothed. The red eyes faded to a dull gray. And then, with a final shudder, the creature collapsed, its body reverting to a natural state.

The old man stared, his face a mask of fury. "You destroyed my work. Months of research. Wasted."

"Good."

"I will not forget this, Lin Xiao." The old man's eyes burned with hatred. "I will take your Resonance talent for myself. And when I do, I will make you watch as I destroy everything you love."

He vanished, his shadow wraith wrapping around him like a cloak and pulling him into the darkness.

Lin collapsed to his knees, the last of his strength gone. The disciples rushed to his side, their voices a blur of thanks and questions he couldn't answer.

The shadow panther materialized beside him, its golden eyes fixed on the spot where the old man had disappeared.

*"He will return,"* the panther said.

"I know."

*"And next time, he will not underestimate you."*

Lin looked at his hands. They were shaking. The power thrumming through his veins was incredible, but he had barely survived. Against a true master, he was still nothing.

"Then I need to get stronger," he said. "Fast."

He stood, ignoring the protests of his battered body. The swamp stretched out before him, dark and full of unknown dangers. But somewhere out there, an old man was plotting his destruction.

And Lin Xiao intended to be ready when he came.

But as he turned to leave, he noticed something strange. The young woman with the jade bracelet was staring at him with an expression he couldn't read. Recognition. Fear. And something else.

"What?" Lin asked.

She swallowed. "Your name. Lin Xiao. I've heard it before."

"From where?"

She hesitated. "The sect elders. They've been looking for you. They say you're a threat to the valley."

"A threat?" Lin laughed bitterly. "I'm just trying to survive."

"No." She shook her head. "They said you're something else. They said you're the key to something. Something that's been sleeping in the valley for a thousand years."

The shadow panther's growl echoed in Lin's mind.

*"She speaks of the Ancient One. The beast that created the Thousand Beast Valley."*

Lin's blood ran cold. "What ancient one?"

But he already knew the answer. He could feel it, pulsing beneath the earth like a second heartbeat. A presence so vast and old that it made the corrupted beast look like an insect.

And it was waking up.

title: The Ancient Bond Awakens
content:
The corrupted beast's massive claw tore through the space where Lin had stood a heartbeat before. Shadow dispersed like morning mist. Lin reappeared twenty feet to the left, lightning crackling along his arms, the wind-snake's venom dripping from his fingertips.

"You're slow," he said, not to the beast, but to himself. Testing. Always testing.

The hunting party stared at him with wide eyes. Five disciples, their robes bearing the crimson sun emblem of the Blazing Sun Sect—one of the three great sects that dominated the eastern reaches of Thousand Beast Valley. Their beasts were impressive: a fire-fanged wolf, a crystal-shelled tortoise, a pair of wind-cutter sparrows. But all of them were cowering, pressed against the rock formation like cornered prey.

The corrupted beast turned, its twisted body scraping against the ground. Up close, Lin could see the corruption clearly now—black veins pulsing beneath translucent skin, bone spurs jutting at unnatural angles, and that terrible red glow in its eyes. It had once been a forest guardian, a Rank 4 Ironhide Bear. Now it was a puppet of the corruption that had been spreading through the valley like a plague for months.

*"There's something inside it,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"Something controlling the corruption."*

Lin's eyes narrowed. He focused his [Beast Resonance], pushing his senses deeper into the creature's spiritual core. And there—buried beneath layers of black qi—he felt it. A presence. Ancient. Alien. And *hungry*.

"What are you?" Lin murmured.

The corrupted beast answered by charging.

It moved faster than something its size should have, black qi erupting from its wounds like rocket exhaust. Lin barely had time to react. He merged with his beasts instinctively—[Beast Resonance: Partial Merge]—borrowing the panther's shadow-step and the hawk's reflexes simultaneously. The world slowed. He saw the trajectory of the beast's charge, saw the opening in its guard, saw the faint shimmer of something metallic embedded in its chest.

A token. A bronze token, half-melted into the corrupted flesh.

Lin's blood ran cold.

He'd seen tokens like that before. They were used by the Beast Tamer Council to mark bonded beasts for official registration. This corrupted beast had been someone's bonded partner. And that someone had either abandoned it or—

"Move!" one of the Blazing Sun disciples screamed.

Lin rolled to the side as the beast's claw crashed down where he'd been standing, shattering the ground into a crater of fractured stone. Dirt sprayed across his face. The impact sent shockwaves through the swamp, rippling across the murky water.

The wind-snake hissed. *"Master, the corruption is spreading. We need to end this quickly."*

*"Agreed."* Lin straightened, wiping blood from a cut on his cheek. He hadn't even felt the wound form. *"Triple Merge. Full power. Now."*

[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge — 100% Synchronization]

The world dissolved into pure sensation.

Lin became shadow, lightning, and venom. He felt the panther's predatory instincts sharpening his own, the hawk's aerial awareness mapping every escape route, the snake's ancient cunning whispering strategies that weren't quite human. His body flickered between forms—solid one moment, immaterial the next. Electricity danced across his skin. His eyes turned black and gold and silver, all at once.

The corrupted beast roared and charged again.

Lin didn't dodge this time.

He met it head-on.

Time seemed to stretch as they collided. Lin's right hand, coated in the shadow panther's darkness, passed through the beast's guard like smoke through a net. His left hand, sparking with the lightning hawk's power, slammed into its chest where the bronze token lay embedded. The wind-snake's venom dripped from his lips as he breathed a cloud of paralytic mist into the creature's face.

For one frozen moment, they stood together—boy and beast, merged in combat.

Then Lin ripped the bronze token free.

The corrupted beast screamed. Not a roar, but a scream—high-pitched, agonized, *human*. Black qi erupted from its body in a violent geyser, and the creature collapsed, its massive form crashing to the ground with enough force to shake the swamp for miles.

Lin stumbled back, breathing hard, the bronze token clutched in his bleeding hand. The triple merge faded, leaving him feeling hollow and exposed. His beasts retreated to their spiritual space, exhausted.

The Blazing Sun disciples stared at him like he was a monster.

"Who... who are you?" the lead disciple asked, a young woman with flame-red hair bound in a warrior's tail. Her fire-fanged wolf pressed against her leg, growling uncertainly.

"Someone who asks questions first," Lin said, turning the bronze token over in his hands. "This beast was bonded. The token's still active. That means its tamer might still be alive."

The red-haired disciple paled. "That's impossible. If the beast is corrupted, the tamer would have to be dead. The corruption spreads through the bond—"

"Unless the tamer wasn't the target." Lin's voice was flat. Cold. "Unless the corruption was designed to spread *through* the bond, into the tamer's spiritual core."

Silence.

The swamp seemed to hold its breath.

Then, from the shadows behind the rock formation, a voice spoke. Low. Ancient. *Wrong*.

"The boy understands faster than most."

Lin spun, the bronze token still in his hand. A figure emerged from the darkness—tall, gaunt, wrapped in torn robes that might once have been white. Its face was hidden behind a ceramic mask, featureless except for a single eye-slit that glowed with the same red light as the corrupted beast.

Behind it, the shadows writhed with more shapes. More corrupted beasts. Dozens of them.

"I am the Warden of the Forgotten," the figure said, its voice echoing as if from a great distance. "And I have been watching you, Lin Xiao. The boy who speaks to beasts. The boy who *merges* with them."

Lin's hand tightened around the token. "What do you want?"

The Warden tilted its head. "You carry the mark of the Ancient Bond. The first bond. The bond that should never have been broken." It took a step forward, and the corrupted beasts behind it stirred, their red eyes glowing brighter. "I want to remind the valley what it has forgotten."

The red-haired disciple grabbed Lin's arm. "We need to run. Now."

Lin didn't move.

His eyes were fixed on the bronze token in his hand. The ancient markings on its surface—markings he'd seen before, in the temple where he'd bonded the shadow panther. Markings that matched the carvings on the temple walls.

Markings that spoke of a bond deeper than any tamer-beast relationship.

A bond between *worlds*.

"I know what you are," Lin said quietly.

The Warden paused.

"You're not a beast tamer. You're not even human anymore." Lin held up the token, letting the moonlight catch its surface. "You're what's left of someone who tried to bond with a spirit beast from beyond the valley. From the *Abyss*."

The Warden's mask cracked. A single line, running from the eye-slit to the chin.

"Clever boy," it whispered. "But clever boys don't live long in this valley."

It raised its hand, and the corrupted beasts surged forward.

Lin grabbed the red-haired disciple and ran.

The swamp exploded into chaos. Trees shattered. Water turned to steam as corrupted energy lashed out in all directions. Lin pulled the disciple through the mist, his beasts screaming warnings in his mind, his heart pounding so hard he thought it might burst.

Behind them, the Warden's laughter echoed through the night.

"RUN, LITTLE TAMER! RUN FAR! BUT KNOW THIS—THE ABYSS HAS ALREADY SEEN YOU. AND IT WANTS YOU BACK."

Lin didn't stop running until the sounds of pursuit faded into the distance.

When he finally collapsed against a moss-covered tree, gasping for breath, the red-haired disciple was staring at him with wide, terrified eyes.

"What did you mean," she whispered, "when you said it wants you *back*?"

Lin looked down at his hands. At the veins of shadow pulsing beneath his skin. At the flickers of lightning dancing between his fingers. At the venom that still dripped from his pores.

He'd thought his [Beast Resonance] was a gift.

He was beginning to realize it might be a curse.

"I don't know," he said, and for the first time in weeks, he was afraid. "But I'm going to find out."

Somewhere in the distance, the Warden's laughter still echoed.

And beneath it, faint but growing stronger, Lin heard a new sound.

The sound of ancient chains breaking.

title: Chapter 13
content:
CHAPTER 13: The Sect That Shouldn't Exist

The corrupted beast's death throes shook the swamp. Lin stood at the center of its dissolving corpse, three beasts' worth of power still singing through his veins as black qi dissipated into the morning mist.

The hunting party stared at him like he'd grown a second head.

"Who—who are you?" stammered the lead disciple, a girl with dirt-streaked features and a jade token marking her as a member of the Crimson Moon Sect. "I've never seen a beast tamer move like that."

Lin dismissed the merge, feeling the power recede as his beasts returned to their spiritual spaces. The shadow panther grumbled in annoyance—it had wanted to hunt longer.

"Lin Xiao. Lone tamer." He stepped over the last dissolving chunk of corrupted flesh. "What were you doing out here? The inner swamp is restricted to sect disciples."

The girl's face paled. "We were running. The corruption—it's not just beasts anymore."

The air grew cold.

"What do you mean?"

She looked at her companions, then back at Lin. "Three days ago, the Obsidian Serpent Sect fell. Wiped out. Not by beasts—by their own disciples. The corruption turned them from within. They're calling it the Tainted Awakening. Anyone who bonds with a corrupted beast becomes a vessel. A puppet."

Lin's blood ran cold. "How many sects have fallen?"

"Seven. Maybe eight." Her voice cracked. "The valley is burning, Lin Xiao. The elders are fighting a war on two fronts—the corruption spreading through the beasts, and the corruption spreading through their own ranks. No one knows who to trust anymore."

The lightning hawk shrieked overhead, a warning. Lin's new senses flared—something was approaching, fast.

"Get behind me," he ordered.

The mist parted.

A figure emerged from the swamp, walking with the casual grace of someone who owned the ground beneath their feet. He wore robes of deepest black, unmarked by any sect insignia. His eyes were wrong—pupils vertical, like a serpent's, and glowing with the same malevolent red light as the corrupted beast.

But he wasn't corrupted.

He was *in control*.

"Impressive work, little tamer." The man's voice was smooth, almost pleasant. "Most beast tamers can't handle a third-rank corruption spawn alone. But you—you have something special, don't you?"

Lin's hand drifted toward his beasts' spiritual spaces. "Who are you?"

"Names are such limiting things." The man smiled, and his teeth were too sharp, too many. "But you can call me Elder Shen. I represent an organization you've never heard of—the Shade Court. We've been watching you, Lin Xiao. Ever since you bonded that crippled wind-snake, actually. We were curious to see how far you'd go."

The Crimson Moon disciples huddled behind Lin, trembling.

"What do you want?"

"Recruitment." Elder Shen spread his hands, a gesture of openness that felt anything but. "The Shade Court has observed your growth. Your [Beast Resonance] ability is unprecedented—a unique mutation that allows true fusion with your bonded beasts. In a thousand years of recorded history, only three beast tamers have manifested anything similar. And none of them survived past their twentieth birthday."

Lin's gut twisted. "Is that supposed to scare me?"

"It's supposed to interest you." Elder Shen's eyes narrowed. "Because we know why they died. Their bodies couldn't handle the strain of multiple merges. Their meridians shredded, their cores shattered. But you—you're different. Your Resonance is evolving. Growing stronger. We want to study that. Help you reach your full potential."

"And if I refuse?"

The smile vanished. "Then you become a threat. And the Shade Court eliminates threats before they can bloom."

The shadow panther growled in Lin's mind. *He's lying. His aura shifts when he mentions the others who died—he knows more than he's telling.*

*I know,* Lin thought back. *But he's also incredibly strong. I can't sense his cultivation level at all.*

*Because he's hiding it,* the panther replied. *And that alone is terrifying.*

Elder Shen tilted his head, as if listening to something. "Your beast is perceptive. Good. You'll need that intelligence in the days to come." He reached into his robe and produced a black jade token, carved with a symbol Lin didn't recognize—a serpent consuming its own tail. "Take this. When you decide to accept our offer, crush it. Someone will find you within the hour."

He tossed the token. Lin caught it reluctantly.

"I won't join an organization I know nothing about."

"You'll learn. Sooner than you think." Elder Shen turned, his form beginning to dissolve into shadow. "One last piece of advice, little tamer. The corruption isn't a disease. It's a *harvest*. Someone is spreading it deliberately, collecting the chaos it creates. And they're almost done."

He vanished.

The mist closed behind him as if he'd never existed.

The Crimson Moon girl collapsed to her knees, sobbing with relief. "Thank you. Thank you so much. We thought we were going to die."

Lin helped her up, his mind racing. "Where are you stationed? I'll escort you back."

"The Rainpetal Sect," she said. "It's the nearest safe zone. The elders there are still uncorrupted—we've been using it as a staging ground for rescue operations."

"Lead the way."

---

The Rainpetal Sect was a fortress under siege.

Even from a distance, Lin could see the walls bristling with defensive formations. Beast tamers patrolled the perimeter in shifts, their bonded creatures alert for any sign of corruption. The air was thick with tension, fear, and the scent of burning herbs meant to purify corrupted qi.

They were let in after a thorough inspection—three separate tests for corruption, each more invasive than the last. By the time Lin stepped through the inner gates, he felt like he'd been turned inside out.

The sect's main courtyard had been converted into a triage center. Wounded beast tamers lay on makeshift beds, their bonded creatures curled beside them, sharing their pain through their connections. Healers moved between them, faces grim.

"Over here," the girl said, leading him toward a large pavilion where a group of elders were arguing over a map.

"—can't hold the eastern line much longer—"

"—need more purification pills—"

"—the Obsidian Serpent Sect's survivors say the corruption came from *inside*. Someone in their inner circle was a plant—"

"—we don't even know who we can trust anymore—"

The girl cleared her throat. "Elders? This is Lin Xiao. He saved us in the swamp. Fought a third-rank spawn alone, using a technique I've never seen before."

The arguing stopped.

Seven pairs of eyes turned to Lin. He felt their gazes like physical weights, each elder's cultivation pressing against his own. He was outmatched, and they knew it.

But he didn't flinch.

"A lone tamer, fighting corruption spawn in the inner swamp?" The lead elder, a woman with silver-streaked hair and eyes like chips of ice, stepped forward. "That's either incredibly brave or incredibly foolish. Which are you, boy?"

"Both," Lin said honestly. "And I have questions."

"Ask."

"Who is the Shade Court?"

Silence.

The elder's expression flickered—fear, recognition, and something else. Something that made Lin's skin crawl.

"Where did you hear that name?" she demanded.

"A man calling himself Elder Shen found me in the swamp. Offered me recruitment. Said they've been watching me since I bonded my first beast."

The elders exchanged glances. The tension in the room spiked.

The lead elder grabbed Lin's arm, her grip like iron. "We need to talk. Privately. *Now*."

Before he could respond, a scream tore through the camp.

Not a beast. A human.

Lin spun, [Beast Resonance] flaring instinctively as he saw a disciple convulsing on the ground. Black qi poured from his mouth, his eyes, his *skin*. The corruption was consuming him from within.

"Get back!" someone shouted.

But it was too late.

The disciple's body *split*—not open, but apart, as if something inside had grown too large and simply burst free. A creature of shadow and bone and writhing tendrils emerged from the husk, its shriek deafening.

And in the chaos, the lead elder's grip tightened.

"I'm sorry, boy," she whispered, her breath hot against his ear. "But you've seen too much."

Her hand *changed*—scales rippling across her skin, claws extending from her fingers.

Her eyes blazed red.

"You're one of them," Lin breathed.

"We're all 'them,'" the elder said, her voice turning serpentine. "And you've just walked into our nest."

title: Chapter 14
content:
CHAPTER 14: The Price of Power

Lin's body hummed with the merged essence of three beasts as he danced around the corrupted creature. The thing was massive—easily twenty feet of twisted muscle and hate—but in his enhanced state, it moved like molasses.

*"Don't kill it,"* Elder Shen's voice echoed in his memory. *"Capture one alive. We need to understand the source."*

Lin pivoted, shadows billowing around him as he dodged a swipe that would have torn a normal man in half. The hunting party huddled against the rocks, their leader—a girl of perhaps seventeen with a jade hairpin marking her as a core disciple of the Celestial Crane Sect—watched him with wide eyes.

"You're that outcast," she said, recognition dawning. "Lin Xiao. The cripple."

"Not anymore," Lin replied, his voice layered with three tones. Human. Serpent. Feline. Predator.

He thrust his palm forward. The wind-snake's venom shot from his fingers in a concentrated stream, striking the corrupted beast's face. The creature howled, black qi sizzling where the poison touched.

But instead of retreating, it grew larger.

The corrupted flesh bulged, splitting open to reveal more teeth, more eyes, more of that terrible red light. The black qi intensified, forming a cocoon around its body.

*"The corruption is adaptive,"* the shadow panther whispered through their bond. *"It learns. It grows. We must strike the core."*

"Where?" Lin asked aloud.

*"I cannot see it. The qi masks everything."*

The lightning hawk screeched from above. *"The origin point is inside. But it moves. Like a worm in rotten fruit."*

Lin cursed. Three beasts, merged power, and still he was blind. The corrupted beast lunged, faster than before. He barely managed to phase through its grasp, becoming shadow for a heartbeat.

When he reformed, the disciples were gone.

No—not gone. The girl with the jade hairpin had used a transport talisman, evacuating her team. She stood alone now, a dozen paces away, a jade staff in her hands.

"I'm not leaving you," she said, answering his unasked question. "You saved us. I owe you."

"I don't need—"

The corrupted beast slammed its fist into the ground between them. The earth split. Lin leaped, using the wind-snake's agility to twist midair, landing on the creature's arm. He ran up its length, lightning crackling along his path.

At the shoulder, he found it. A pulsating knot of black qi, buried beneath layers of twisted flesh. The core.

He drove his hand in.

The world exploded into darkness.

---

Lin fell.

Not physically—his body remained tangled with the corrupted beast, locked in a battle of wills. But his consciousness plummeted into a void, a realm of pure black where only whispers existed.

*"You think you understand power?"*

The voice came from everywhere and nowhere. Ancient. Hungry.

*"You bind weak beasts to yourself and call it strength. You merge their pathetic essences and think yourself mighty."*

"Who are you?" Lin shouted into the void.

*"I am what sleeps beneath. I am the rot that consumes. I am the end of all bonds."*

The darkness coalesced into a shape. A serpent, impossibly large, its scales made of shadow and grief. Its eyes were twin suns of malevolent crimson.

*"You have bonded three beasts, little tamer. But every bond is a chain. Every chain is an anchor. And every anchor... can be pulled."*

The serpent lunged.

---

Lin's eyes snapped open.

He was on his back, staring at the canopy. The corrupted beast lay nearby, dissolving into black mist. The girl from the Celestial Crane Sect knelt over him, her hands glowing with healing qi.

"You passed out," she said. "The beast collapsed. Whatever you did, it worked."

But Lin barely heard her. Something was wrong.

He reached for the wind-snake's presence in his mind.

Silence.

He reached for the lightning hawk.

Nothing.

He reached for the shadow panther.

*Empty.*

"No," he whispered. "No, no, no—"

He sat up so fast the girl fell back. He clawed at his chest, at his dantian, feeling for the bonds. They were there—he could sense the faint threads—but they were... muffled. Like hearing voices through water.

*[Warning: Beast Bonds Compromised]*

The system notification appeared in his vision, flickering like a dying flame.

*[Corruption detected in resonance connection. All bonded beasts have entered defensive hibernation. Bond strength decreasing at rate of 2% per hour.]*

"Two percent per hour," Lin breathed. "I have fifty hours before..."

Before he lost them entirely.

"What's wrong?" the girl asked, concern in her voice. "Your face is pale. Let me—"

"Don't touch me." Lin scrambled away from her, his hands shaking. "Stay back."

The corruption. It had done something to him. When he'd touched the core, when he'd forced his hand into that pulsating mass—something had crawled back along his connection, infecting the bonds themselves.

*"Every bond is a chain,"* the serpent had said. *"Every chain is an anchor."*

He'd thought his [Beast Resonance] was a gift. A unique talent that made him special. But the corruption had shown him the truth: it was also a vulnerability. A highway into his soul.

"I need to find Elder Shen," Lin said, forcing himself to stand. His legs wobbled. "I need—"

"Wait." The girl grabbed his arm. "You're in no condition to—"

"I said stay back!"

The shadows around them exploded. Lin's eyes blazed with purple light as the last dregs of his merged power reacted to his panic. The girl flew backward, slamming into a tree. She crumpled, unconscious.

Lin stared at his hands. The shadow panther's essence flickered through them, weak and fading.

*"I will test you,"* the panther had said. *"And if you prove unworthy, I will devour your soul."*

But it wasn't the panther that would devour him. It was the corruption. Eating away at his bonds. Starving his beasts. Leaving him hollow.

He fell to his knees, clutching his head.

The wind-snake. His first bond. The crippled creature he'd saved from the slaughterhouse, the one everyone said was worthless. It had trusted him. Given him its broken core.

The lightning hawk had chosen him over a hundred other tamers. Had flown through storm and fire to reach him.

The shadow panther had knelt and accepted his bond, even while threatening to kill him.

And now they were fading. Dying. Because he'd been careless.

*"There is a way."*

Lin's head snapped up. The voice came from his own mind, but it wasn't his. It was older. Colder. And impossibly familiar.

*"You heard me in the darkness, little tamer. You felt my presence. I am the corruption's source, but I am also its opposite."*

"Show yourself," Lin growled.

*"I cannot. I am bound. Trapped beneath the valley by the very beast tamers you idolize. They sealed me away ages ago, but the corruption I was meant to contain has learned to leak through."*

"Lies. You're trying to trick me."

*"Am I? Your bonds are dying. Your beasts are slipping away. In less than two days, you will be a cripple again—worse than before, because you will feel the empty spaces where their souls used to reside."*

Lin's hands balled into fists.

*"I can give you the power to sever the corruption's hold. I can give you the strength to not only save your beasts, but to elevate them beyond anything this valley has ever seen."*

"In exchange for what?"

The voice laughed. It sounded like grinding stones and breaking bones.

*"In exchange for a simple thing. Free me from my prison. Let me walk the world again."*

"You're the source of the corruption. If I free you, you'll destroy everything."

*"I am the source of the corruption's CONTAINMENT. The corruption itself is something else—a hunger that predates me. I was created to hold it in check. But without my full power, I am failing. Every day, more of it seeps out. More beasts fall. More tamers die."*

Lin closed his eyes. The wind-snake's life force flickered like a candle in a storm.

*"You have a choice, Lin Xiao. Let your beasts die, and live the rest of your life as a hollow shell of what you could have been. Or free me, and become the greatest beast tamer this world has ever seen."*

"The valley will be destroyed."

*"The valley is already dying. You've seen the corruption spreading. You've fought the twisted beasts. How long do you think the sects can hold out? A month? A year? They will fall, and everything you love will burn."*

Lin opened his eyes. The girl from the Celestial Crane Sect was stirring, moaning softly.

"Where are you?" he asked. "Where is your prison?"

*"Beneath the Temple of Shattered Moons. In the heart of the valley's deepest cave. Follow the black rivers, and you will find me."*

"And if I free you, you'll save my beasts?"

*"I will give you the tools to save them yourself. That is the only promise I make."*

Lin looked at his hands. The shadow panther's mark was fading from his skin. The wind-snake's coil was barely visible on his wrist. The lightning hawk's sigil on his chest was a dim, sputtering ember.

He had fifty hours.

"Fine," he said, his voice barely a whisper. "I'll do it."

The voice laughed again, triumphant and terrible.

*"Excellent. I will send you a guide. Follow her, and do not stray from the path. The corruption will try to claim you before you reach me. You must resist."*

"Her? Who—"

A sound interrupted him. Footsteps. Many of them.

A dozen figures emerged from the mist, their robes bearing the emblem of the Celestial Crane Sect. At their head was an old man with a white beard that reached his waist and eyes like frozen lakes.

"Disciple Lin," the old man said, his voice carrying the weight of authority. "I am Elder Feng of the Celestial Crane Sect. We have been watching you."

The girl sat up, rubbing her head. "Elder Feng? What are you—"

"Quiet, Mei." The elder's gaze never left Lin. "You bonded with a shadow panther in the forbidden temple. You used forbidden techniques to merge your beasts. And now, you have been touched by the corruption."

Lin's blood ran cold.

"According to the laws of the Thousand Beast Valley," Elder Feng continued, "any tamer who shows signs of corruption must be quarantined. And any tamer who willingly communicates with the sealed entity beneath the temple..."

The elder's hand moved, and the disciples around him drew their weapons.

"...must be executed."

title: The Price of Resonance
content:
Lin Xiao's body sang with power as the corrupted beast swung its massive claws through where he'd been standing. Shadow and lightning intertwined, he flowed around the attack like water around a stone.

*"Left flank,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered through their bond. *"It favors its right leg."*

He saw it now—the twisted limb, the way the creature's weight shifted awkwardly. Three beasts' worth of combat instinct merged in his mind, processing information faster than any single entity could manage.

"Wind-Snake, bind its legs!" Lin commanded.

The snake shot forward, growing to thrice its normal size as it wrapped around the corrupted beast's ankles. The creature stumbled, roaring in fury.

"Now, Lightning Hawk!"

The hawk shrieked, diving from above. Lightning gathered around its beak, creating a spear of pure electricity that punched through the creature's skull. The corrupted beast convulsed, black qi exploding from the wound, before collapsing with a ground-shaking thud.

Silence fell over the swamp.

The hunting party stared at Lin with a mixture of awe and terror. Their leader, a young woman with a jade hairpin and robes bearing the emblem of the Azure Sky Sect, stepped forward cautiously.

"You... you're Lin Xiao," she said, recognition dawning in her eyes. "The failure from the outer valley."

"Former failure," Lin corrected, allowing the beast resonance to fade. The power receded, leaving him feeling strangely hollow. "What are you doing this deep in the Deadwater Swamp?"

"We were tracking the corruption source," the woman replied. "I'm Mu Yiran, disciple of Azure Sky Sect. We've been investigating the outbreaks for weeks." She gestured to her companions—five other disciples, all nursing various injuries. "We found more than we bargained for."

"Corruption source?" Lin's blood ran cold. He'd heard rumors, but no one had confirmed its existence. "You mean someone is creating these creatures?"

Mu Yiran's face darkened. "Not someone. Something. We found an ancient altar deep in the swamp, pulsing with corrupted qi. Before we could examine it, that beast attacked." She pointed at the corpse. "We barely escaped with our lives."

*"She lies,"* the shadow panther hissed in Lin's mind. *"I taste fear on her words, but not of the beast. She fears what she found."*

Lin studied Mu Yiran more carefully. Her hands trembled slightly, but her eyes held a desperate determination that spoke of more than simple survival instinct.

"What did you find at the altar?" he asked bluntly.

Mu Yiran's companions exchanged nervous glances. She bit her lip, then pulled a small object from her robe. A fragment of black jade, carved with symbols that writhed and shifted when Lin looked at them directly.

"The altar was calling to something," she whispered. "Something ancient. This fragment was the focus. When I touched it..." She shuddered. "I saw a city of beasts. Not beasts as we know them—beasts that had evolved beyond their forms, taken human shape, built civilization."

Lin's heart stopped.

*"Impossible,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"The Beast Kings were legends. Myths told to cubs."*

*"Not myths,"* the shadow panther countered, its voice cold. *"I remember. My blood remembers. The ancient ones who first learned cultivation from the beasts themselves. They were real."*

"What did you see?" Lin pressed, stepping closer.

Mu Yiran's eyes went distant. "A war. A betrayal. The Beast Kings were betrayed by their human partners—the first beast tamers. They sealed them in the void between worlds, draining their power to create the cultivation system we use today." Her voice cracked. "And the altar... the altar is trying to bring them back."

The disciples stirred uneasily. One of them, a young man with a bandaged arm, spoke up. "Senior Sister, we shouldn't be telling an outer valley failure about—"

"Shut up, Wang Lei," Mu Yiran snapped. "He just saved our lives. Besides..." She looked at Lin with new eyes. "He merged with three beasts simultaneously. That's not just rare. That's impossible. If anyone can help us stop this, it's him."

*"She's right about one thing,"* the lightning hawk's voice crackled through Lin's mind. *"The corruption is spreading faster than the sects can contain it. If the Beast Kings return, everything we know will end."*

Lin made a decision. "Take me to the altar."

---

The journey took two hours through increasingly treacherous terrain. The swamp grew darker, the mist thicker, and the corrupted qi heavier with every step. Lin kept his three beasts close, their presence a comfort in the oppressive atmosphere.

They found the altar in a clearing that should have been impossible—a perfect circle of stone, untouched by the swamp's decay. The altar itself was a massive slab of obsidian, covered in the same shifting symbols as the fragment. But it was what stood *behind* the altar that made Lin's blood freeze.

A portal. Crackling with black lightning, swirling with darkness that seemed to drink the light around it.

"It's grown," Mu Yiran whispered, horror in her voice. "When we left, it was barely the size of a man. Now..."

The portal was thirty feet tall. And it was *pushing* something through.

Lin felt them before he saw them—beasts, dozens of them, their signatures flickering like dying stars. But these weren't ordinary beasts. They were *wrong*. Their cores had been twisted, their very essence corrupted. And they were all staring at the group with hungry red eyes.

"We need to leave," Lin said, backing away. "Now."

But before they could move, the portal flared. A figure stepped through—humanoid, but wrong. Its skin was covered in beast scales, its eyes glowed with ancient malevolence, and its arms ended in claws that dripped with black qi.

*"So,"* the figure said, its voice resonating in Lin's very soul. *"A child of resonance. How delightful."*

Lin's beasts screamed in his mind, a chorus of fear and rage.

"Who are you?" Lin demanded, forcing his voice steady.

The figure smiled, revealing teeth that had been filed to points. *"I am what your ancestors feared. I am the first of the betrayed. And I have come to reclaim what was stolen."* It raised a clawed hand. *"You have a gift, boy. A gift that belongs to my kind. [Beast Resonance] was never meant for humans. It was ours. The blood of the Beast Kings flows in your veins."*

Lin's world tilted. "That's impossible. I'm just an outer valley failure."

*"Are you?"* The figure laughed, a sound like breaking bones. *"Your mother was a Beast Tamer of the ancient line. She sealed your power at birth to protect you from our enemies. But now..."* It gestured at Lin's three bonded beasts. *"Now you've broken the seal yourself."*

Mu Yiran grabbed Lin's arm. "He's lying. Trying to confuse you."

But the shadow panther's voice was quiet, thoughtful. *"He speaks truth. I sensed it when we bonded. Your soul carries the mark of the ancient bloodline."*

Everything clicked into place. His unnatural affinity with beasts. His ability to merge with them. The way they accepted him despite his supposed weakness.

He wasn't a failure.

He was a *threat*.

"Come with me," the figure said, extending its hand. "Help me tear down the walls between worlds. Help me free my kind. And I will give you power beyond your wildest dreams. The entire valley will bow to you. The sects will kneel."

Lin looked at his beasts. The wind-snake, once crippled, now coiled with deadly grace. The lightning hawk, sparks dancing along its feathers. The shadow panther, its eyes gleaming with ancient knowledge.

He looked at Mu Yiran, at the terrified disciples behind her.

He looked at the portal, at the darkness pressing against it from the other side.

And he made his choice.

"No."

The figure's smile vanished. *"What?"*

"I said no." Lin activated [Beast Resonance], feeling the power surge through him. "I don't know who I really am. I don't know what my mother did or why. But I know one thing—I won't become a monster to gain power."

The figure's eyes narrowed. *"Foolish child. You have no idea what you're refusing."*

"Then show me."

Lin attacked.

Shadow and lightning and venom merged into a single devastating strike. He appeared before the figure, his clawed hands aimed at its heart. But the creature moved faster, catching his wrist with contemptuous ease.

*"You have potential,"* it said, squeezing until Lin's bones groaned. *"But potential means nothing against millennia of experience."*

It flung Lin away. He crashed through trees, tumbling across the swamp floor until he came to a stop against a half-submerged log.

*"Lin!"* Mu Yiran screamed, but her voice was distant, muffled by the blood roaring in his ears.

The figure walked toward him, casual, unhurried. *"I'll give you time to reconsider. The portal will fully open in seven days. On that day, the Beast Kings will rise. You can either stand with us... or be consumed by us."*

It turned and walked back through the portal, which collapsed behind it with a thunderclap.

The corrupted beasts vanished, dissolving into black mist.

And Lin Xiao lay in the swamp, broken and bleeding, with seven days to save a world that had never believed in him.

---

Mu Yiran found him an hour later, barely conscious, his beasts gathered protectively around him.

"We need to get him to the sects," she said. "The Azure Sky Sect has healers who can—"

"No." Lin's voice was a rasp. "The sects will kill me. I'm a Beast King descendant. They'll see me as a threat."

"Then what do we do?"

Lin forced himself to sit up, ignoring the agony in his ribs. He looked at his bonded beasts—his family, the only ones who had ever truly accepted him.

"We find the other descendants," he said. "The ones like me. If the Beast Kings are returning, we need an army of our own."

"But you don't even know if there *are* others."

Lin smiled grimly. "My mother knew. She left me clues. I just never understood them until now." He reached into his robe and pulled out a pendant—a simple wooden charm carved with the image of a beast with seven heads. "She told me to find the Seven-Headed One if I ever needed help."

"The Seven-Headed One?" Mu Yiran's face paled. "That's a legend. A myth."

"All legends come from somewhere."

Lin pushed himself to his feet, swaying. The shadow panther pressed against his leg, supporting his weight.

"Seven days," he murmured, looking at the spot where the portal had been. "We have seven days to find the descendants, unite them, and stop the Beast Kings from returning."

*"And if we fail?"* the wind-snake asked.

Lin thought of the creature's smile, of the darkness pressing against the walls of reality.

"Then the Thousand Beast Valley becomes a graveyard."

title: Chapter 16
content:
CHAPTER 16: The Price of Power

Lin Xiao's fist connected with the corrupted beast's skull, and the impact sent shockwaves through the swamp. The creature stumbled, black ichor spraying from the wound, but it didn't fall. Its twisted body rotated impossibly, jaws snapping at where Lin had been standing a heartbeat ago.

He was already gone, flickering through shadows, reappearing on a low-hanging branch twenty feet away.

*"It's stronger than it looks,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"The corruption has fused with its core. Killing it won't be clean."*

"Nothing in this valley ever is," Lin muttered, wiping sweat from his brow.

The disciples huddled behind the rock formation—three of them, young, their robes bearing the silver moon emblem of the Crescent Sect. The smallest one, a girl with twin braids, was crying. The eldest, a boy of maybe sixteen, held a broken sword in trembling hands.

"Stay back," Lin called to them. "I'll handle this."

The corrupted beast turned its burning gaze toward him. Black qi coiled around its body like living chains. When it opened its mouth to roar, Lin saw something that made his blood run cold.

A core fragment. Embedded in the creature's throat, pulsing with the same malevolent light as its eyes.

Human cultivation cores.

*"They've been feeding it cultivators,"* the lightning hawk screeched from above. *"The corruption isn't natural. Someone is making these things."*

Lin didn't have time to process the implications. The beast charged, faster than something its size should be. He activated [Beast Resonance: Triple Merge] again, feeling the familiar rush of power as his three beasts' abilities merged into a single coherent whole.

He became smoke and shadow. Lightning and venom. Speed and precision.

The beast's claw passed through his afterimage. He slid beneath its legs, slashing upward with a blade formed from compressed wind and shadow energy. The attack carved a trench along the creature's belly, spilling corrupted organs onto the swamp floor.

It didn't even slow down.

*"The core,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"You must destroy the core. Nothing else will stop it."*

Lin cursed. The core fragment was embedded deep in the creature's throat, protected by layers of corrupted flesh and bone. A direct assault would mean getting close. Very close.

The beast wheeled around, its attention fixed on him. But then its head swiveled toward the disciples, and Lin's heart stopped.

"No—"

It was too fast. The creature lunged, covering the distance in a single bound. The eldest disciple raised his broken sword in a futile gesture. The girl with braids screamed.

Time slowed.

Lin's body moved before his mind caught up. He poured everything into a single burst of speed—the panther's shadow-step, the hawk's lightning reflexes, the snake's fluid motion. He appeared between the beast and the disciples, arms outstretched.

The corrupted beast's jaws closed around him.

Pain. White-hot, searing pain as the creature's teeth sank into his shoulder and chest. He felt bones crack, felt blood pour down his side. The beast shook him like a dog with a rat, and the world became a blur of agony and darkness.

*"LIN!"*

The shadow panther's roar echoed through their bond. He felt his beasts surge with desperate fury—the lightning hawk diving, talons extended; the wind-snake coiling around the creature's neck, venom glands pumping; the shadow panther materializing behind the beast, claws aimed at its spine.

But Lin was inside the creature's mouth. Close enough to see the core fragment pulsing in its throat.

Close enough to touch it.

He reached out with his free hand, ignoring the pain of his shattered shoulder, and grabbed the fragment.

[Beast Resonance: Core Absorption]

The world exploded.

Lin felt the corruption flood into his body—toxic, malevolent, hungry. It tried to consume him, to twist his flesh and corrupt his core. But something else stirred inside him. The [Beast Resonance] ability, the thing that let him merge with his beasts, recognized the corruption for what it was.

Power. Raw, untamed, dangerous power.

He could take it. Absorb it. Make it his.

*"No,"* the wind-snake's voice was sharp, urgent. *"That path leads to madness. You'll become one of them."*

*"He's right,"* the shadow panther added. *"Corruption isn't meant to be controlled. It controls you."*

But Lin could feel the fragment pulsing in his hand, could feel the centuries of cultivation—stolen from countless cultivators—thrumming through it. The power was intoxicating. With this, he could skip years of cultivation. He could match the sect elders. He could—

His vision doubled, and he saw himself reflected in the corrupted beast's remaining eye. Saw the black qi leaking from his own pores, the red tinge creeping into his irises.

He was becoming like it.

With a roar of effort, Lin ripped the fragment free.

The corrupted beast collapsed instantly, its body dissolving into black sludge. Lin fell to the ground, the fragment still clutched in his bloody hand. The disciples rushed forward, but he waved them back.

"Don't touch me," he gasped. "The corruption—"

"We have medicine," the eldest disciple said, reaching into his robe. "Our sect can purify—"

"I said don't touch me!"

His voice cracked with something that wasn't entirely human. The disciples froze, staring at him with wide eyes. Lin looked down at his hand. The veins were black, crawling up his arm like roots.

The fragment had done something to him. Changed something.

*"You need to expel it,"* the lightning hawk said, landing nearby. *"Now. Before it reaches your core."*

Lin closed his eyes and focused. His cultivation base—two layers of the Beast Melding Realm—pushed back against the corruption. The shadow panther's dark energy mixed with the wind-snake's purification and the lightning hawk's cleansing fire. Together, they drove the corruption back, forcing it out through his pores.

Black mist rose from his body, dissipating into the swamp air.

When he opened his eyes, they were normal again. But the fragment in his hand still pulsed with stolen power.

"What are you going to do with that?" the girl with braids asked, her voice small.

Lin looked at the fragment. It was warm in his palm, almost alive. He could feel the cultivators whose cores had been consumed to create it—their last moments, their terror, their despair.

"I'm going to find out who made this," he said. "And I'm going to make them pay."

The eldest disciple stepped forward, his expression grim. "That's... that's going to be difficult. The corruption has been spreading for months. We were sent to investigate a disturbance in this swamp, but we didn't expect—" He gestured at the dissolving remains. "This."

"What disturbance?" Lin asked, pocketing the fragment carefully.

"A cave. About a mile east of here." The disciple's voice dropped. "The elders detected a massive concentration of dark qi. They think it might be a nest."

A nest. Lin's mind raced. If someone was creating corrupted beasts, they'd need a base of operations. A place to conduct their experiments. A place to store their victims.

"Take me there."

The disciples exchanged glances. "You're injured," the girl said. "You should—"

"I said take me there."

His tone brooked no argument. The eldest disciple nodded slowly, then turned and began walking east. The others followed, casting nervous glances back at Lin.

He fell in step behind them, his beasts moving through the shadows around him. The fragment burned in his pocket, a constant reminder of what he'd almost become.

*"You're making a mistake,"* the shadow panther said quietly. *"That cave will be guarded by things worse than corrupted beasts."*

"Probably."

*"And you're going in with a shattered shoulder and a half-corroded cultivation base."*

"Yep."

*"...You're insane, little tamer."*

Lin smiled, though it didn't reach his eyes. "Maybe. But someone has to stop this. And right now, I'm the only one who can."
