They walked for twenty minutes through the fetid swamp. The mist grew thicker, the air heavier. Strange sounds echoed from the darkness—things moving, things feeding, things waiting.

The cave mouth appeared suddenly, hidden behind a curtain of hanging vines. It was larger than Lin had expected, easily thirty feet across, and it descended into darkness that not even the shadow panther's eyes could penetrate.

But it wasn't the cave that made Lin stop.

It was the symbol carved above its entrance. A circle with three intersecting lines, each one ending in a sharp point.

The mark of the Soul Devourer Sect.

Lin's blood turned to ice. The Soul Devourer Sect had been destroyed fifty years ago, their members hunted to extinction by every major power in the valley. They were the ones who had pioneered the art of consuming cultivation cores—not from beasts, but from humans.

They were the ones who had created the first corrupted beasts.

"That's impossible," he whispered. "They're all dead."

"Apparently not," the eldest disciple said, his voice hollow. "And if they're back..."

Lin finished the thought. "Then the entire valley is in danger."

He stepped toward the cave, but the girl grabbed his arm. "You can't go in there alone. We'll send word to our sect, gather reinforcements—"

"By the time they arrive, it'll be too late." Lin gently freed his arm. "I have three bonded beasts and a Resonance ability that lets me merge their powers. I'm not going in blind."

He turned to face the darkness of the cave. The corruption inside pulsed like a heartbeat, calling to the fragment in his pocket.

*"This is stupid,"* the wind-snake muttered.

*"Agreed,"* the lightning hawk added.

*"Absolutely idiotic,"* the shadow panther finished.

Lin grinned. "Then we'll fit right in."

He stepped into the cave, and the darkness swallowed him whole.

Behind him, the girl with braids watched him disappear. Then she turned to her companions, her expression hardening into something far older than her years.

"We need to warn the elders. Not about the cave." She paused, her voice dropping to a whisper. "About him."

"What do you mean?"

She looked back at the cave entrance, where the last traces of Lin's shadow were fading into the black.

"I mean he absorbed a core fragment and lived. He resisted corruption that would have killed any other cultivator in minutes." Her eyes narrowed. "He's not just a beast tamer. He's something else entirely. And if the Soul Devourers are back..."

She didn't finish the sentence.

She didn't need to.

title: Chapter 17
content:
CHAPTER 17: The Price of Power

Lin's fist connected with the corrupted beast's spine.

The impact sent shockwaves through his arm, but the merged power of three beasts absorbed the recoil. Bone cracked beneath his knuckles. The creature stumbled, black ichor spraying from the wound.

*"Again,"* the shadow panther whispered in his mind. *"It's not dead."*

Lin didn't need the encouragement. He pivoted, shadows gathering around his legs like living armor, and kicked the beast's knee. The joint buckled. The creature fell, shaking the ground.

The disciples watching from the rock formation shouted in relief. One of them—a young woman with a jade hairpin and a torn blue robe—raised her hand. A golden light surrounded her. "Beast Merge: Solar Crane!"

A massive bird of light materialized behind her, its wings spreading like dawn breaking over mountains. She didn't attack. Instead, she released a wave of healing energy that washed over her companions.

Lin noticed. *A healer-type beast tamer. Rare.*

But he had no time to study. The corrupted beast roared again, forcing itself upright despite its broken leg. Its red eyes locked onto him with undeniable hatred.

"Stay back," Lin said, not to the disciples but to his own beasts. "I want to try something."

*"That's stupid,"* the lightning hawk crackled. *"It's three times your size."*

*"That's why it's perfect."*

Lin deactivated the merge.

The power left him like a tide receding. His vision narrowed. The world felt smaller, quieter. He was just a boy again, standing before a nightmare.

The corrupted beast lunged.

Lin raised his hand. He didn't activate Beast Resonance. He didn't call on any of his beasts. He just... reached out.

*You're not a monster,* he thought, aiming the words not at the creature, but at the corruption itself. *You're a wound. A disease. And diseases can be cured.*

The black qi covering the beast's body rippled.

For a single heartbeat, the red eyes flickered. Something beneath the madness surfaced—a spark of recognition, of pain, of pleading. The creature's claw stopped inches from Lin's face.

Then the corruption surged back, stronger than before.

"KILL ME!" the beast screamed.

Not a roar. Words.

Human words.

Lin's blood went cold. "Who—"

The creature's body convulsed. Flesh tore. Bones snapped. From the chest cavity, a hand emerged—pale, human, covered in black veins. Then an arm. Then a face.

A man's face.

Half-transformed, half-devoured, his upper body bursting from the corrupted beast's torso like a parasite emerging from its host. His eyes were the same burning red, but his mouth moved, forming words through cracked lips.

"Please... before I become... one of them..."

Lin's heart pounded. "Who are you?"

"No time!" the man screamed. "The Valley Master—he's not—he's been corrupted for years! He's the source! He's—

The man's eyes went blank. The corruption swallowed him whole. His body dissolved back into the beast's flesh, and the creature's roar shook the heavens.

It charged.

Lin activated Beast Resonance so fast his meridians screamed in protest. Shadow. Lightning. Venom. The powers slammed into him, and he became something beyond human once more.

He ducked under the creature's swing, slid between its legs, and drove his hand into the spot where the man's face had emerged. His fingers found a core—rough, pulsing, corrupted.

He ripped it out.

The beast collapsed, its body dissolving into black mist that evaporated into the morning air.

Lin stood over the remains, breathing hard, the corrupted core still clutched in his hand. It pulsed once, twice, then crumbled to ash.

"No," he whispered. "No, no, no—"

He had killed him. He had killed a *person*.

The disciples approached cautiously. The healer-type girl bowed. "Thank you for saving us. I am Yue Ling, disciple of the Solar Crane Sect. These are my companions."

Lin didn't hear her. He was staring at the ash in his hands, remembering the man's words.

*The Valley Master—he's been corrupted for years.*

*He's the source.*

"Where is the Valley Master?" Lin asked, his voice hollow.

Yue Ling blinked. "The Valley Master? He rarely leaves the central temple. Why do you—"

"Take me there."

"That's impossible. Outsiders aren't allowed near the temple without an audience, and those are scheduled months in advance, and—"

"I don't care about your rules." Lin raised his head. His eyes, still flickering with merged beast essence, met hers. "The Valley Master is corrupted. He's the source of the corruption spreading through Thousand Beast Valley. And I just killed a man because of it."

The disciples went silent.

Yue Ling's face paled. "That's... that's a serious accusation. If you're wrong—"

"I'm not wrong."

"Even if you're right, what can you do? You're a third-level Beast Melding disciple. The Valley Master is a ninth-level Soul Fusion expert. He commands a Phoenix-Tailed Dragon Turtle. He could kill you by *breathing* in your direction."

Lin looked at his hands. The shadow panther's power pulsed beneath his skin. The lightning hawk's energy crackled along his fingers. The wind-snake's venom coiled in his blood.

Three beasts. Three fragments of power.

Was it enough?

*No,* he realized. *Not even close.*

But the man had begged for death. Begged for mercy. And Lin had given it. If he walked away now, that man's death meant nothing.

"I need to get stronger," Lin said. "Faster. How long do I have before the Valley Master's corruption spreads beyond the valley?"

Yue Ling exchanged glances with her companions. "The outer sects have been containing it. But the barriers are weakening. A month. Maybe less."

A month.

Lin turned away from the corpse and faced the swamp. Somewhere beyond it, the Valley Master sat in his temple, rotting from the inside out, pretending to be a savior while spreading plague.

"I have a month to become strong enough to kill a ninth-level Soul Fusion expert."

The shadow panther laughed in his mind. *"Impossible."*

*"Then we'll make the impossible possible."*

The lightning hawk shrieked agreement. The wind-snake tightened its coils, ready for anything.

Lin started walking.

"Wait!" Yue Ling called after him. "Where are you going?"

"To the Forbidden Depths."

The disciples gasped.

The Forbidden Depths. A chasm at the edge of Thousand Beast Valley where even the beasts refused to go. Legend said that ancient spirit beasts, remnants of a forgotten era, slumbered at the bottom. Legend said that those who entered never returned.

"Are you insane? No one survives the Depths! The beasts there are rank five, six, *seven!* They'll kill you before you take three steps!"

Lin didn't stop walking.

"Then I'll take four steps before I die."

Yue Ling bit her lip. She looked at her companions, then back at Lin's retreating figure. "Wait," she said again, softer this time. "I know someone. A outcast elder, living in the mountains beyond the eastern ridge. He was banished from the central temple twenty years ago for speaking against the Valley Master. If anyone knows the truth, it's him."

Lin paused.

"His name is Elder Feng. He was the strongest beast tamer in the valley before his exile. He bonded with a Twilight Kirin—a rank six mythic beast. If he's still alive..." She trailed off, as if doubting her own words.

Lin turned. "Why are you helping me?"

Yue Ling met his gaze. "Because I've seen the corruption spread. I've lost friends to it. If the Valley Master is truly the source..." She swallowed. "Then I want him stopped. And you're the first person I've met who actually tried to save a corrupted beast instead of killing it."

Lin nodded slowly. "The eastern ridge. Elder Feng. Twilight Kirin."

"I'll draw you a map."

An hour later, Lin stood at the base of the eastern ridge, a crude map in his hands and three beasts bonded in his soul. The sun was setting, painting the sky in shades of blood and gold.

The shadow panther emerged from Lin's shadow. *"The healer is hiding something."*

"Everyone's hiding something."

*"Are you going to trust her?"*

"No." Lin folded the map. "But I'm going to use her information. There's a difference."

He climbed.

The mountain was steep, covered in jagged rocks and twisted trees. The wind howled, carrying the scent of snow from peaks he couldn't see. His beasts strained under the elevation, but they didn't complain.

Halfway up, Lin found a path—not natural, but carved. Old stone steps, worn smooth by centuries of rain, winding upward like a serpent's spine.

He followed them.

At the top, he found a hut. Small. Crude. A single lantern flickered in the window, casting a warm orange glow over a garden of herbs and flowers.

And sitting on the porch, a man with white hair and a scarred face, sharpening a knife.

"You're early," the man said without looking up. "I wasn't expecting visitors for another three decades."

Lin stopped. "Are you Elder Feng?"

The man finally raised his head. His eyes were pale blue, almost white, and they held an exhaustion that seemed older than the mountains themselves.

"I was." He set down the knife. "But Elder Feng died twenty years ago. I'm just a ghost now."

"I need your help."

"Everyone needs help. That's why they come here. That's why they leave disappointed." The man leaned back. "But you're different. You smell like three beasts, but you walk like one. And you've got death in your eyes."

Not a question. An observation.

"I killed a man today," Lin said. "He was trapped inside a corrupted beast. He begged me to end it. And I did."

The man's expression didn't change. "The corruption."

"Yes."

"And you want to stop it."

"Yes."

"Then you're a fool." The man stood, walking toward his garden. "The Valley Master isn't just corrupted. He *is* the corruption. He's been feeding it for decades, using the beasts of the valley as vessels, growing stronger with every death. You can't stop him by getting stronger. You can't stop him with more beasts."

Lin's fists clenched. "Then how?"

Elder Feng stopped. He turned, and for the first time, his eyes held something other than exhaustion.

Pity.

"You can't," he said. "The only way to stop the Valley Master is to become something that even corruption fears. Something that exists beyond the boundaries of beast and tamer."

Lin's breath caught. "What do you mean?"

Elder Feng pointed at Lin's chest. "You have Beast Resonance. I can feel it. It's rare. It's dangerous. And it's the only thing that might save you."

"Then teach me."

"Teach you?" Elder Feng laughed—a dry, bitter sound. "Boy, Beast Resonance isn't a technique. It's a curse. The more you use it, the more you merge with your beasts, the less human you become. One day, you'll wake up and realize you can't remember your own face. You'll look in a mirror and see a monster."

Lin thought of the corrupted man. Of his red eyes. Of

title: The Corruption's True Face
content:
Lin Xiao's body sang with power as he moved through the corrupted beast's attack, shadow and lightning trailing behind him like a cape of impossibility. The creature's massive claw tore through empty air where he'd been standing a heartbeat before.

"Keep it distracted!" he shouted to the pinned disciples. "I need time to find its core!"

One of the disciples—a girl with dirt-streaked face and desperate eyes—raised her trembling hands. A spirit bloom erupted from her palms, thorny vines shooting toward the corrupted beast's legs. The roots wrapped around its ankles, slowing its advance.

"We're from the Verdant Lotus Sect!" she cried. "I'm Mei Ling! Please—our master was taken three days ago by one of these things. We came to rescue her."

Lin's eyes widened as he dodged another swipe. "Taken? Since when do corrupted beasts take prisoners?"

"Since never!" another disciple shouted, an older boy with a broken arm. "That's why we're here. Something's wrong. These creatures aren't just mindless anymore. They're *herding* us."

The corrupted beast howled, black qi exploding from its body. The vines snapped like wet paper. Mei Ling screamed as the backlash sent her flying.

*"Little tamer,"* the shadow panther's voice cut through Lin's mind, urgent and sharp. *"There is something inside this creature. Something that *thinks*."*

Lin froze. "What do you mean?"

*"I have hunted corrupted beasts before. They are rage and hunger given form. But this one... it watches. It *plans*. It positioned itself between us and the sect disciples intentionally."*

The lightning hawk shrieked confirmation from above. *"It drove them here. Herded them like prey toward a kill zone."*

Lin's blood ran cold. Corrupted beasts weren't supposed to be intelligent. They were the result of broken bonds, shattered cultivation, and qi poisoning—mindless remnants of failed beast tamers. They didn't *plan*.

But as he studied the creature, he saw what the panther meant. The beast's red eyes weren't wild. They tracked him with predatory calculation. When he feinted left, it adjusted its stance before he'd fully committed to the movement. When Mei Ling tried to crawl away, the creature's tail lashed out, forcing her back into position.

It was *corralling* them.

"Everyone, stay close to me!" Lin commanded. He activated [Beast Resonance: Double Merge], pulling the panther and hawk into his body while keeping the wind-snake coiled around his arm for venom support. Shadows wrapped around his frame, and lightning crackled between his fingers.

The corrupted beast charged.

Lin met it head-on.

He moved like smoke and struck like thunder. His fist connected with the creature's jaw, and the impact sent shockwaves through the swamp. But instead of staggering, the beast *absorbed* the blow, its flesh rippling like water.

*"It's learning,"* the panther hissed. *"Every attack you use, it adapts."*

Lin gritted his teeth. He'd never faced anything like this. Normal corrupted beasts were predictable—they attacked in patterns, relying on brute force. This one was different. It studied. It *evolved*.

He needed to end this fast.

"Wind-snake, venom strike!" he shouted, extending his arm. The snake launched like a living spear, fangs sinking into the creature's shoulder. Black venom pumped into the wound.

The corrupted beast roared, but instead of retreating, it *smiled*.

That was wrong. Corrupted beasts didn't smile.

The wound where the snake had bitten began to *heal*, black tendrils knitting flesh back together. But worse—the snake's venom was being *absorbed*, turning the creature's skin a sickly green.

*"It took my venom,"* the wind-snake's voice trembled through the bond. *"It's adapting to it. Using it."*

Lin's mind raced. If the beast could adapt to his attacks, he couldn't win through brute force. He needed to think differently.

*"The core,"* the shadow panther whispered. *"Every corrupted beast has a corruption core. Destroy that, and the body falls."*

"Right." Lin focused his senses, extending his perception through the bond. He could feel the creature's energy now—a pulsing knot of dark qi buried deep in its chest, protected by layers of corrupted flesh and bone.

But there was something else. Something *beyond* the core.

A thread of consciousness, faint but unmistakable, stretching from the beast's core into the swamp behind it. As if something else was *controlling* it.

"Mei Ling!" Lin called out. "When I give the signal, I need you to bind its legs completely. Can you do that?"

The girl's face was pale, but she nodded. "I'll try."

"Don't try. *Do* it."

Lin took a deep breath. This was going to hurt.

He deactivated [Beast Resonance], letting the merged beasts separate back into their individual forms. The shadow panther materialized at his side, hackles raised. The lightning hawk circled overhead, sparks cascading from its wings. The wind-snake wrapped around his waist, tail flicking nervously.

"All three of you," Lin said, his voice low. "I need you to hit it at the same time. Full power. Draw its attention to you."

*"And what will you do, little tamer?"* the panther asked.

Lin's eyes hardened. "I'm going to find out what's pulling its strings."

He activated [Beast Resonance: Triple Merge] one more time, but this time, he didn't use the power for combat. Instead, he *pushed* his perception outward, following that thread of consciousness back to its source.

The world blurred around him. Colors bled together. Sounds distorted into a single harmonic note.

And then he saw it.

Deep beneath the swamp, in a cavern that shouldn't exist, a massive corruption core pulsed like a black heart. It was easily ten feet across, its surface covered in runes that writhed and shifted like living things. Tendrils of black qi extended from it, burrowing through the earth, connecting to every corrupted beast in the valley.

But that wasn't the worst part.

The worst part was the figure suspended inside the core.

A woman. Middle-aged. Her eyes were closed, and her body was covered in corruption scars. But she was *alive*. Barely. Her hands were bound above her head by chains of black qi, and her mouth hung open in a silent scream.

*"Mei Ling's master,"* Lin breathed. *"She's being used as a battery."*

The realization hit him like a physical blow. This wasn't a natural corruption event. Someone—something—was *farming* beasts and tamers, using them to fuel a massive corruption core. And if the core grew strong enough...

*"Lin Xiao!"*

The panther's scream snapped him back to reality. The corrupted beast had broken through his beasts' assault, its body now covered in cracks that leaked black fire. It was charging directly at Mei Ling.

"No!"

Lin moved on instinct. He threw himself between the beast and the disciples, activating [Beast Resonance: Full Merge]. All three beasts merged into his body at once, pushing his cultivation past its limits. His meridians screamed in protest as power flooded through him.

He caught the creature's charge with both hands, digging his heels into the mud. The impact sent cracks spidering through the ground, but he held. His arms trembled. Blood trickled from his nose.

"You want them?" he growled, teeth gritted. "Come through me first."

The corrupted beast's eyes narrowed. For a moment, Lin swore he saw intelligence in those crimson depths. Recognition. And then something worse:

*Amusement.*

*"You see it now, little tamer,"* a voice whispered in his mind. It wasn't the panther, the hawk, or the snake. It was something else. Something ancient and cold. *"You see the truth of this valley. You see what lies beneath."*

Lin's blood went cold. "Who are you?"

*"I am what your ancestors feared. I am what they sealed away. And I am waking up."* The voice laughed, and the sound was like breaking bones. *"Enjoy these final moments, Lin Xiao. Soon, everyone you love will become part of me."*

The corrupted beast exploded.

Black qi erupted in a wave, throwing Lin backward. He crashed into the rock formation, pain exploding through his ribs. When he looked up, the creature was gone. Vanished. Reduced to ash.

But the thread of consciousness remained, pulsing like a heartbeat.

And it was growing stronger.

Mei Ling ran to his side, her face ashen. "What was that? What did you see?"

Lin coughed, blood splattering his hand. "We need to warn the valley. There's something beneath the swamp. Something that's been corrupting beasts deliberately." He looked at the disciples, his eyes burning with urgency. "And it's going to wake up soon."

The Verdant Lotus disciples exchanged terrified glances.

"How soon?" the boy with the broken arm whispered.

Lin stared at the spot where the corrupted beast had died, the echo of that ancient voice still ringing in his skull.

"Three days," he said. "At most."

The shadow panther growled low in its throat. *"Then we'd better find allies fast, little tamer. Because whatever that thing is, you can't fight it alone."*

Lin struggled to his feet, his body aching in ways he'd never felt before. He looked at his three bonded beasts, then at the terrified disciples, then at the path leading deeper into the swamp.

Three days.

He didn't know if that was enough time to prepare. He didn't know if *anyone* could stand against whatever was awakening beneath Thousand Beast Valley.

But he knew one thing for certain:

He was going to try.

"Come on," he said, wiping blood from his chin. "We've got work to do."

title: Chapter 19
content:
CHAPTER 19: The Fragment's Awakening

The corrupted beast's charge carried it through Lin Xiao's afterimage, its twisted claws raking through mist and shadow. Lin materialized fifteen feet to its left, three beasts' worth of power singing through his meridians like a symphony of destruction.

"Too slow," he whispered.

The shadow panther struck first—a blur of absolute darkness that severed tendons in the beast's left leg. The creature stumbled, roaring in agony, black qi spraying from the wound like arterial blood. Before it could recover, the lightning hawk descended, talons crackling with electricity that bypassed the corrupted flesh entirely and struck something deeper. Something *rotten*.

The wind-snake waited. Watching. Calculating.

Lin had learned something important in the weeks since bonding the shadow panther. Each beast had its role. The hawk was aggression—strike fast, strike hard. The panther was precision—find the weakness, exploit it. The snake was patience—wait for the killing blow.

And Lin was the conductor of their symphony.

"Second wave," he commanded.

The panther dissolved into shadow, reappearing on the beast's blind side. The hawk circled higher, building charge. Lin himself stepped forward, drawing the creature's attention.

"Look at me," he said, his voice carrying an authority that surprised even himself. "I'm the one who's going to end you."

The corrupted beast's red eyes locked onto him. It charged again, faster this time, its ruined body somehow pushing past its limitations. Lin didn't flinch. He raised his hand—

*[Beast Resonance: Split Merge]*

The technique he'd developed three days ago. Instead of merging all three beasts into himself, he split his consciousness, merging with each beast individually while maintaining his own body as a separate vessel. It was like playing three instruments simultaneously while singing.

The panther lunged. The hawk dove. The snake struck.

Three attacks. One target. Perfect synchronization.

The corrupted beast's head separated from its body before it even realized it had been hit. The massive corpse continued forward three more steps before collapsing, black qi dissipating into the swamp air like dying smoke.

Silence.

The hunting party stared at Lin with a mixture of awe and terror. They were disciples from the Azure Frost Sect—their blue and white robes marked them clearly. Six of them, all at the fourth or fifth level of the Beast Melding Realm. Strong by normal standards.

Lin had just killed something that would have killed all of them in a heartbeat.

"Who... who are you?" the lead disciple asked, her voice trembling.

"Lin Xiao. Thousand Beast Valley."

"The *cripple*?" one of the male disciples blurted out, then immediately looked like he wanted to swallow his own tongue.

Lin's smile didn't waver. "That's me."

The shadow panther materialized beside him, its golden eyes fixed on the disciples with predatory interest. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, still crackling with residual electricity. The wind-snake coiled around his waist, tongue flickering.

Three beasts. A cripple's team.

"This is impossible," the female lead murmured. "You're not even in the Spirit Beast Realm. How did you—"

"Questions for another time," Lin interrupted. "What were you doing out here? This part of the swamp is restricted."

The disciples exchanged glances. Something passed between them—fear, but not the fear of a beast. The fear of a secret.

"We were tracking a corrupted beast," the lead said carefully. "But not like this one. A smaller one. We thought..."

"You thought you could handle it."

Silence.

"And then you found this one instead."

More silence. The lead disciple's hands were shaking. Lin noticed something else—the way her eyes kept darting to a pouch at her belt. A spatial pouch. Whatever was inside, it was more important than the beast they'd been hunting.

"What's in the pouch?" Lin asked.

"Nothing."

"Liar."

The word came out cold, and Lin felt all three of his beasts respond to his sudden suspicion. The panther growled. The hawk's feathers bristled. The snake hissed.

The disciples stepped back, forming a defensive circle. Their hands went to their own beasts—summoning talismans, ready to release whatever creatures they'd bonded.

"I wouldn't," Lin said. "I really wouldn't."

The female lead's face hardened. She reached into the pouch and pulled out a small, crystalline object. It pulsed with a sickly red light—the same light that had been in the corrupted beast's eyes.

"A corruption fragment," Lin breathed. "You were *collecting* them."

"We're researching a cure," she said quickly. "The Azure Frost Sect has been studying corruption for months. We've found that fragments contain traces of the original beast's soul. If we can purify them—"

"You're carrying a piece of a plague that's been ripping through the valley for months, and you're *studying* it?"

"We know what we're doing."

"Do you?" Lin took a step forward, and the disciples tensed. "Because that beast you just watched me kill? It wasn't naturally corrupted. It was *attracted*. Someone or something called it here."

The female lead's face went pale.

"How do you know that?"

Lin didn't answer. He'd felt it—during the battle, when he'd merged with his beasts, his senses had extended beyond the physical. He'd felt a pull from the corruption fragment, like a beacon calling out to the darkness.

And he'd felt something else. Something deeper. Something that had been waiting in the swamp since before he'd arrived.

"You need to leave," Lin said. "Now. Take that fragment back to your sect and tell your elders what happened here. Tell them the corruption is changing."

"Changing how?"

"It's becoming aware."

The words hung in the air, heavy with implication. The disciples looked at each other, then back at Lin. The female lead tucked the fragment back into her pouch.

"We'll report this," she said. "But Lin Xiao... be careful. The Thousand Beast Valley isn't what it used to be. There are things moving in the shadows that even the sect masters don't understand."

She turned and led her group away, disappearing into the mist.

Lin watched them go, his beasts settling around him. The panther's voice echoed in his mind.

*You let them live.*

"They're not our enemies."

*They carry corruption. Corruption spreads. It's simple.*

"Nothing is simple anymore."

The panther fell silent, but Lin could feel its disapproval. It was a creature of the shadows, of survival. It didn't understand mercy.

The wind-snake, however, understood perfectly. It had been with Lin from the beginning, through every failure and triumph. It recognized the path they were walking.

*Master,* the snake's voice whispered, softer than the panther's. *What did you feel?*

Lin closed his eyes, reaching out with his senses. The corruption fragment was already fading from his awareness, carried away by the Azure Frost disciples. But the other presence—the one that had been waiting—it was still there.

And it was getting closer.

"Something's coming," Lin said. "Something old."

*How old?*

"Older than the valley. Older than the sects. Older than anything we've ever encountered."

The lightning hawk shrieked, a warning cry that echoed through the swamp. Lin's eyes snapped open, and he saw it—a ripple in the mist, like something massive moving beneath a blanket of fog.

"Three beasts," he murmured, "might not be enough."

The ripple stopped, directly in front of him. The mist parted, and Lin felt a presence press against his consciousness—a presence so vast, so ancient, that his Beast Resonance quivered in response.

A voice spoke, not through sound, but directly into his mind.

*Little tamer. You carry the Mark of Resonance.*

Lin's blood ran cold. No one knew about Beast Resonance. No one except him and his beasts.

*Who are you?* he demanded.

The presence laughed—a sound like grinding mountains and crashing waves.

*I am what the valley was built to contain. I am what your ancestors feared. I am the First Beast, the one who taught the first tamer how to bond.*

A pause.

*And I have been watching you, Lin Xiao. You are the first in a thousand years to awaken the Resonance fully. The first who might actually be worthy.*

The mist cleared, and Lin saw it—a single eye, larger than a house, staring at him from the darkness. An eye of pure, unfathomable gold.

*Come find me,* the voice said. *Before the corruption does.*

The eye blinked, and the presence vanished.

Lin stood frozen, sweat pouring down his face, his beasts trembling around him. The First Beast. The legend that every child in the valley grew up hearing—the primordial entity that had created the very concept of beast taming.

It was real.

And it wanted him.

*"Well,"* the shadow panther said, breaking the silence, *"that's not terrifying at all."*

Lin let out a breath he didn't know he'd been holding. "We need to find it."

*Find it?* The panther's mental voice was incredulous. *Find the being that just spoke to you from across the entire valley? How?*

Lin looked at his hands—hands that had merged with three beasts, hands that had killed a corrupted monster, hands that now trembled with the weight of a destiny he'd never asked for.

"I don't know," he admitted. "But I think... I think it's been waiting for me."

A new scream echoed through the swamp, closer this time. Louder. More desperate.

And then another.

And another.

The corruption fragments the Azure Frost disciples had been collecting—there had been more than one. Lin felt them now, scattered across the swamp, each one a beacon calling to the darkness.

The darkness was answering.

*Master,* the wind-snake said urgently. *They're coming.*

Lin turned, his beasts readying for battle. The mist churned with shapes—dozens of shapes, all glowing with that sickly red light.

"Then we fight," he said.

But even as the words left his mouth, Lin felt something else stirring deep within the valley. The First Beast's awakening had not gone unnoticed. And the corruption that had been spreading for months...

It had a source.

And that source was finally making its move.

title: The Price of Power
content:
The corrupted beast's claw swept through empty air where Lin had stood a heartbeat ago. Shadow merged with shadow as he reappeared twenty feet to the left, his body humming with the combined essence of three beasts.

"Keep pressure on its flanks!" he shouted to the pinned disciples. "Don't let it focus!"

One of them—a girl with dirt-streaked face and trembling hands—nodded and unleashed a fire-aspected viper. It struck the creature's leg, sizzling against corrupted flesh. The beast howled, turning its attention toward her.

*Bad move.*

Lin surged forward, lightning crackling along his arms. The [Triple Merge] made him feel invincible, like he could tear mountains apart with his bare hands. He slammed both palms into the creature's knee joint.

Bone cracked. The beast stumbled.

The shadow panther materialized behind its head, claws raking across its neck. Black qi splattered like oil. The wind-snake struck at the same moment, fangs sinking into the creature's shoulder, flooding its veins with paralytic venom.

For a moment, Lin thought they had won.

Then the corrupted beast *screamed*.

The sound wasn't just noise—it was *force*. A shockwave of pure corruption blasted outward, throwing Lin back fifty feet. He crashed through a tree, wood splintering around him. The [Triple Merge] shattered, leaving him gasping, his three beasts scattering.

"Tamer!" The shadow panther's voice was urgent in his mind. "It's going to—"

The beast charged.

Lin rolled, barely avoiding a stomp that cratered the ground where he'd been. He came up coughing, blood in his mouth. The corruption was stronger than anything he'd faced before. This wasn't a normal corrupted beast—this was something *made*.

"Who sent you?" he spat, buying time.

The beast's red eyes flickered. Something intelligent moved behind them. "The valley... belongs to the *true* masters now," it rasped. Its voice was wrong—multiple voices layered on top of each other, as if dozens of creatures were speaking through one throat. "You are... an anomaly. The Lord of Whispers... wants you eliminated."

*The Lord of Whispers.*

Lin had heard that name before. Whispered in the market. Murmured around campfires. A figure who had been building power in the valley's depths for years, gathering corrupted beasts to his cause.

"So he sent a hit squad," Lin said, forcing a grin. "I'm flattered."

The beast lunged again.

This time, Lin was ready. He activated [Beast Resonance] not for power, but for *precision*. The wind-snake's senses merged with his own, letting him see the creature's movements a fraction of a second before they happened. The lightning hawk's speed coursed through his legs. The shadow panther's stealth wrapped around him like a cloak.

He ducked under the claw, spun, and drove his fist into the beast's chest.

The impact sent a shockwave through the clearing. The beast stumbled back, black qi gushing from the wound. But instead of falling, it *laughed*.

"Foolish child," it said, the voices twisting. "You think you can defeat me? I am not a beast. I am a *vessel*."

The creature's skin split open.

Lin's blood ran cold.

Dozens of smaller corrupted beasts poured out of the wound—snakes, rats, birds, all twisted and wrong, their eyes burning with the same red light. They swarmed across the ground, climbing trees, filling the air.

"Kill him," the vessel said. "Bring his core to the Lord."

The disciples screamed as the swarm descended. Lin's lightning hawk dove, electrocuting a dozen of them, but more kept coming. The wind-snake coiled protectively around Lin's legs, hissing. The shadow panther appeared at his side, claws dripping with corrupted blood.

"We need to retreat," the panther said.

"We can't leave them!"

"Look at them, tamer. They're already dead."

Lin turned. The fire-aspected disciple was on the ground, a corrupted rat burrowing into her chest. Her viper lay severed in two pieces nearby. The other hunters were faring no better—one by one, they fell to the swarm.

Rage built in Lin's chest. Hot. Uncontrollable.

*No.*

He wouldn't run. He wouldn't let more people die because he was too weak.

"[Triple Merge]," he whispered. "Full output."

"Tamer, that will—"

"I know what it will do."

The shadow panther fell silent. Then, reluctantly, it nodded.

The three beasts flowed into him like rivers converging into an ocean. The sensation was overwhelming—pain and pleasure and power all at once. Lin felt his meridians stretch, felt his cultivation base strain against its limits. The third level of the Beast Melding Realm cracked, then shattered.

*Fourth level.*

Power exploded from him in a wave.

The swarm froze. Every corrupted creature in the clearing turned to look at him, their red eyes reflecting his own changed appearance. Lin's skin had gone pale, almost translucent, and shadows writhed beneath it like living tattoos. Lightning arced between his fingers. His eyes had become slitted, serpentine.

"I'll give you one chance," he said, his voice layered with three different tones. "Tell me where the Lord of Whispers is hiding. Or I'll tear the answer from your core."

The vessel laughed again. "You think that changes anything? The Lord has already won. The corruption has spread to the valley's heart. By tomorrow, every beast in Thousand Beast Valley will belong to him."

"Then I'll stop him tomorrow."

Lin moved.

He was faster than sound. He was darkness given form. He struck the vessel a hundred times in a single second, each blow carrying the combined strength of three beasts. The creature's body disintegrated under the assault, black qi dispersing into the air like smoke.

The swarm shrieked and collapsed, their connection to the vessel severed.

When Lin stopped, the clearing was silent. The vessel was nothing but a crater of corrupted ash. The disciples lay dead around him, their faces frozen in terror.

He stood alone in the aftermath, breathing hard.

"Tamer." The shadow panther's voice was weak. "Your body..."

Lin looked down.

His skin was cracking.

Thin lines of black energy traced across his arms, his chest, his face. The corruption was spreading through him, seeping into his meridians from the vessel's death throes. He could feel it eating at his cultivation, poisoning his core.

*No. No, no, no—*

He fell to his knees, clawing at his own skin. The shadow panther materialized beside him, pressing its head against his chest.

"Let me absorb it," it said. "I can—"

"You'll die."

"Then we die together."

Lin shook his head. "No. We're not dying today."

He forced himself to stand. The world swam around him. The corruption was spreading faster now, reaching toward his heart. He had hours, maybe less.

A noise came from the forest. Footsteps. Many of them.

Lin turned, ready to fight.

A group of figures emerged from the mist. They wore robes of deep crimson, their faces hidden behind bone masks. At their head walked a woman with silver hair and eyes like frozen lakes.

"You," she said, looking at Lin. "You killed one of the Lord's vessels."

"Who's asking?"

"I am Mei Ling, third disciple of the Crimson Fang Sect." She tilted her head. "And you are dying."

"Perceptive."

Mei Ling stepped closer, ignoring the warning hiss from Lin's wind-snake. She studied him with cold, clinical interest. "The corruption has reached your dantian. In three hours, you will become one of them."

"Thanks for the timeline."

"I can save you."

Lin's eyes narrowed. "At what cost?"

Mei Ling smiled. It was not a kind expression. "The Lord of Whispers has a weakness. A beast—his first beast, bonded before the corruption took him. If that beast dies, the Lord dies with it."

"You want me to kill it."

"I want you to *find* it." She reached into her robe and pulled out a small jade token, etched with symbols that writhed and shifted. "This will lead you to the beast's location. In return, I will purge the corruption from your body."

Lin stared at the token. His skin was burning now, the black lines reaching his collarbone.

"How do I know you're telling the truth?"

"You don't." Mei Ling's smile widened. "But you're out of options, little tamer. Accept my deal, or become another mindless monster in the Lord's army. Choose."

The shadow panther pressed closer, its warmth a comfort against the cold spreading through his veins. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, feathers crackling with weak sparks. The wind-snake coiled around his wrist, a living bracelet.

His beasts. His partners.

They believed in him.

Lin reached out and took the token.

"Fine. But if you betray me—"

"I won't." Mei Ling placed her palm against his chest. A cold energy flowed into him, pushing back the corruption. The black lines receded, the burning faded. Lin gasped as the poison was forced out of his body, gathering in a small black orb that floated above her hand.

"There." She crushed the orb, and it dissolved into nothing. "Your debt begins now."

Lin straightened, testing his limbs. The corruption was gone. His cultivation was stable.

"How long do I have to find this beast?"

"Three days." Mei Ling turned and walked back toward her disciples. "After that, the deal expires. And I will collect my payment in other ways."

She vanished into the mist, her crimson-robed followers trailing behind her.

Lin stood alone in the clearing, surrounded by dead disciples and the ash of a corrupted vessel. The jade token pulsed in his hand, warm and demanding.

"Three beasts," he muttered, looking at his partners. "Three days. One impossible mission."

The shadow panther snorted. "When have we ever done things the easy way?"

Lin laughed, despite everything. "Never. And I don't plan to start now."

He looked at the token. At the path ahead. At the future that was rapidly closing in around him.

"Let's go hunt a Lord."

title: The Corruption's First Test
content:
The corrupted beast's claw tore through the space where Lin had been standing, shredding mist and earth alike. But Lin was already gone, a whisper of shadow and wind, his three beasts' perceptions overlaying his own in a dizzying kaleidoscope of senses.

*Three angles. Three bodies. One mind.*

The wind-snake's heat-sensing tongue painted the world in thermal gradients—the corrupted beast's core burned like a forge, black qi pulsing with each ragged breath. The lightning hawk's eagle vision picked out every crack in the creature's hide, every weak point where the corruption had eaten deepest. And the shadow panther—the panther showed him darkness itself, the spaces between light where he could exist and move and *strike*.

"Spread out!" Lin shouted to the pinned disciples. "Don't let it corner you!"

They scrambled, robes torn and faces pale. Four of them, armed with standard-issue beast-taming equipment—capture ropes, suppression talismans, and a single spirit bell that chimed weakly as one of them rang it. Pathetic against something like this.

The corrupted beast turned, its red eyes locking onto Lin. Black drool dripped from its jaws, sizzling where it hit the ground. It had once been a Mountain Breaker Bear, a Rank 4 beast known for its raw strength. Now it was something else—a puppet of twisted qi, its original spirit long since consumed.

"Little tamer," the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind, "that thing is beyond your rank. Even with us three, we cannot match its power directly."

"I know," Lin replied, his voice calm despite the adrenaline flooding his system. "That's why we're not going to match it."

He raised his hand, and the lightning hawk screamed, diving straight at the bear's face. Electricity flared, blinding bright, and the creature recoiled, swatting at the air with massive paws.

*Now.*

Lin moved, shadow-walking through the panther's domain. One moment he was fifty feet away; the next, he was beneath the bear's guard, his hand pressed against the soft flesh of its throat.

*[Beast Resonance: Partial Merge - Wind-Snake Venom]*

His fingernails elongated, hardened, and turned a sickly green. He drove them deep into the bear's neck, pumping every drop of venom the snake could produce.

The bear roared, a sound that shook the ground and sent the disciples stumbling. It backhanded Lin across the clearing, and he crashed through a rotten tree trunk before slamming into a mud bank. Pain exploded through his ribs, and he tasted blood.

"Lin Xiao!" One of the disciples, a girl with short-cropped hair and desperate eyes, started toward him. "He's hurt!"

"Stay back!" Lin coughed, forcing himself upright. The wind-snake's regeneration was already working, knitting torn muscle and sealing cracked bone. "It's not dead yet."

It should have been. Mountain Breaker Bears had no resistance to wind-snake venom—that was basic beast taxonomy, taught in the first year of every academy in the valley. But the corruption burning in the bear's veins was burning out the poison too, black qi consuming the green venom like fire eating dry grass.

*The corruption is purifying it,* Lin realized, horror dawning. *It's not just a plague—it's a defense system.*

"Panther. Hawk. Buy me time."

The beasts obeyed without hesitation. The shadow panther materialized behind the bear, raking its claws across the creature's hamstrings. The lightning hawk circled above, diving and striking, keeping the bear's attention split. They couldn't hurt it badly, but they could slow it down.

Lin closed his eyes, ignoring the sounds of battle, and reached out with his [Beast Resonance].

*What are you really?*

His awareness pressed against the corrupted beast's surface, and immediately he recoiled. The creature's spirit—what was left of it—was a screaming void, a darkness so complete it threatened to swallow his consciousness. But beneath that, buried deep in the corruption's core, he felt something else.

A pattern. Like a seal, or a brand, etched into the very essence of the beast's qi.

*Someone did this. Someone made this.*

"Lin Xiao!" The panther's mental voice was sharp, urgent. "It's breaking through!"

Lin opened his eyes. The bear had shrugged off the venom entirely now, its wounds closing with horrifying speed. The corruption was spreading, tendrils of black qi reaching out from its body like grasping hands. Where they touched the ground, the plants withered and died.

He had seconds. Maybe less.

*Think. The corruption is qi-based. It consumes normal qi, but what about merged qi? What about something that's not quite beast, not quite human, but something new?*

Lin had never tried this before. It could kill him. It could shatter his cultivation and leave him a cripple for life.

But he was already the laughingstock of the valley. He had nothing left to lose.

"All three," he breathed. "Full merge. Now."

*[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge - Full Synchronization]*

The world stopped.

Lin felt himself dissolving—not dying, but *expanding*. His body became a vessel, and into that vessel poured the shadow panther's predatory grace, the lightning hawk's furious speed, the wind-snake's ancient cunning. Three spirits, three souls, three sets of memories, all flowing together into a single point of existence.

He opened his eyes.

The corrupted beast was frozen mid-charge, its massive form caught in the moment between heartbeats. Lin could see everything—the flow of corruption through its body, the weak points in its structure, the exact amount of force needed to shatter its core.

*This is what it means to be a beast tamer.*

He moved.

Not with speed, but with *simultaneity*. His body occupied multiple positions at once, shadow and lightning and serpent coiling together into a strike that defied physics. His hand, wreathed in darkness and electricity and venom, pierced the corrupted beast's chest.

Found its core.

*Crushed it.*

The beast's roar became a whimper, then silence. Its body collapsed, corruption dissipating into the air like smoke. Lin stood over it, breathing hard, his three beasts' power still thrumming through his veins.

The disciples stared at him in stunned silence.

"That..." the short-haired girl whispered. "That's not normal. No beast tamer can do that."

"He's a freak," one of the boys muttered, backing away. "A monster."

Lin turned to face them, and they flinched. He realized, distantly, that his eyes were still glowing—one gold, one silver, and one serpentine green.

"I'm not going to hurt you," he said, his voice layered with three distinct tones. "But I need to know. What were you doing out here? And why was that thing hunting you?"

The short-haired girl swallowed, visibly gathering her courage. "We were tracking it. For the past three days, it's been attacking villages on the edge of the valley. We thought we could capture it, bring it back to the sect for study." She laughed bitterly. "Stupid, I know."

"Not stupid. Underestimated." Lin's eyes slowly returned to normal as the merge faded, leaving him exhausted but alive. "The corruption isn't natural. Someone's controlling it."

"Someone?" The girl's face paled. "You mean..."

"A beast tamer. A powerful one." Lin looked down at the corpse of the corrupted bear, his expression grim. "And they're still out there, turning beasts into weapons."

A new voice cut through the clearing, cold and amused.

"Very perceptive, little rat."

Lin spun, his beasts surging to defensive positions. Standing at the edge of the mist was a figure wrapped in black robes, face hidden behind a porcelain mask painted with a single blood-red eye. The corruption rolling off them was faint but unmistakable—the same signature Lin had felt in the bear's core.

"I was wondering when someone would notice," the figure said, tilting their head. "But I didn't expect it to be a cripple with a broken snake and a handful of misfit beasts."

Lin's hands clenched into fists. "Who are you?"

"Someone who's been watching you, Lin Xiao." The figure laughed, a sound like breaking glass. "Ever since you bonded that panther, your resonance has been... interesting. The [Beast Resonance] talent—I thought it was a myth. But here you are, living proof."

"You know about my ability?"

"I know everything about you. Your failures. Your triumphs. Your desperate little climb toward relevance." The figure stepped closer, and Lin felt his beasts stir with unease. "And I've decided you're too dangerous to leave alive."

The shadow panther growled, muscles coiling to strike. The lightning hawk shrieked, electricity crackling. The wind-snake hissed, venom dripping.

But the figure only laughed again.

"Don't worry. I'm not going to kill you today." They turned, robes swirling, and began walking back into the mist. "But I wanted you to know—the corruption is just the beginning. Something much bigger is coming to Thousand Beast Valley. And when it arrives, all your little beasts won't be enough to save you."

Lin took a step forward, ready to pursue, but the panther's voice stopped him.

*Don't. It's a trap. They want you to follow.*

He hesitated, then stopped, fists trembling.

"Who are you?" he called out again, desperation creeping into his voice.

The figure paused at the edge of the mist, looking back over their shoulder.

"You can call me the Corruptor." The mask's blood-red eye seemed to glow. "And we'll meet again, Lin Xiao. When the time is right."

They vanished into the mist, leaving nothing but the echo of their laughter and the cold certainty that Lin's world had just become infinitely more dangerous.

The short-haired girl approached him cautiously. "What was that?"

Lin didn't answer. He was staring at his hands, watching the last traces of his merged power fade. The corruption seal he'd sensed in the bear's core was still burned into his memory—a pattern he now recognized.

It matched the mark on the wind-snake's broken core.

*They've been watching me from the beginning.*

"Lin Xiao?" The girl touched his arm, and he flinched. "Are you okay?"

He looked up, his expression hard.

"No," he said quietly. "But I'm going to be. Because whatever's coming, I'm going to be ready for it."

The shadow panther pressed against his leg, a silent promise of loyalty. The lightning hawk settled on his shoulder, talons gentle. The wind-snake coiled around his arm, its tongue flicking against his skin.

Three beasts. Three bonds. And a power that even the Corruptor seemed to fear.

*I don't know what's coming,* Lin thought, watching the mist swirl where the figure had disappeared. *But I know one thing—I'm not going down without a fight.*

And deep in the valley, unseen by any living eyes, something ancient stirred in its slumber, drawn by the resonance of a beast tamer who refused to stay broken.

title: Chapter 5
content:
# CHAPTER 5: The Mark of Corruption

Lin Xiao's claws—shadow-forged and razor-sharp—sank into the corrupted beast's back before it could complete its charge. The creature howled, black ichor spraying across the swamp as it twisted violently, trying to dislodge him.

*"Stubborn little—"* the panther's voice growled in his mind, but there was approval in it.

Lin held on. The lightning hawk's electricity surged through his arm, amplifying the strike. The wind-snake's venom dripped from his fingers, seeping into the wound. The combination was devastating—the corrupted beast stumbled, its roars turning to whimpers.

The pinned disciples stared with wide eyes. One of them, a girl with dirt-smudged cheeks and a broken spear in her hand, whispered something Lin couldn't hear.

He didn't have time to care.

The corrupted beast swung a massive arm, and Lin flickered—*shadow step*—reappearing twenty feet away. The panther's power was intoxicating. Darkness didn't just welcome him; it obeyed him.

"What is that thing?" he shouted at the disciples.

"Corruption spawn!" the girl yelled back. "Third stage! It's been hunting our party for two days—killed three of my brothers!"

Third stage. That put it on par with a beast master at the fourth level of the Beast Melding Realm. Lin was only at the third level—barely, having just broken through with the panther's bond.

But he wasn't just a third-level beast master.

He was a *Resonator*.

*[Beast Resonance: Dual Shift — Shadow Lightning]*

The panther's power and the hawk's power merged in his chest like a collapsing star. His body became a blur of black and blue, shadows and electricity intertwining. He moved faster than any single beast should have allowed.

The corruption spawn swung again. Lin ducked under it, slid between its legs, and drove both hands upward into its gut. Lightning exploded from within, and shadows followed, tearing the creature apart from the inside.

It collapsed with a ground-shaking thud.

Silence.

Then the girl stumbled forward, her eyes fixed on Lin like he was a miracle. "You're... you're a disciple? From which sect? I've never seen a technique like that. The way you moved, the way your beasts—"

"I'm not from a sect," Lin said, cutting her off. The triple merge was fading now, leaving him drained but buzzing with residual power. "I'm from the valley. A beast tamer."

The girl's face fell. "A valley tamer? But... but that's impossible. Valley tamers don't have techniques like that. They barely have *beasts*."

"Hey!"

She caught herself, flushing. "I'm sorry. I didn't mean—it's just—" She shook her head. "I'm Mei Lin, third disciple of the Crimson Cloud Sect. My master sent us to investigate the corruption spreading through the northern marshes. We didn't expect a spawn this powerful."

"Crimson Cloud Sect?" Lin's eyebrows rose. The Crimson Cloud Sect was one of the major powers in the region, controlling a territory ten times the size of Thousand Beast Valley. Their disciples were trained from childhood, bonded with high-ranking beasts, and given resources that valley tamers could only dream of.

And she was looking at *him* like *he* was impressive.

"How many survived?" Lin asked.

"Just me and Chen Wei," she said, pointing to a trembling boy huddled against the rocks. "The others... the corruption got them. But the spawn—it should have taken us too. If you hadn't come..." She trailed off, her voice cracking.

Lin felt a cold weight settle in his stomach. "The corruption is getting worse, isn't it? More frequent, more aggressive."

Mei Lin nodded slowly. "My master thinks something is causing it. A source. Deep in the valley's heart, maybe. The corrupted beasts are spreading like a plague, and they're getting smarter. They're not just attacking randomly—they're hunting."

A chill ran down Lin's spine. *Hunting.* That meant coordination. That meant intelligence.

That meant something was controlling them.

"Do you know where the source might be?" he asked.

"There are rumors," she said, lowering her voice. "An ancient temple, buried beneath the valley. They say that's where the corruption began. But no one who's gone looking has come back."

The shadow panther stirred in Lin's mind. *"She speaks of the Hollow Temple. I have felt its presence, even from my prison. Darkness calls to darkness, little tamer. That place is a wound in the world."*

*"You know about it?"* Lin thought back.

*"I know that whatever sleeps there is waking. And when it fully wakes, the valley will burn."*

Lin's blood ran cold. "We need to go there."

Mei Lin stared at him. "To the temple? Are you insane? I just told you—no one comes back!"

"I have to." Lin's voice was firm, even as his heart hammered against his ribs. "The corruption is spreading. It's killing beasts and tamers alike. If I don't stop it, there won't be a valley left to protect."

"You're just one person!"

"I'm not." Lin looked at his hands. The phantom sensations of his three beasts still lingered—the snake's patience, the hawk's speed, the panther's stealth. "I have them. And that's enough."

Mei Lin opened her mouth to argue, but a sharp cry from above cut her off.

The lightning hawk was circling, screeching in alarm. Through their bond, Lin felt what it had sensed: movement. Lots of it. Coming fast.

"What's wrong?" Chen Wei squeaked, finally finding his voice.

Lin's enhanced senses pushed outward. The swamp went quiet—too quiet. The insects stopped chirping. The frogs stopped croaking. Even the wind seemed to hold its breath.

Then he felt them.

A dozen corruption spawn, emerging from the mist like ghosts. They moved in perfect synchronization, surrounding the group on all sides. Their eyes burned with that same malevolent red light, and their bodies were twisted in ways that made Lin's stomach turn.

"Ambush," Lin breathed.

Mei Lin's face went pale. "They... they waited. They let us kill the first one so we'd be tired, so we'd think we were safe. Then they—" She couldn't finish.

The spawn closed in.

*"This is not natural,"* the shadow panther snarled. *"This is a hunt, orchestrated by a single will. Something is commanding them."*

*"Can we fight them?"* Lin asked.

Silence from the panther. Then: *"Not all of them. Not like this."*

Lin's mind raced. He could maybe take three, four at most, before the triple merge depleted his qi entirely. Mei Lin and Chen Wei were wounded, exhausted. They wouldn't last a minute.

But running? Through a swamp full of corruption spawn?

They didn't have a choice.

"Follow me," Lin said, his voice low. "Stay close. Don't stop for anything."

"But—"

"FOLLOW ME!"

He broke into a sprint, heading east—away from the temple, away from the village, toward the densest part of the swamp. The corruption spawn hissed and gave chase, their twisted limbs carrying them across the water with horrifying speed.

The lightning hawk dove, striking the lead spawn with a bolt of electricity. It stumbled, giving them precious seconds. The shadow panther faded into Lin's shadow, ready to strike if anything got too close.

Mei Lin grabbed Chen Wei's hand and ran, her broken spear raised like a talisman.

They crashed through the underbrush, branches cutting at their faces, mud sucking at their feet. Lin's lungs burned, but he didn't stop. He could feel the corruption spawn behind them, gaining, always gaining.

*"There,"* the wind-snake whispered. *"A cave. Hidden by the waterfall."*

Lin changed direction without hesitation. He heard the waterfall before he saw it—a thundering curtain of water that plunged into a black pool below. Behind it, barely visible, was the dark mouth of a cave.

"Dive!" Lin shouted.

He leaped, plunging through the waterfall. The cold hit him like a wall, but he pushed through, emerging on the other side into a cavern lit by faint bioluminescent moss.

Mei Lin landed beside him, gasping. Chen Wei splashed in a second later, sputtering.

The corruption spawn stopped at the water's edge. They paced back and forth, hissing, but didn't enter.

"They're afraid of something," Mei Lin whispered.

Lin turned around.

The cavern stretched deep into the earth, its walls covered in ancient carvings. Symbols he didn't recognize, depicting beasts of impossible size and power, locked in eternal combat. At the far end, illuminated by a shaft of pale light, stood an altar.

And on that altar, pulsing with black light, was a shard of something that made Lin's soul recoil.

*"No,"* the shadow panther hissed. *"No, no, no—"*

"What is it?" Lin asked, though he already knew.

The panther's voice was barely a whisper.

*"The heart of the corruption. The seed of the Hollow Temple's power. Little tamer... we have found what we were never meant to find."*

The shard pulsed. And deep in the cave, something awakened.

Mei Lin screamed.

Lin turned to see her clutching her arm. Black veins were spreading from a wound she hadn't mentioned, crawling up her neck, reaching for her eyes. Her breath came in ragged gasps.

"Mei Lin—"

"Don't touch me!" She stumbled backward, her face contorting in pain. "It's inside me. The corruption—it got me when the spawn attacked. I thought I could hide it, but—"

Her eyes flashed red.

And then she attacked.

---

**End of Chapter 5**

title: Chapter 6
content:
CHAPTER TITLE: The Price of Power

Lin's fist connected with the corrupted beast's spine.

The impact sent shockwaves through his arm, the merged power of three beasts amplifying his strength tenfold. Bone cracked beneath his knuckles, and the creature stumbled forward, black ichor spraying from the wound.

*"Left flank!"* the shadow panther's voice screamed in his mind.

Lin twisted, shadows wrapping around his body like a second skin. The corrupted beast's tail—barbed and thick as a tree trunk—whistled past his ear, close enough to draw blood. He felt the sting, felt the venom trying to seep into his veins, but the wind-snake's essence burned through him, neutralizing the poison before it could take hold.

"Keep it pinned!" one of the disciples shouted, launching a fireball that splashed harmlessly against the creature's hide.

Lin ignored them. His focus narrowed to a single point: the glowing red core pulsing in the beast's chest. Every corrupted beast had one. Destroy it, and the creature collapsed into ash.

Easier said than done.

The beast turned, its twisted face contorting into something that might have been a snarl. Black qi erupted from its body, forming tendrils that lashed out in all directions. One caught a disciple in the chest, sending him flying into the rocks with a sickening crunch.

*"Hawk, now!"*

The lightning hawk dove from above, talons crackling with electricity. It raked across the beast's face, blinding one of its red eyes. The creature howled, swatting blindly, but the hawk was already gone, weaving through the air like a living thunderbolt.

Lin moved.

He became shadow, became smoke, became the space between heartbeats. The panther's gift wrapped around him, hiding his approach until the last possible moment. He appeared beneath the beast's guard, both hands pressed against its chest, directly over the core.

*[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge - Full Release]*

The world went white.

Power exploded from Lin's palms—lightning, venom, and shadow all compressed into a single point of destruction. The corrupted beast's chest caved inward. Its core shattered like glass. For a single, frozen moment, Lin saw the creature's eyes clear, saw the human agony that had been trapped inside the corruption, and then it was gone, dissolving into black mist that scattered on the wind.

Lin fell to his knees, gasping.

The triple merge had taken everything from him. His meridians felt like shattered glass, raw and burning. The shadow panther materialized beside him, its breathing ragged. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, trembling. Even the wind-snake, usually so stoic, seemed drained.

"Holy ancestors," one of the disciples whispered. "You... you killed a rank 4 corrupted beast. Alone."

Lin looked up, forcing himself to stand. "I wasn't alone."

The disciple—a young woman with a scar across her cheek and robes bearing the insignia of the Crimson Crane Sect—bowed deeply. "I am Mei Lin. We owe you our lives. That creature had been stalking our hunting party for three days. We lost two members before you arrived."

*"The corruption spreads,"* the shadow panther said, its mental voice grave. *"I have not seen a beast this twisted in decades. Something is wrong in the valley."*

Lin nodded slowly. The corruption had been growing for months, but this was different. This creature hadn't been a wild beast that had fallen to corruption. It had been a bonded beast. He saw the remnants of a tamer's mark on what was left of its shoulder.

"Where did it come from?" he asked.

Mei Lin's face paled. "The Northern Reaches. Our sect sent scouts to investigate reports of a corrupted beast surge. They found something worse." She hesitated, glancing at her surviving companions. "They found a crater. A massive one, still smoking. And at its center..."

"At its center, what?"

"A corpse. A beast tamer, but not like any we've seen. His body was covered in seals, and his core—" She stopped, swallowing hard. "His core was still intact. Still pulsing with corruption. We think... we think someone tried to create a corrupted beast tamer. And failed."

The words hit Lin like a physical blow.

A corrupted beast tamer. It was the ultimate taboo in Thousand Beast Valley, the thing that every disciple was taught to fear. Beast tamers who fell to corruption didn't just die—they became something worse. Their bonded beasts turned against them, twisted into monsters that spread the plague wherever they went.

But someone had tried to *create* one.

"Take me there," Lin said.

Mei Lin stared at him like he'd grown a second head. "Are you insane? The area is crawling with corrupted beasts. We barely escaped with our lives!"

"Which means the source is still there." Lin's hand drifted to his chest, where the shadow panther's bond pulsed like a second heartbeat. "If someone is creating corrupted beast tamers, we need to know why. And we need to stop them before they succeed."

*"The little tamer has grown bold,"* the shadow panther observed. There was no mockery in its voice. Only respect. *"I will follow. But if we die, I am haunting you."*

"You'd haunt me anyway," Lin muttered.

The journey took four hours through increasingly treacherous terrain. The swamp gave way to rocky hills, which gave way to a forest that looked like it had been burned from the inside out. Trees stood blackened and twisted, their bark weeping black sap. The air grew thick with corruption, making every breath feel like swallowing ash.

Lin kept his beasts close, their merged senses extending in all directions. They avoided three more corrupted beast packs, each one larger and more aggressive than the last. By the time they reached the crater, the sun had begun to set, painting the sky in shades of blood and amber.

The crater was exactly as Mei Lin had described. A perfect circle, half a mile wide, carved into the earth as if by a god's fist. The ground at its center still glowed faintly, radiating heat and corruption in equal measure. And there, lying in the exact middle, was the corpse.

It was human. Or it had been, once. Now it was something else entirely—a husk of blackened skin and exposed bone, its chest split open to reveal a core that pulsed with malevolent red light. Seals covered every inch of its body, intricate patterns that looked like they'd been carved with a knife rather than drawn with ink.

"Stay here," Lin ordered Mei Lin and her party. "If I don't come out in an hour, run. Tell the sects what you saw."

"You'll die," Mei Lin said flatly.

"Probably." Lin stepped over the crater's edge. "But I've been dying my whole life. I'm getting good at it."

He descended into the crater, the shadow panther fading into darkness beside him. The heat grew more intense with every step, until his skin felt like it was peeling. The corruption pressed against his mind, whispering promises of power, of strength, of the ability to protect everyone he cared about.

*"Do not listen,"* the wind-snake warned. *"The corruption speaks with honeyed words. Its gift is poison."*

Lin grit his teeth and kept walking.

He reached the corpse ten minutes later. Up close, it was even worse. The seals weren't just on the skin—they were carved into the bone, burned into the marrow. Someone had spent a long time preparing this body, turning it into a vessel for something terrible.

And then he saw it.

A journal, clutched in the corpse's skeletal hand. The leather was charred, but the pages inside were still intact, protected by some kind of preservation seal. Lin pried it free, opening it with trembling fingers.

The handwriting was elegant, precise. A scholar's hand.

*"Experiment 47: Subject failed to contain the corruption seed. Core detonated at 73% saturation. Note: the issue appears to be spiritual resonance, not physical tolerance. Subjects with strong bonds to their beasts reject the corruption faster. Those with weak bonds or no bonds accept it too readily, leading to instability. The solution is clear: we need a tamer with multiple bonds, strong enough to resist the corruption, but open enough to accept it. We need a perfect vessel."*

Lin's blood ran cold.

He flipped through more pages, his eyes scanning faster and faster. Names. Dates. Locations. A list of beast tamers who had been captured and experimented on. Some of them he recognized—disciples who had gone missing over the past year, written off as casualties of the corruption.

And at the back of the journal, a final entry:

*"The prototype is complete. Subject 0 has accepted the corruption seed and achieved stable integration. The process requires a tamer with at least three bonded beasts, all of minimum rank 3. Once transformed, the subject's power exceeds anything the valley has ever seen. We will begin the mass conversion within the month. The Thousand Beast Valley will fall, and from its ashes, a new order will rise."*

Signed: *Elder Shen.*

Lin stared at the name. Elder Shen. The head of the Celestial Harmony Sect. One of the most respected beast tamers in the entire valley. A man who had been leading the fight against the corruption for months.

A man who had been causing it all along.

"We need to leave," he said, his voice barely a whisper. "We need to leave now."

But when he turned around, the crater's edge was no longer empty.

Figures stood silhouetted against the setting sun. Dozens of them. Their eyes glowed red, and black qi leaked from their bodies like smoke from dying embers.

Corrupted beast tamers. Fully formed. Waiting.

And at their center stood Elder Shen, his white robes immaculate, a gentle smile on his weathered face.

"Lin Xiao," he said, his voice carrying across the crater like a bell. "I've been expecting you. You see, I've been watching your progress with great interest. Three bonded beasts, all rank 3 or higher. A unique talent that allows you to merge their powers." He clasped his hands behind his back. "You are exactly what I've been looking for."

Lin's hands clenched into fists. The shadow panther growled, the lightning hawk shrieked, and the wind-snake coiled, ready to strike.

"I'd rather die," Lin said.

Elder Shen's smile never wavered. "That can be arranged. But first, I want you to meet someone." He stepped aside, revealing a figure that had been hidden behind him.

A girl, no older than sixteen. Her eyes were red, her skin pale, black veins crawling up her neck like roots. She wore the robes of the Celestial Harmony Sect, and in her chest, a corrupted core pulsed with steady, rhythmic light.

"Allow me to introduce Subject 0," Elder Shen said. "Or, as you might know her—your sister."

Lin's world shattered.

The girl looked up, and despite the corruption, despite the black veins and the red eyes, he recognized her.

"Xiao Mei?" he whispered.

She smiled, and it was the most horrific thing he had ever seen.

"Brother," she said, her voice layered with a thousand whispering echoes. "I've missed you."

title: Chapter 7
content:
CHAPTER 7: The Mark of Corruption

The corrupted beast's claw swept through empty air where Lin had stood a heartbeat before. Shadow energy rippled around him as he reformed twenty feet to the left, the panther's gift making him insubstantial at will.

*"Watch its tail,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"Corrupted beasts often develop secondary attacks."*

Lin's eyes flicked downward. The creature's tail was indeed twitching, barbed tip gleaming with the same black qi that oozed from its wounds. But he had more immediate concerns—like the three disciples huddled against the rock formation, their cultivation bases flickering like candles in a storm.

"Get behind me!" Lin shouted, his voice carrying an echo of all three beasts. "Now!"

The disciples scrambled, robes tearing on jagged stone. A girl with a jade hairpin—clearly the leader—dragged two injured companions behind her. "Who are you? I've never seen a beast tamer with three bonds before!"

"No time." Lin's hands crackled with lightning as he called on the hawk's power. The wind-snake's venom pooled in his palm, mixing with electricity to create something new—something dangerous. "When I attack, run for the eastern ridge. There's a safe path through the mist."

"But our beasts—"

"Are useless against this thing. Go!"

The corrupted beast lunged, mouth opening impossibly wide. Rows of jagged teeth, each one dripping with black corruption, descended toward Lin like a guillotine.

He didn't dodge.

Instead, Lin raised his hand and *pushed*. Shadow energy, lightning, and venom exploded outward in a concentrated blast that caught the creature directly in the throat. The beast recoiled, choking, black blood spraying from its maw.

*"Bold,"* the shadow panther noted. *"Foolish, but bold."*

*"Worked, didn't it?"*

The disciples were running now, the girl with the jade hairpin casting one last look over her shoulder. "Find us after! I owe you—"

"Just go!"

The corrupted beast shook off the attack, its red eyes fixing on Lin with undivided hatred. Black qi swirled around it, forming patterns that made Lin's skin crawl. Patterns that looked almost... deliberate.

*"That's not random corruption,"* the lightning hawk observed, circling high above. *"Someone is controlling it."*

Lin's blood ran cold. "What?"

*"Look at the marks on its flesh. Those are formation symbols—crude ones, but intentional. This beast was created, not born."*

Before Lin could process this, the creature roared and slammed its fists into the ground. The swamp exploded upward, geysers of mud and corrupted water raining down like artillery. Lin shadow-stepped, reformed, shadow-stepped again, but the attacks were getting faster.

*"It's learning,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"Adapting to your movement patterns."*

*"Then we change the patterns."*

Lin stopped running.

The corrupted beast paused, confused by the sudden stillness. Its head tilted, black qi rippling across its twisted features.

"Hey." Lin's voice was calm, almost conversational. "You're not the only one who can adapt."

He raised both hands. The shadow panther's power coiled around his left arm, the lightning hawk's around his right. The wind-snake's essence wrapped around his chest, binding them together.

*[Beast Resonance: Synchronized Strike]*

This was something new—something he hadn't tried before. Instead of merging all three beasts into himself, Lin channeled their separate powers through different parts of his body, creating three simultaneous attacks with independent trajectories.

The shadow panther's claw struck from below, tearing through the creature's ankle. The lightning hawk's bolt hit from above, searing its shoulder. The wind-snake's venom targeted its eyes, blinding it in a spray of caustic liquid.

The corrupted beast screamed, a sound that was almost human. It flailed wildly, black blood painting the surrounding trees.

Lin pressed the advantage. Three more synchronized strikes, each one targeting a different weakness. The creature was slowing, its movements becoming jerky and uncoordinated.

*"One more hit,"* the shadow panther urged. *"Its core is exposed—the corruption's source. Destroy that, and it falls."*

Lin saw it: a pulsing black orb embedded in the creature's chest, just below the ribcage. All the black qi flowed from that point, like a heart made of darkness.

He gathered his power for the final blow.

And then something grabbed him from behind.

Lin's body went rigid as cold hands—no, *claws*—dug into his shoulders. The familiar weight of the shadow panther's power *screamed* in warning, but it was too late. He was lifted off the ground, held aloft by a creature that moved like liquid shadow.

*"Interesting,"* a voice purred in his ear. Female. Ancient. *"A beast tamer with three bonds. And using Beast Resonance, no less. I'd heard rumors, but I didn't believe them."*

Lin struggled, but the grip was iron. He twisted his head and saw her—a woman, or something that wore a woman's shape. Pale skin, black robes that seemed to drink the light, and eyes that held the same red malevolence as the corrupted beast's.

The corrupted beast was kneeling now, head bowed. *Obeying.*

"You—" Lin's voice came out ragged. "You're the one controlling it."

"Controlling is such an ugly word." The woman smiled, and her teeth were too sharp, too many. "I prefer *guiding*. These beasts were going mad anyway, their cores shattered by the corruption spreading through the valley. I simply... gave them purpose."

She turned him around, studying him like a curious scientist examining a specimen. Her fingers traced the lines of his face, and Lin felt something cold enter his meridians, searching.
