"Three bonds," she murmured. "And not just any bonds—quality bonds. A wind-snake, broken but loyal. A lightning hawk, proud but curious. A shadow panther, ancient and cunning." Her smile widened. "You have excellent taste, little tamer."

"What do you want?"

"Want?" She laughed, a sound like breaking glass. "I want to understand you, Lin Xiao. I want to know how a nobody from a backwater valley bonded three powerful beasts when the so-called geniuses of the major sects struggle with one."

"How do you know my name?"

"I know many things." She released him, and Lin dropped to the ground, gasping. The corrupted beast remained kneeling. The woman—whatever she was—stood over him, her shadow stretching impossibly long. "I know you awakened Beast Resonance. I know you're the only one in a hundred years who can merge with spirit beasts. I know you're the key to something much larger than a simple valley feud."

Lin's heart pounded. "What are you talking about?"

The woman crouched, bringing her face close to his. Up close, he could see the corruption eating away at her skin, black veins crawling beneath the surface. She was infected too—or maybe she *was* the infection.

"There's a war coming, little tamer. Not between sects, not between beasts—between worlds. The Thousand Beast Valley sits on a fracture point, a place where the barrier between our realm and the Abyss grows thin." She tilted her head. "And you, with your ability to merge with beasts... you could be either humanity's greatest weapon, or its destroyer."

"I don't understand."

"You will." She stood, gesturing to the kneeling corrupted beast. "This was a gift—a demonstration of what I can offer. But I'm not the only one interested in you. The sects have noticed your power. The elders are debating whether to recruit you or eliminate you. And the Abyss... the Abyss is waking."

She began to fade, her form dissolving into shadow.

"Wait!" Lin scrambled to his feet. "Who are you? What's your name?"

She paused, a silhouette against the dying light. "In your language, I suppose you'd call me the Night Mother. But names are meaningless when the end comes." Her eyes glowed one last time. "Find me when you're ready, Lin Xiao. I'll be watching."

And then she was gone.

The corrupted beast collapsed, its body dissolving into black smoke. The swamp was silent, the only sound the distant screaming of birds and the drip of corrupted water from the trees.

Lin stood alone, shaking.

*"That was..."* the shadow panther began.

*"Dangerous,"* the lightning hawk finished.

*"Fascinating,"* the wind-snake added, and Lin could feel its curiosity overriding its fear.

He took a shaky breath. "We need to find those disciples. They might know more about what's happening."

*"Or they might be the ones who set you up,"* the shadow panther warned. *"The sects are not to be trusted."*

"Maybe." Lin started walking toward the eastern ridge, his beasts' senses extending around him like a web. "But I need answers, and that girl with the jade hairpin—she had a sect insignia I didn't recognize. That's either very good or very bad."

He found them half an hour later, huddled in a cave at the base of the ridge. The girl with the jade hairpin stood guard, a bronze-ranked spirit fox growling at her side. When she saw Lin, relief flooded her face.

"You're alive!"

"Barely." Lin entered the cave, his shadow panther materializing behind him. The fox bristled, but a calming hand from its master settled it. "We need to talk."

The girl nodded. "I'm Mei Ling, of the Azure Cloud Sect. Those two are my disciples, Xia and Bao." She gestured to the injured pair, who were tending to wounds that oozed black corruption. "We were hunting a corrupted viper when the big one found us. You saved our lives."

"I need information." Lin sat across from her, his three beasts circling in his consciousness. "What do you know about the corruption spreading through the valley? And what do you know about a woman called the Night Mother?"

Mei Ling's face went pale.

"How do you know that name?"

"She just tried to recruit me."

The silence that followed was deafening.

Mei Ling's hands trembled as she reached into her robes and pulled out a jade token, cracked down the middle. "The Azure Cloud Sect has been fighting the corruption for three years. We thought it was a natural disaster—a plague spreading through the beasts." She held up the token. "But last month, we discovered the truth."

"Which is?"

"The corruption is intentional. Someone—or something—is spreading it deliberately. And the Night Mother..." She swallowed hard. "She's not from this world. She's an Abyssal Lord, a creature from beyond the fracture point. And she's been gathering an army of corrupted beasts for a purpose no one understands."

Lin's mind raced. An Abyssal Lord. An army of corrupted beasts. A war between worlds.

And somehow, he was in the middle of it all.

"There's more," Mei Ling said, her voice dropping to a whisper. "The sects are gathering for a council in three days. They're going to seal the valley—cut off all exits. No one in or out until the corruption is contained."

"That's insane. There are thousands of people in this valley."

"The elders believe it's necessary. They think the corruption might spread beyond the valley if it's not stopped." She met his eyes. "They're going to

title: Chapter 8
content:
CHAPTER 8: The Price of Resonance

The corrupted beast's claw passed through Lin's afterimage, tearing a chunk from the rock face behind him. He was already ten feet to the left, shadow panther's instincts guiding his movements. The lightning hawk struck from above, talons raking across the creature's twisted spine. Black ichor sprayed, sizzling where it hit the ground.

"Keep pressure on its flanks!" Lin shouted to the pinned disciples. "Don't let it focus!"

A girl in azure robes—the one who'd cried for help—scrambled to her feet. Her beast, a frost-ridged wolf with a cracked fang, limped to her side. "Who are you? I've never seen a tamer with three bonds before."

"Questions later." Lin dodged another swipe, the wind-snake's coils tensing around his waist, ready to launch him anywhere. "That thing's corruption is spreading. We need to put it down before—"

The corrupted beast *screamed*.

It wasn't a roar. It was a sound that cut through flesh and bone, resonating with something deep in Lin's chest. His beasts recoiled. The shadow panther flickered, momentarily losing its solid form. The lightning hawk tumbled from the sky, barely catching itself before impact. Even the wind-snake went rigid, its broken core pulsing with pain.

"What was that?" the azure-robed girl gasped, clutching her head.

Lin's vision swam. The corrupted beast was changing. Its twisted flesh rippled, black qi condensing around its chest. A symbol burned into existence there—a jagged rune that made Lin's blood run cold.

He recognized it.

*[Beast Seal: Corruption Anchor]*

Someone had *made* this creature. Deliberately.

"Fall back!" Lin grabbed the girl's arm, pulling her toward the rock formation. "Everyone, behind the rocks! Now!"

The disciples didn't argue. They scrambled, dragging their wounded beasts, as the corrupted beast's body began to swell. The black qi intensified, forming a vortex around its core. The ground cracked. Trees withered. The very air turned thick and poisonous.

Lin's mind raced. The seal was designed to detonate—to spread corruption across the entire swamp. Whoever set this trap wanted to create a plague zone. Wanted to kill everything.

*"Little tamer,"* the shadow panther's voice came, strained, *"that seal will consume everything in a mile radius. We need to break it before it completes its cycle."*

"Break it how?"

*"From the inside."*

Lin stared at the corrupted beast. Its body was a bomb, but its core—the seal's anchor—was the trigger point. If he could reach it, disrupt it, maybe he could stop the explosion.

Or die trying.

"I need a distraction," Lin said. "Biggest you can manage."

The shadow panther's golden eyes met his. *"You're going to try the merge? Inside that thing?"*

"Got a better idea?"

The panther was silent for a moment. Then, *"I hate stupid tamers."*

It vanished into the darkness. A moment later, a massive shadow erupted behind the corrupted beast—the panther, fully materialized, three times its normal size. It slammed into the creature's back, claws digging deep. The corrupted beast howled, twisting to face this new threat.

The lightning hawk didn't wait for orders. It dove, electricity building, and struck the beast's face in a blinding flash. The wind-snake added its voice, a high-pitched screech that disoriented the creature further.

*Now.*

Lin ran.

He'd never moved so fast. The shadow panther's speed, the wind-snake's agility, the lightning hawk's reflexes—all of it merged into a single purpose. He was a blur, a ghost, a streak of motion that the corrupted beast couldn't track.

He reached its leg and climbed.

The creature's flesh was wrong—too hot, too soft, pulsing with corruption that tried to seep into his skin. Lin gritted his teeth and pushed through. His hands burned. His lungs ached. But he kept climbing, up the beast's side, toward the pulsing rune on its chest.

*[Beast Resonance: Deep Merge]*

The world narrowed to a single point. Lin felt himself sinking into his beasts, felt their cores aligning with his. The shadow panther's stealth became his breath. The lightning hawk's speed became his heartbeat. The wind-snake's resilience became his bones.

He reached the rune.

The seal was intricate—dozens of smaller symbols woven into a pattern of destruction. Lin didn't know much about sealcraft, but he knew one thing: every seal needed a power source.

He plunged his hand into the beast's chest.

The corruption screamed. It clawed at his arm, tried to burrow into his meridians, but Lin's merged form was too dense, too *alive* for it to take hold. His fingers closed around something solid—the corrupted core, pulsing with black qi.

*"Destroy it,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"Quickly."*

Lin squeezed.

The core cracked.

The corrupted beast *imploded*.

Black qi collapsed inward, sucking air, light, sound into a single point. Lin felt himself being pulled, felt his grip on the physical world slipping. He was falling into the void, into the corruption's dying grasp, and there was nothing to hold onto.

Then something grabbed him.

The azure-robed girl. Her frost wolf howled, ice forming a bridge from the rock formation to Lin's position. She'd wrapped a rope around his waist, her feet planted against the stone, her face set in grim determination.

"I said *questions later*," she grunted, pulling. "Not *die later*."

Lin grabbed the rope. Together, they dragged him free as the corrupted beast's body collapsed into a black puddle of nothing.

He landed hard, gasping, his beasts retreating to their respective forms. The shadow panther materialized beside him, panting. The lightning hawk perched on a nearby branch, wings trembling. The wind-snake coiled around his neck, its scales cracked and bleeding.

"Thanks," Lin managed.

"Don't thank me yet." The girl pointed. "Look."

Lin turned.

The black puddle was moving.

It wasn't dying. It was *reforming*. The corruption was alive, and it was pulling itself together, piece by piece. Worse, it was spreading—tendrils of black qi creeping across the ground, touching the corpses of smaller beasts, *infecting* them.

"What did you do?" the girl whispered.

"Broke its core." Lin's voice was hollow. "But the corruption... it's not anchored anymore. It's free."

The tendrils reached a dead swamp boar. The boar's eyes snapped open, glowing red. Its body twisted, bones breaking and reforming, until it rose as a new corrupted beast—smaller, but just as vicious.

Then another. And another.

"Run," Lin said.

They ran.

The disciples scattered, each following their own survival instincts. Lin grabbed the azure-robed girl and pulled her toward the swamp's edge. Behind them, the corruption spread like wildfire, infecting everything it touched. Trees turned to twisted mockeries. Water turned black. The very ground seemed to rot.

"Who are you?" the girl shouted as they ran. "Really?"

"Lin Xiao. Nobody. Wastrel."

"Nobody with *three beasts*? Nobody who *merged* with them?" She shook her head. "I'm Song Yiren. Outer disciple of the Azure Peak Sect. And I think you just triggered a catastrophe."

"I noticed."

They burst out of the swamp, onto a ridge overlooking the valley. Below, the corruption was spreading faster than Lin had anticipated. It wasn't just the swamp anymore. It was reaching into the forest, toward the sect territories, toward—

*Home.*

Lin stopped.

The orphanage. The village. The people who'd called him worthless, but had never let him starve. They were in the path of the corruption.

"I have to go back."

"Are you insane?" Song Yiren grabbed his arm. "That corruption will kill you. It'll *consume* you."

"I have beasts now." Lin met her eyes. "I can't just watch them die."

"Then you'll die with them."

"Maybe." He pulled free. "But at least I'll die trying."

He turned to leave. The shadow panther padded beside him. The lightning hawk took flight. The wind-snake tightened its coils, ready for anything.

"Wait."

Song Yiren stepped forward. Her frost wolf limped beside her, but its eyes were clear, determined.

"Azure Peak owes you a debt. If you're going to fight, you're going to need more than three beasts." She extended her hand. "Let me help."

Lin stared at her. Then, slowly, he smiled.

"Fine. But if we die, I'm haunting you."

"Deal."

They ran toward the corruption together, toward the valley, toward the people who needed them. Behind them, the black tide surged, hungry and endless.

And somewhere in the distance, Lin felt it—a presence. Something old. Something watching. Something that had set this trap, and was now smiling at the chaos it had unleashed.

*[New Threat Detected: Unknown Entity - Corruption Source]*

*[Quest Updated: Survive the Plague]*

*[Warning: Beast Resonance Overuse Detected. Bond stability at 67%.]*

Lin ignored the notifications. He had a valley to save.

Whether it wanted saving or not.

title: The Sect's Judgment
content:
The corrupted beast's massive fist crashed through the spot where Lin had stood, pulverizing rock and sending debris spraying through the mist. But Lin was already gone, a shadow sliding between raindrops, the merged power of his three beasts singing through his blood.

*"Left,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind.

Lin pivoted, lightning crackling along his forearm as he thrust his hand forward. A bolt of electricity lanced out, striking the creature's twisted shoulder. The corrupted flesh sizzled, black qi hissing as it evaporated, but the beast barely flinched. It swung again, faster this time.

Too fast.

The blow caught Lin across the chest, sending him tumbling through the muddy ground. He rolled, gasping, feeling ribs crack under the impact. The wind-snake's venom pulsed through his veins, numbing the worst of the pain, but the message was clear: this thing was beyond anything he'd faced.

"What is that thing?" one of the pinned disciples shouted, her voice trembling.

"A corrupted titan," Lin said, spitting blood. He'd read about them in the valley's archives. Beasts that had been exposed to concentrated corruption, their cores twisted into something that fed on life itself. They didn't tire. They didn't feel pain. They only killed.

The titan roared again, and Lin felt the ground shake. It was preparing another charge, its body swelling with black qi that made the air feel thick and poisonous.

*"We cannot defeat this alone,"* the shadow panther said, its mental voice carrying an edge of fear that Lin had never heard before. *"Not in our current state."*

Lin's mind raced. The disciples behind him—five of them, all wearing the silver-and-jade robes of the Jade Serpent Sect—were watching him with desperate hope. But he could see their cultivation levels. None of them were above the fourth level of the Beast Melding Realm. Against a corrupted titan, they might as well be children.

"Get out of here," Lin ordered. "I'll hold it off."

"You'll die," the female disciple said.

"Probably." Lin grinned, feeling the shadow panther's approval through their bond. "But I'll give you enough time to run."

He activated [Beast Resonance] again, pushing the merge to its limits. His vision split into three perspectives: the hawk's aerial view, the panther's darkness-enhanced sight, the snake's heat-sensing gaze. Combined, they painted a picture of the battlefield that no single beast could provide.

The titan lunged.

Lin dodged, but barely. The creature's clawed hand tore through his sleeve, drawing blood. He retaliated with a burst of lightning that struck the beast's face, blinding it temporarily. The shadow panther materialized behind the titan, raking its claws across the creature's hamstrings. Black ichor sprayed, but the wounds closed almost instantly.

*"It regenerates,"* the lightning hawk shrieked.

*"No,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"It absorbs. It's pulling life from the swamp itself."*

Lin looked down. The ground beneath the titan was pulsing with dark energy, roots and plants withering as the corruption spread outward. The creature wasn't just fighting them—it was poisoning the entire area, turning the swamp into fuel for its twisted core.

"We need to sever its connection to the ground," Lin muttered. "But how—"

The answer came in the form of an arrow, blazing with golden light, that slammed into the titan's chest and exploded. The beast staggered, roaring in pain and confusion. Lin spun around and saw them: a group of cultivators emerging from the mist, their robes pristine white with crimson trim. The emblem on their chests—a phoenix rising from flames—was unmistakable.

The Vermilion Phoenix Sect. The most powerful sect in Thousand Beast Valley.

At their head stood a young woman with hair like spun gold and eyes that burned with fierce determination. She held a bow made of what looked like crystalline bone, and even from this distance, Lin could feel the power radiating from her. This wasn't a Beast Melding cultivator. This was something far beyond.

"Fall back!" she commanded, her voice carrying authority that brooked no argument. "Leave the titan to us."

Lin didn't need to be told twice. He released the merge, his three beasts retreating into the shadow of his spirit, and scrambled toward the disciples. The female disciple grabbed his arm, pulling him behind the rock formation as the Vermilion Phoenix Sect formed a circle around the corrupted titan.

"Who are they?" Lin asked, gasping for breath.

"Phoenix Hunters," the disciple whispered, her eyes wide with awe. "They're the sect's elite. They hunt corruption at its source."

The golden-haired woman raised her bow, and the air around her began to shimmer with heat. Arrows of pure flame materialized around her, hovering in a deadly halo. She released them all at once, and they struck the titan like a rain of falling stars. The creature screamed, its regeneration unable to keep up with the relentless fire.

But as the titan fell, something strange happened. The black qi that had been leaking from its body began to coalesce, forming a dark cloud that hung in the air like a living thing. The golden-haired woman's eyes narrowed.

"Everyone, shield your cores!" she shouted. "It's releasing a corruption pulse!"

Lin felt it before he could react—a wave of malevolent energy that washed over him, seeking to burrow into his spirit. His three beasts recoiled, their bonds straining under the assault. But then something unexpected happened.

His [Beast Resonance] activated on its own.

The corruption energy hit his core and *shattered*, dispersing like mist against stone. Lin gasped as the power of the resonance flared, not just defending him but *absorbing* the corruption, converting it into pure qi that flooded his meridians. His cultivation, which had been stuck at the third level of the Beast Melding Realm, surged forward.

*Fourth level. Fifth. Sixth.*

He felt his bones crack and reform, his meridians expanding to accommodate the influx of power. His three beasts howled in ecstasy as the shared energy strengthened them, pushing them toward evolution. The wind-snake's broken core began to mend. The lightning hawk's feathers took on a metallic sheen. The shadow panther's form grew more solid, more real.

By the time the pulse passed, Lin was kneeling in the mud, his body trembling with newfound power. He had reached the seventh level of the Beast Melding Realm in a single instant.

The golden-haired woman stared at him, her bow lowering slightly. "Impossible," she breathed. "No one can absorb corruption. It destroys the core."

Lin looked at his hands. They were covered in black residue that slowly dissolved, revealing skin that seemed to glow with inner light. "I don't know what happened," he said honestly.

The woman walked toward him, her hunters parting to let her pass. Up close, she was even more striking—and more terrifying. Her cultivation level was like a bonfire compared to his candle. Lin couldn't even guess what realm she was in.

"What's your name?" she asked.

"Lin Xiao."

Her eyes flickered with recognition. "The boy with the crippled snake."

"Not so crippled anymore," Lin said, and he felt the wind-snake's approval through their bond.

"Clearly." The woman studied him for a long moment. "I am Yue Qing, leader of the Phoenix Hunters. We've been tracking this corruption for weeks. It's spreading faster than we anticipated, and it's not natural."

"What do you mean?"

Yue Qing's expression darkened. "Someone is creating these corrupted beasts. Intentionally. We've found traces of a cultivation technique that should not exist—one that twists beast cores into weapons of mass destruction."

Lin's blood ran cold. "Who?"

"We don't know yet. But we've captured one of their agents." She paused, her gaze piercing. "He was carrying a message. It mentioned a boy with three beasts. A boy who could merge with them."

The swamp fell silent. Lin felt the eyes of everyone—the disciples, the hunters, Yue Qing herself—boring into him.

"Coincidence?" Lin asked, though he already knew the answer.

"I don't believe in coincidences," Yue Qing said. "Someone is hunting you, Lin Xiao. And they're willing to corrupt an entire valley to get to you."

She reached into her robe and pulled out a small jade slip, tossing it to him. Lin caught it, and the moment his fingers touched its surface, a message burned into his mind:

*"The boy with the resonance must be brought to the Hollow Heart Sect. Alive. The master has plans for him."*

Lin's hands trembled as he read the name at the bottom.

The signature of his own father.

---

Yue Qing's voice was soft, almost gentle. "I'm sorry, Lin Xiao. But it seems your family is at the center of this."

Lin crushed the jade slip in his fist, shards cutting into his palm. His three beasts stirred within him, sensing his turmoil—his rage—his betrayal.

"Where is the Hollow Heart Sect?" he asked, his voice flat.

"Hidden. In the mountains to the east. None of our scouts have been able to find it."

Lin looked at his bloodied hand, then at the spot where the corrupted titan had fallen. He thought about the scream that had brought him here, the disciples he'd saved, the power that had surged through him when he absorbed the corruption.

He thought about his father's face, and the years of neglect, the cold dismissal, the whispered rumors that Lin was a disappointment.

"Take me to your sect," Lin said. "I need to train. I need to get stronger."

Yue Qing's lips curved into a hint of a smile. "And then?"

Lin's eyes hardened. "And then I'm going to find my father. And I'm going to ask him why he wants me dead."

The lightning hawk shrieked overhead, and the shadow panther materialized at Lin's side, its eyes gleaming with predatory hunger. The wind-snake coiled around his arm, its scales now shimmering with an unnatural light.

Yue Qing nodded slowly. "The Vermilion Phoenix Sect will offer you sanctuary. But I warn you—if you're being hunted by the Hollow Heart Sect, nowhere in Thousand Beast Valley is truly safe."

"Good," Lin said, feeling the power of his three beasts thrumming through his veins. "I don't want to be safe. I want to be ready."

As they turned to leave, Lin caught a glimpse of something in the mist—a figure watching from the shadows, its eyes burning with red corruption light. It smiled, revealing teeth that were too sharp, too many.

Then it was gone, leaving only the faint whisper of a voice in Lin's mind:

*"The master is pleased. Soon, little resonance. Soon."*

title: The Price of Power
content:
Lin Xiao's fist connected with the corrupted beast's flank, and the impact sent shockwaves through his entire body. Shadows rippled around his arm, lightning crackled along his spine, and the wind-snake's venom pulsed through his veins like liquid fire.

The beast stumbled, black ichor spraying from the wound.

But it didn't fall.

*"Something's wrong,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"This corruption is not natural. It's too concentrated."*

Lin didn't have time to process the warning. The creature whirled around, its massive claw swiping through the air where he'd been standing a moment before. He'd already moved, the lightning hawk's speed carrying him twenty feet to the left, but the wind from the attack still tore at his robes.

"Get back!" he shouted at the pinned disciples. "Form a defensive circle!"

They scrambled to obey, drawing their own spirit beasts. A stone tortoise, a fire fox, a wind wolf. Decent beasts. Strong enough for disciples of a major sect, but nothing compared to the monster they faced.

Or the one fighting it.

Lin's triple merge was burning through his qi at an alarming rate. He could feel it, like sand slipping through an hourglass. Three minutes. Maybe four. Then he'd collapse.

*"Don't drag this out,"* the lightning hawk screeched. *"Strike the core!"*

The core. Every corrupted beast had one—a crystallized mass of corrupted qi that served as its heart. Destroy it, and the creature fell.

But finding it was the problem.

Lin's eyes, enhanced by all three beasts, scanned the creature's twisted form. Its body was a nightmare of misplaced bones and pulsing veins, the corruption having warped it beyond recognition. The core could be anywhere.

Then he saw it.

A faint glow, pulsing in rhythm with the beast's movements, deep within its chest cavity. Protected by layers of corrupted flesh and bone.

*"I need an opening,"* Lin muttered.

The shadow panther understood. It melted away from his body, reforming behind the beast. Claws extended, it slashed at the creature's hamstring, severing tendons with surgical precision.

The beast howled, turning to face this new threat.

That was all Lin needed.

He exploded forward, lightning crackling around his body, shadows cloaking his movements. The wind-snake's power guided his strike, showing him the exact angle, the precise point where the bone was thinnest.

His hand, wreathed in all three elements, plunged into the beast's chest.

The corrupted flesh screamed as it parted. Black qi lashed out, trying to corrupt him, but the triple merge protected him, each beast's energy shielding the others. His fingers closed around the core.

He ripped it out.

The beast froze. Its red eyes flickered once, twice, then went dark. The massive body collapsed, already beginning to dissolve into black mist.

Lin stood over it, breathing hard, the core clutched in his bloody hand.

It was beautiful, in a terrible way. A crystal of pure darkness, pulsing with malevolent light. Corruption given form.

"Thank you," one of the disciples said, stepping forward. She was young, maybe sixteen, with the robes of the Azure Sky Sect. "We were on a routine hunt. We didn't expect—"

"Routine hunt?" Lin cut her off, not looking away from the core. "In the Shadowmire Swamp? At night?"

She hesitated. "We... we were tracking something."

"Something that created this." Lin held up the core. "This isn't natural corruption. This is engineered."

The disciples exchanged glances. The wind wolf growled low in its throat.

"You know something," Lin said. It wasn't a question.

"We shouldn't be talking about this," another disciple muttered. A boy, older than the girl, with a scar across his chin. "The elders said—"

"The elders aren't here." Lin's voice hardened. "I just saved your lives. The least you can do is tell me what's going on."

The girl took a deep breath. "There's been... experiments. In the valley. Someone has been capturing spirit beasts and forcing corruption into them. Creating weapons."

Lin's blood ran cold. "Who?"

"We don't know. But they're getting bolder. This is the third corrupted beast we've encountered this week. The first two were smaller, weaker. This one..." She looked at the dissolving corpse. "This one was meant to kill."

*"She's telling the truth,"* the shadow panther said. *"I can taste the fear on her. She's terrified."*

Lin pocketed the core. "Where was the last attack?"

"Three miles north. Near the old temple ruins."

"The temple where I bonded the panther," Lin murmured. That was too close to coincidence.

Something was happening in Thousand Beast Valley. Something bigger than a single beast tamer's rise to power.

The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, its feathers still crackling with residual electricity. *"We should investigate. If someone is creating corrupted beasts, they'll have a base. Supplies. Records."*

*"It's a trap,"* the wind-snake hissed. *"Whoever is doing this knows about the temple. They're drawing people there."*

Both arguments made sense. Lin looked at the disciples, still huddled against the rocks, their beasts trembling.

"Go back to your sect," he said. "Don't come to this swamp again. Not alone."

The girl nodded, relief flooding her features. "Thank you again. I'm Mei Lin, of the Azure Sky Sect. If you ever need assistance—"

"I'll remember."

They left, their beasts carrying them through the mist. Lin watched them go, the core burning against his chest.

*"You're going to the temple,"* the shadow panther said. It wasn't a question.

"Someone is using corruption to create weapons. I just absorbed a corrupted core into my storage ring. I have three bonded beasts, one of which is a crippled wind-snake that everyone said was useless." Lin's lips curled into a humorless smile. "I think I'm already involved."

He moved through the swamp, the triple merge still active, his senses extended to their limits. The lightning hawk scouted from above, the shadow panther stalked at ground level, and the wind-snake coiled around his arm, ready to strike.

The old temple ruins loomed ahead, dark against the moonlit sky.

But something was different.

There were lights.

Faint, flickering lights, coming from within the collapsed structure. Voices, too—muffled, but unmistakable.

*"Someone is here,"* the shadow panther confirmed.

Lin extinguished his own aura, pulling the shadows around him like a cloak. He crept forward, each step silent, each breath controlled.

The temple's main hall had been cleared. Rubble pushed to the sides, a crude altar erected in the center. And around it, figures in dark robes, their faces hidden by hoods.

A cult.

A corruption cult, right here in the heart of Thousand Beast Valley.

Lin's hand tightened into a fist. The lightning hawk's electricity sparked, barely contained.

*"Easy,"* the shadow panther warned. *"We don't know their numbers. Their strength."*

But then the central figure spoke, and all thoughts of caution vanished.

"The experiment is progressing. The triple merge subject has bonded with three beasts, just as predicted. His power grows faster than anticipated."

Lin froze.

They were talking about him.

"We must accelerate the timeline," the figure continued. "The shadow panther was meant to be the final piece. Now that he has it, the fusion can begin."

Fusion? What fusion?

"Summon the others. We need to capture the subject before he becomes too powerful."

The figures began to disperse, moving with purpose. Lin backed away, his mind racing.

They'd been watching him. Following him. The shadow panther hadn't been a random encounter—it had been a planned piece of his power growth. The corrupted beast attack hadn't been a coincidence—it had been a test.

Everything he'd done, every step of his journey, had been manipulated.

*"We need to leave,"* the wind-snake urged. *"Now."*

But Lin couldn't move. His eyes were fixed on the altar, on the symbol carved into its surface.

It was the same symbol that had been on the core. The same symbol that was now burning against his chest.

A beast's skull, surrounded by chains.

The mark of the Beast Fracture.

The organization that had been banned from Thousand Beast Valley a hundred years ago. The organization that had tried to create the ultimate weapon by fusing beasts and humans into abominations.

The organization that, according to the histories, had been destroyed.

But they were here. In the valley. And they knew exactly who he was.

"Lin Xiao."

The voice came from behind him, soft and amused.

He spun around.

A woman stood there, her robes immaculate, her smile serene. She looked young, barely older than him, but her eyes held centuries of knowledge.

And she was holding the lightning hawk by the throat.

"Let it go," Lin growled, shadows gathering around his fists.

"Or what?" She tilted her head, studying him like a curious insect. "You'll attack me? Try to save your bonded beast?" She laughed, a sound like breaking glass. "You don't even understand what you are, do you?"

"Enlighten me."

"You're not a beast tamer, Lin Xiao. You never were." Her grip tightened. The lightning hawk choked, electricity flickering uselessly against her fingers. "You're a vessel. A perfect, beautiful vessel, designed to hold the power of a thousand beasts."

The world tilted.

"You're the Beast Fracture's greatest creation. And now that you've reached full maturity, it's time to collect."

She crushed the lightning hawk's throat.

The bond shattered like glass in Lin's chest. He screamed, falling to his knees, as the hawk's death flooded through him—its pain, its fear, its final, desperate thought of flight.

The woman smiled.

"Don't worry. You'll get used to the pain."

Around him, the cultists emerged from the shadows, forming a circle, their hands glowing with corrupted qi.

Lin looked up, tears streaming down his face, the shadows around him darkening to pure black.

"Tell me your name," he whispered. "So I know who to kill."

The woman laughed again.

"I'm the one who's been watching you since birth. The one who guided your steps, arranged your bonds, shaped your power." She stepped closer, crouching down to meet his eyes. "I'm your mother."

And the shadows consumed him.

title: The Eighth Seal's Whisper
content:
Lin Xiao moved through the corrupted beast's onslaught like a ghost made of lightning. The triple merge had transformed him into something the disciples had never seen—a being that existed in three states simultaneously. Shadow, storm, and serpent.

The corrupted beast's massive club-arm swept through where he'd been standing. But Lin was already gone, having flowed around the attack like water around a stone. He appeared on the beast's shoulder, both hands crackling with lightning.

"[Thunder Fang Strike]"

The technique wasn't one he'd learned. It was the lightning hawk's instinct, translated through his human meridians. Electricity erupted from his palms, drilling into the corrupted flesh. The beast howled, black qi exploding from the wound like blood from an artery.

"Keep pressure on it!" one of the disciples shouted, raising a bow. An arrow wreathed in blue flame streaked past Lin's ear, striking the beast's eye.

Lin spared a glance at the archer. Young woman, maybe sixteen. Core Formation realm—impressive for her age. Her robes bore the emblem of the Jade Serpent Sect, one of the valley's three major powers.

The corrupted beast stumbled back, and Lin pressed his advantage. The shadow panther's instincts whispered tactics in his mind: *Strike the left knee. It's compensating for an old injury.*

He didn't question how the panther knew. He simply acted.

His body dissolved into shadow, reforming at the beast's knee. A single palm strike, reinforced by the wind-snake's crushing coils, and the joint buckled. The creature fell, and Lin was already moving to finish it—

*Stop.*

The voice wasn't his beasts. It came from somewhere deeper. Ancient. Impossibly vast.

Lin froze. The corrupted beast froze. Everything in a hundred-foot radius seemed to hold its breath.

And then he heard it. A whisper, crawling through his bones like ants through dead wood:

*The Eighth Seal weakens. The Nameless One stirs.*

Behind him, someone screamed.

Lin spun around. One of the disciples—a young man with a spear—had collapsed, clutching his head. Black veins crawled up his neck, pulsating with the same corrupted qi that infected the beast.

"No," the archer breathed. "No, that's not possible. He was fine. He was fine a moment ago—"

The disciple's eyes snapped open. They were solid black. No whites. No pupils. Just endless, hungry darkness.

"Run," Lin said.

"What?"

"RUN!"

The corrupted disciple moved faster than any human should. He was across the clearing before the archer could draw her next arrow, his spear thrusting directly at her heart.

Lin intercepted. Shadow merged with lightning, and he became a blur of motion. His hand caught the spear inches from her chest. The force of the impact sent shockwaves through his arm, numbing his fingers.

"That's not your friend anymore," Lin growled. "He's a vessel."

The corrupted disciple's mouth opened, and a voice that wasn't human emerged. It was deep, resonant, and ancient. "*The Eighth Seal will break. The Nameless One will return. And this valley will become his hunting ground.*"

Then the disciple's body exploded.

Not in blood and bone—but in shadow. A cloud of living darkness erupted from his flesh, consuming everything in its path. Lin grabbed the archer and dove, using the wind-snake's speed to carry them clear. The other disciples weren't so lucky. Their screams lasted only a second before the darkness swallowed them whole.

Lin landed hard, rolling to protect the archer. When he looked up, the shadow cloud had coalesced into a single form. A man, seven feet tall, wrapped in chains made of pure darkness. His eyes were twin points of crimson light, and when he smiled, it revealed teeth filed to points.

"Ah," the being said, stretching like he'd just woken from a long nap. "Freedom. Even temporary freedom tastes sweet."

"Who are you?" Lin demanded, keeping the archer behind him.

The being laughed. It was a horrible sound—like grinding bones and breaking glass. "Names have power, little tamer. But if you must call me something, call me the Warden's Shadow. I was imprisoned alongside the Nameless One, bound by the same seals. But your corruption here—so delicious, so abundant—it gave me enough strength to slip a single thread of my being through the cracks."

Three beasts. Lin had three bonded beasts, his cultivation was rising, and he'd just discovered a technique that let him fight way above his weight class. He could take this thing.

*No.*

The shadow panther's voice was urgent, almost panicked. *That being is Rank 7 at minimum. Maybe higher. We cannot fight it. We must flee.*

Rank 7. The highest ranked beast in Thousand Beast Valley was the Azure Dragon, a Rank 6 guardian beast that hadn't moved in a century. This thing was beyond anything Lin had ever faced.

"I see you have beasts," the Warden's Shadow said, tilting his head. "Three of them. And a resonance ability I haven't seen in... oh, a thousand years. How interesting." His smile widened. "The Nameless One would love to meet you."

He raised a hand, and the shadow beneath Lin's feet came alive.

Lin reacted instantly—shadow merge, becoming one with the darkness himself. He grabbed the archer and propelled them both backward, the Warden's Shadow's attack grasping at empty air.

"Running?" The being laughed again. "How delightful. I do love the hunt."

"Who are you?" the archer whispered, her voice shaking. "What is that thing?"

"Bad news," Lin said. "Really, really bad news."

He needed a plan. The Warden's Shadow was stronger than anything he'd faced, but it wasn't fully manifested. It had only slipped a thread through the seal. That meant it was limited—both in power and in duration. If Lin could outlast it, or force it to expend enough energy...

*That's a terrible plan,* the lightning hawk chimed in. *But it's the only plan we have.*

"Hey," Lin said to the archer. "What's your name?"

"L-Liu Mei. Jade Serpent Sect, inner disciple."

"Liu Mei. I need you to run. Find the nearest sect elder, anyone who can help. Tell them what you saw here. Tell them the seals are breaking."

She stared at him. "What about you?"

"I'll buy you time."

"That's suicide."

"Probably." Lin grinned, though he felt anything but confident. "But I've got three very angry beasts who aren't ready to die yet."

Liu Mei hesitated, then nodded. She turned and ran, her blue flame qi flaring as she pushed her speed to the limit.

The Warden's Shadow watched her go with idle amusement. "Let her run. More prey for later." His crimson eyes fixed on Lin. "But you, little tamer—you're special. I think I'll keep you."

"Flattering," Lin said. "But I'm not the keeping type."

He activated [Beast Resonance: Triple Merge] again, pushing the bond harder than ever before. The wind-snake hissed in protest—its broken core was straining—but it didn't pull back. The lightning hawk screamed, pouring every ounce of its power into the connection. The shadow panther simply growled, promising violence.

Lin's body flickered. Shadows wrapped around him like armor. Lightning arced along his limbs, forming crackling blades. His eyes became serpentine slits, and for just a moment, he felt like he could see everything—every shadow, every current of air, every thread of fate.

The Warden's Shadow's smile faltered. For just an instant, something like recognition flickered in those crimson eyes.

"That resonance..." he whispered. "Where did you learn it?"

"Found it in a temple," Lin said. "Why?"

The being's expression shifted. The amusement was gone, replaced by something cold and calculating. "The Beast Resonance technique was lost two thousand years ago. It was sealed away by the first beast tamers, hidden so that no one could ever use it again. Do you know why?"

Lin didn't answer. He was too busy gathering power for his next attack.

"Because it wasn't meant for humans," the Warden's Shadow said. "It was created by the Nameless One. A gift to his generals, so they could merge with their beasts and conquer the world." He laughed, but there was no humor in it. "You've been using a technique created by the very entity we're trying to keep imprisoned. Every time you use it, you weaken his chains."

Lin's blood turned cold.

"No," he said. "That's not possible. It's just a resonance technique—"

"It's a key," the Warden's Shadow interrupted. "And you've been turning it in the lock with every bond you form." He stepped closer, and Lin felt the pressure of his presence like a mountain on his chest. "By my count, you've used it four times today. Do you know what that means?"

Lin's mind raced. The temple. The ancient murals. The way the technique had felt so natural, so instinctive, as if it had been waiting for him.

He'd thought it was a gift.

What if it was a trap?

"You've shortened the seal's lifespan by a decade," the Warden's Shadow said. "And the Nameless One thanks you for your service."

The ground beneath Lin's feet cracked. Black light erupted from the fissures, and he felt something massive stirring far beneath the earth. Something ancient. Something hungry.

*The Eighth Seal,* the wind-snake whispered, its voice trembling. *He's not lying. The seal is breaking.*

No. No, this couldn't be happening. Everything Lin had done—bonding with his beasts, mastering the technique, growing stronger—was it all just playing into the enemy's hands?

"You're lying," Lin said, but his voice lacked conviction.

"Am I?" The Warden's Shadow spread his arms. "Then why does the ground tremble? Why do the beasts of the valley howl in fear? Why can I feel the Nameless One's breath on my neck, even now?"

He was right. Lin could feel it too. A presence, vast and malevolent, pressing against the edges of reality. It was like standing at the bottom of an ocean, feeling the weight of all that water above you, knowing that at any moment it could come crashing down.

"Congratulations, little tamer," the Warden's Shadow said, his voice dropping to a whisper. "You've just become the most important person in Thousand Beast Valley. The Nameless One's freedom depends on you."

He laughed one last time, and then his form began to dissolve. "I'll be watching. Use your resonance well. Break more seals. Free my master."

And then he was gone, leaving Lin alone in the clearing, surrounded by the bodies of dead disciples and the lingering echoes of ancient power.

Lin fell to his knees. His beasts retreated from the merge, exhausted. The wind-snake's core was flickering dangerously, and the lightning hawk could barely keep its wings extended.

"What have I done?" Lin whispered.

A scream echoed in the distance. Then another. The beasts of the valley were howling, their cries a chorus of fear and fury. The corruption was spreading faster now, and Lin knew—deep in his bones—that it was his fault.

He'd thought he was getting stronger.

He'd been digging his own grave.

*We need to fix this,* the shadow panther said, its voice grim. *Find the source of the seals. Seal them again. Or—*

"Or the Nameless One wakes," Lin finished.

He looked at his

title: The Puppeteer's Strings
content:
The corrupted beast's roar shook the swamp as Lin Xiao's merged form struck like liquid shadow. His claws—part panther, part snake, part hawk—tore through the creature's twisted hide, releasing a spray of black ichor that sizzled where it touched the ground.

But something was wrong.

The beast didn't flinch. Didn't react to pain that would have sent any normal creature howling. Instead, it turned with mechanical precision, its burning red eyes fixed on Lin with an intelligence that didn't belong to a mindless corrupted.

*"Again,"* the shadow panther whispered in his mind. *"It's reading your movements."*

Lin dodged as a massive claw swept through his afterimage. The lightning hawk's shriek echoed from above—she'd spotted something. A pattern. The beast's attacks weren't random. They were choreographed.

"You're not corrupted," Lin breathed, skidding to a halt twenty feet away. "You're controlled."

The beast's head tilted. A gesture too human, too deliberate. Then its mouth opened, and words emerged—not from its throat, but from somewhere deeper. A voice that echoed with multiple tones, like several people speaking in perfect unison.

"Clever little tamer. The cripple who tamed the broken snake. We've heard of you."

The hunting party disciples gasped. One of them, a girl with dirt-streaked cheeks and a torn blue robe, pointed a shaking finger at Lin. "You're Lin Xiao? The one from the outer valley?"

"Not the time," Lin snapped. His merged senses were screaming at him. The voice wasn't coming from the beast—it was *projecting* through it. Someone, somewhere, was puppeteering this creature like a marionette.

"Who are you?" Lin demanded. "Show yourself."

The beast laughed. A horrible, grinding sound. "Oh, but we are showing ourselves. We are everywhere, Lin Xiao. In every corrupted beast that stalks these lands. In every tamer who's lost their bond. We are the rot beneath the valley's skin."

Black qi swirled around the beast's body, forming symbols in the air—characters Lin didn't recognize. Ancient script. Forbidden script.

The shadow panther growled, its presence pulsing with alarm. *"That language. I've seen it before. In the temple. Buried beneath the oldest stones."*

"What does it say?"

*"Bond-Breaker. Soul-Stealer. The First Betrayer's Mark."*

Lin's blood ran cold. The First Betrayer. Every beast tamer knew the legend—the first human to tame a beast, only to betray it for power. The story was told to children as a warning. A myth. A fairy tale.

But the symbols burning in the air were very, very real.

"You're not supposed to exist," Lin said, his voice low.

"And yet, here we are." The beast took a step forward, and the ground beneath it withered. Grass turned to ash. Water evaporated into steam. The very life force of the swamp was being drained. "Your little trio is impressive, Lin Xiao. Beast Resonance is a rare gift. But rare gifts attract rare attention."

The lightning hawk dove, striking the beast with a bolt of concentrated lightning. The electricity should have paralyzed any normal creature. Instead, it passed through the beast like smoke, leaving it unharmed.

*"It's not fully here,"* the hawk's voice chimed in. *"Its body is a vessel. The real threat is elsewhere."*

Lin's mind raced. If the beast was a puppet, attacking it was useless. He needed to find the puppeteer. But how? The voice was everywhere and nowhere, echoing off the mist and trees like a ghost.

"You're stalling," Lin said suddenly. "You're not here to kill me. You're here to distract me."

Silence. The beast's red eyes flickered—the first sign of uncertainty Lin had seen.

"What are you distracting me from?"

The beast lunged, faster than before. Lin barely had time to merge with the panther's shadow form, phasing through the attack. But the beast anticipated his movement, its claw sweeping in an arc that caught him across the chest.

Pain. White-hot and searing.

Lin crashed into a tree, the merged form shattering. He gasped, clutching his ribs. Blood seeped through his fingers. The wind-snake hissed in alarm, coiling protectively around him. The lightning hawk screeched, diving to intercept another attack.

The shadow panther materialized between Lin and the beast, its form rippling with barely contained fury. *"Enough."*

"Stay back," Lin coughed. "It's too strong."

*"I am Shadowmire Stalker. I do not retreat."*

But before the panther could strike, the beast stopped. Its head turned, as if listening to something only it could hear. Then it smiled—a grotesque parody of human emotion.

"Ah. It's done."

Lin's heart stopped. "Done? Done what?"

The beast didn't answer. Instead, its body began to dissolve, black qi leaking from every pore. The corruption that held it together was unraveling, the puppet being discarded.

"Your little friend," the voice said, growing fainter. "The girl with the silver hair and the broken bond. We've been looking for her."

Yue Ling.

"No." Lin forced himself to his feet, ignoring the agony in his chest. "You stay away from her."

"Too late, little tamer. The strings have already been attached."

The beast collapsed, its body crumbling to ash. The symbols faded from the air, leaving nothing but silence and the smell of rot.

Lin didn't wait. He turned and ran, his beasts following without question. The hunting party called after him, but their voices faded into the swamp's mist. Nothing mattered except reaching the outer valley. Reaching Yue Ling.

She'd been waiting for him at the edge of the swamp. He'd promised to return after the temple. Promised to show her his new power.

If that thing had touched her—

He pushed himself harder, faster, ignoring the blood that soaked his robes. The shadow panther wrapped itself around him, lending him speed. The lightning hawk guided his path from above. The wind-snake whispered encouragement, its presence a steady warmth in his chest.

The swamp gave way to familiar terrain. The outer valley. His home.

And then he saw it.

Smoke.

Black, acrid smoke rising from the direction of his village.

"No," he whispered. "No, no, no—"

He burst through the tree line and stopped dead.

The village was gone.

Not destroyed. Not burned. *Gone.* Where buildings had stood, there was only scorched earth. Where people had lived, there were only shadows imprinted on the ground—the last remnants of bodies turned to ash.

And in the center of it all, standing amidst the devastation, was a figure.

A woman. Tall and elegant, with hair as white as bone and eyes that glowed with the same malevolent red light as the corrupted beast. She wore robes of black silk, embroidered with symbols that writhed and twisted like living things.

Behind her, suspended in a cage of black qi, was Yue Ling.

The girl's silver hair was matted with blood. Her eyes were closed. Her bond-beast, a crippled fox with a missing leg, floated beside her, equally trapped.

"You," Lin growled, his voice raw with fury. "You're the one behind this."

The woman smiled. It was a beautiful smile, cold and sharp as a blade.

"I am the First Betrayer's heir," she said. "And you, Lin Xiao, have something I want."

She raised her hand, and the cage around Yue Ling tightened. The girl whimpered, her face contorting in pain.

"Give me your beasts," the woman said. "All three of them. Or I'll tear her bond apart piece by piece."

Lin's fists clenched. The wind-snake hissed. The lightning hawk shrieked. The shadow panther growled.

Three beasts. Three voices in his head. Three souls bound to his.

"Don't," a weak voice said.

Yue Ling's eyes fluttered open. She looked at Lin, and despite everything—the pain, the fear, the hopelessness—she smiled.

"She can't take your beasts. Not if you don't let her."

"Silence." The woman waved her hand, and Yue Ling gasped, the cage constricting further.

"Stop!" Lin shouted. "I'll do it. I'll give you whatever you want."

The woman's smile widened. "I knew you'd see reason."

But as Lin raised his hand, ready to sever the bonds that had become his everything, Yue Ling's voice echoed in his mind—not through their bond, but through something deeper. Something that had been growing between them since the day they'd met in the ruins.

*"Lin. Use Beast Resonance. On me."*

He froze. *What?*

*"Trust me. Merge with me. I'll show you."*

The woman was approaching, her hand extended, ready to claim his beasts. The corrupted qi around her pulsed like a living heartbeat.

Lin made his choice.

He extended his hand—not to his beasts, but to Yue Ling.

*[Beast Resonance: Human Merge]*

The world shattered.

Everything went white.

And when Lin opened his eyes, he was no longer himself.

He was *them.*

title: Chapter 13
content:
CHAPTER 13: The Weight of a Thousand Souls

The corrupted beast fell.

Its body crashed into the swamp with a thunderous splash, black qi dissolving into the mist like smoke from a dying fire. Lin stood over it, breathing hard, his triple-merged form flickering between shadow, lightning, and serpent scales.

The disciples stared at him in disbelief.

"Who... who are you?" one of them stammered.

Lin let the merge dissolve, his body returning to its normal state. The exhaustion hit him immediately—merging three beasts at once was like trying to hold a hurricane in a jar. His knees buckled slightly.

"Lin Xiao," he said, steadying himself against a tree. "From the Outer Valley."

The disciples exchanged glances. The name clearly meant nothing to them. They wore the silver-and-blue robes of the Celestial Peak Sect—one of the five major sects that dominated Thousand Beast Valley's inner regions.

"You're an outer disciple?" the lead disciple asked, a young woman with sharp features and a dragon-scale whip coiled at her hip. "You just killed a corrupted rank-three beast. Alone."

"I had help," Lin said, gesturing to the shadows where the panther lurked.

The woman's eyes narrowed. "A Shadowmire Stalker. And a Lightning Hawk. And..." She paused, her gaze dropping to the wind-snake coiled around Lin's arm. "Is that a Broken-Core Wind Viper?"

"She has a name," Lin said flatly.

The woman's expression shifted from surprise to something Lin didn't like—calculation. "Interesting. A cripple beast tamer with three bonded beasts, one of which is literally worthless. And yet you defeated a corrupted creature that's been hunting our hunting parties for weeks."

"It's not worthless," Lin said, his voice hardening. "And I didn't do it for thanks. I did it because it needed to be done."

"Of course." The woman smiled, but it didn't reach her eyes. "I'm Song Yueling, senior disciple of the Celestial Peak Sect. You've done us a great service, Lin Xiao. The sect will want to thank you properly."

Something about her tone made Lin's skin crawl. The shadow panther growled low in his mind—a warning.

*"She's lying,"* the panther said. *"Her heart beats too fast. Her scent changes when she speaks of thanks."*

*I know,* Lin thought back. *But we can't fight an entire sect. Not yet.*

He forced a smile. "I'm honored. But I should be going—"

"Nonsense." Song Yueling stepped forward, her hand landing on his shoulder with surprising strength. "The inner valley is dangerous at night. Corrupted beasts are everywhere. Allow us to escort you to our camp. We have healers, food, and a warm fire."

It wasn't a request.

Lin felt the other disciples shift positions, subtly blocking his exits. The lightning hawk shrieked overhead, agitated. The wind-snake tensed.

"Fine," Lin said, keeping his voice even. "Lead the way."

---

The Celestial Peak camp was a small fortress.

Wooden palisades lined with formation flags created a barrier against beast attacks. Tents were arranged in perfect rows, each one marked with sect insignias. Disciples moved with military precision, their beasts walking beside them—armored bears, crystal wolves, a single three-tailed fox that watched Lin with unsettling intelligence.

They led him to a central tent, larger than the others. Inside, a man sat behind a table covered in maps and scrolls. He was older, maybe thirty, with graying temples and eyes that had seen too much. A massive scar ran from his forehead to his jaw, and his left arm ended in a stump wrapped in bandages.

"Elder Zhao," Song Yueling said, bowing. "We found this outer disciple fighting a corrupted rank-three. Alone. He won."

Elder Zhao looked up, his one remaining eye studying Lin with uncomfortable intensity. "Sit."

Lin sat.

"You killed a corrupted beast," Elder Zhao said. "That's no small feat. The corruption has been spreading for months. We've lost seventeen disciples to it. Three elders."

"I got lucky," Lin said.

"Luck doesn't bond three beasts to a single soul." Elder Zhao leaned forward. "What's your secret?"

Lin's heart hammered. [Beast Resonance] was his greatest weapon—and his greatest vulnerability. If the major sects learned of his ability, they wouldn't just want to study him. They'd want to own him.

"I don't know what you mean," he said carefully. "I just... have a way with beasts."

Elder Zhao's scarred face twisted into something between a smile and a grimace. "A way with beasts. Is that what we're calling it now?"

Before Lin could respond, a scream tore through the camp.

Everyone in the tent was on their feet in an instant. Elder Zhao grabbed a sword from beside his table, his stump already bleeding from the sudden movement. "Report!"

A disciple burst through the tent flap, pale as death. "Elder! The perimeter—something's broken through. It's—it's not a beast. It's a *person*."

Lin followed the others outside.

The camp was chaos. Tents were burning. Disciples were running, their beasts either dead or fleeing. In the center of it all stood a figure wreathed in black qi—so dense it looked like solid smoke.

The figure was human. Barely.

Its skin was cracked, glowing with malevolent red light from within. Its eyes were pits of darkness, and its mouth hung open in a silent scream. Black tendrils extended from its body, lashing out at anything that moved.

A corrupted beast tamer.

Lin had heard rumors—beast tamers who pushed their bonds too far, who let their beasts consume them from within. But he'd never seen one.

"Everyone fall back!" Elder Zhao roared. "Formation three! Seal it in!"

The disciples moved with practiced precision, forming a circle around the corrupted tamer. Beasts howled and roared, attacking from all sides. But the tamer's tendrils moved faster, impaling a crystal wolf, crushing an armored bear's skull, sending a three-tailed fox fleeing with its tails between its legs.

"It's too strong!" someone screamed.

Elder Zhao stepped forward, his sword blazing with golden light. "I'll hold it. Get the disciples out—"

"No." The word left Lin's mouth before he could stop it.

Elder Zhao turned, furious. "Outer disciple, this is not your place—"

"I can stop it." Lin was already moving, his beasts responding to his call. The shadow panther materialized beside him. The lightning hawk dove from the sky. The wind-snake uncoiled, hissing.

*"You can't be serious,"* the panther said. *"That thing is beyond us."*

*Maybe,* Lin thought. *But I have to try.*

He raised his hand. The corrupted tamer's head snapped toward him, its pitch-black eyes locking onto his. The tendrils retracted, coiling like snakes preparing to strike.

And then—impossibly—it *spoke*.

"Lin... Xiao..."

The voice was distorted, layered with a dozen other voices screaming beneath it. But the name was clear.

Lin's blood ran cold.

"You know me?"

The corrupted tamer took a shambling step forward. Its cracked face twisted into something like recognition. "We... know you. The one who... resonates. The one who... *binds*."

"What are you talking about?"

The tamer's tendrils lashed out, not to attack—but to point. Directly at Lin's chest. "The plague... wants you. The corruption... *hungers* for you. You are... the key. The key to... *everything*."

Before Lin could respond, the corrupted tamer's body began to convulse. The black qi swirled, condensing, compressing. The disciples screamed and fled. Elder Zhao grabbed Lin's shoulder, trying to pull him back.

But Lin couldn't move.

The corrupted tamer's body exploded.

Not outward—*inward*. The black qi collapsed into a single point, a sphere of pure darkness no bigger than a marble. It hovered in the air for a moment, pulsing with malevolent light.

And then it shot toward Lin.

It struck him in the chest before he could react.

The world went white.

---

Lin woke on his back, staring at a canopy of stars.

His body ached. His head throbbed. And something was *different*—a presence in his mind that hadn't been there before. Not his beasts. Something else.

*"Hello, little tamer."*

The voice was ancient. Cold. Hungry.

Lin sat up, gasping. He was alone in a clearing, the camp's fires visible in the distance. The corrupted tamer's body was gone, reduced to ash scattered by the wind.

But the voice remained.

*"I am the Seed of Corruption. And I am now a part of you."*

Lin looked down at his chest. A small black mark had appeared over his heart—a spiral of darkness, pulsing with faint red light.

*"You cannot remove me. You cannot suppress me. But you can use me."*

*"Use you?"* Lin thought, his mind racing.

*"The corruption that plagued Thousand Beast Valley... I am its source. Its heart. Its will. The beasts that fell to it, the tamers who lost themselves—they were all searching for something. For me."*

*"And now you're inside me."*

*"Yes. And no. I am a fragment. A seed. But I am also a key. The corruption is spreading because something beneath this valley is waking up. Something ancient. Something that should never have been disturbed."*

Lin's hands trembled. The shadow panther was growling in his mind, the lightning hawk shrieking in alarm, the wind-snake hissing in fear.

*"What does it want?"* Lin asked.

The Seed of Corruption laughed—a sound like breaking glass.

*"It wants to be free. And you, Lin Xiao—you who can resonate with beasts, who can merge with them, who can bind them to your soul—you are the only one who can release it."*

*"Or?"*

*"Or you can try to stop it. But know this: if you fail, the corruption will consume everything. Every beast. Every tamer. Every blade of grass in Thousand Beast Valley."*

Lin stared at the mark on his chest, his mind a hurricane of fear and determination.

*"And if I succeed?"*

The Seed was silent for a long moment.

*"Then you will become something the world has never seen. A beast tamer who can command not just beasts—but the very essence of corruption itself."*

From the camp, another scream echoed through the night.

And Lin knew the war for Thousand Beast Valley had only just begun.

title: Chapter 14
content:
CHAPTER 14: The Elder's Betrayal

The corrupted beast's charge shattered the ground where Lin had stood moments before. But he was already gone, a ghost woven from shadow and lightning, his body moving with the fluid grace of three beasts merged into one.

*"Left side, exposed rib,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind.

Lin twisted mid-air, his right hand extending into claws that dripped with venom. He raked them across the beast's flank, and where his fingers touched, the black qi sizzled and dissolved. The creature howled, spinning to face him, but Lin had already vanished into the panther's shadow cloak.

"Keep it distracted!" one of the pinned disciples shouted, raising a talisman. "I need three breaths to activate the binding seal!"

Lin didn't respond—he was already moving, his consciousness split three ways. Through the lightning hawk's eyes, he saw the beast's blind spot above its left shoulder. Through the wind-snake's senses, he felt the exact moment the creature's muscles tensed for its next attack. Through the shadow panther's instincts, he knew precisely where to strike.

He appeared on the beast's back, both hands plunging into the corrupted flesh at the base of its skull. The black qi surged up his arms, trying to infect him, but his bonded beasts' energy burned it away. The creature convulsed, its legs buckling.

"Now!" Lin yelled.

The disciple slammed the talisman against the ground. Golden light erupted in a cage of sealing runes, wrapping around the corrupted beast and pinning it to the earth. The creature thrashed, but the bindings held, slowly purifying the corruption from its body.

Lin landed softly, dismissing the merge. His three beasts reappeared beside him, panting but victorious. The hunting party stared at him with a mixture of awe and terror.

"Who... who are you?" the lead disciple asked, her voice shaking.

"Lin Xiao," he said simply. "From the outer valley."

The disciples exchanged glances. Lin recognized their robes now—deep blue with silver trim. The Azure Cloud Sect. One of the four major sects in Thousand Beast Valley. Their disciples rarely ventured into the outer swamps.

"You're the one who awakened the ancient temple," the lead disciple said, her eyes widening. "The one with the crippled wind-snake."

"Formerly crippled," Lin replied, letting a hint of amusement creep into his voice.

She bowed deeply. "I am Song Yuxi, inner disciple of the Azure Cloud Sect. We owe you our lives." She straightened, her expression shifting to urgency. "But you must leave this place immediately. The corruption isn't natural—it's spreading from within the valley's core."

Lin's blood ran cold. "What do you mean?"

"The elders," another disciple spat, his face twisted with anger. "They've been feeding the corruption. Using it to cull the weak and test the strong. It's a selection process—a cruel game to determine who's worthy of the Thousand Beast Legacy."

*[System Alert: New information detected. The Thousand Beast Legacy—a forbidden inheritance that grants control over all beasts within the valley's boundaries. Current possessor: unknown.]*

The screen flickered in Lin's vision, and for a moment, he saw something else—a flash of an elderly man with cold eyes and a staff carved from beast bone. The vision vanished as quickly as it appeared.

"How do you know this?" Lin demanded.

Song Yuxi's face paled. "Because I saw it. Three days ago, I followed my master to a secret meeting beneath the Sect Head's Pavilion. The four major sect elders were there, along with someone else. A figure wrapped in shadows, his face hidden. They spoke of the corruption as a 'necessary pruning.' My master sensed me watching and—" She pulled back her sleeve, revealing a blackened wound that pulsed with corruption. "This was his gift to me. I've been running ever since."

Lin stepped forward, examining the wound. The corruption was deep, threading through her meridians like poison ivy. She had days, maybe hours, before it consumed her.

"I can help," he said slowly. "But I need to touch the wound directly. It will hurt."

"Do it," she said without hesitation.

Lin pressed his palm against her arm, activating [Beast Resonance]. The wind-snake's purification venom flowed through him, entering her bloodstream and burning away the corruption. Song Yuxi screamed, her body convulsing, but Lin held fast. The black qi bubbled and evaporated, leaving clean flesh behind.

When it was done, she collapsed, gasping. "How... how did you..."

"I have my secrets." Lin helped her to her feet. "You need to take me to this meeting place. Tonight."

"That's suicide," the other disciple protested. "The elders are all at the fourth level of the Beast Melding Realm or higher. You're barely third level."

"I'm not planning to fight them." Lin's eyes hardened. "I'm planning to expose them. The outer valley has been suffering for months. People dying. Beasts turning. If the elders are responsible, the valley needs to know."

Song Yuxi stared at him for a long moment, then nodded. "I'll take you. But if we die, I'm haunting you in the afterlife."

"Fair enough."

---

They moved through the swamp as night fell, Lin's shadow panther scouting ahead while the lightning hawk circled above. The wind-snake remained coiled around Lin's arm, its senses extended to detect any threats.

The Azure Cloud Sect's compound rose before them like a fortress of jade and marble. Walls twenty feet high, patrolled by disciples with bonded beasts of their own. Lin could feel them—hundreds of spirits, ranging from rank 1 to rank 5, all contained within those walls.

"We can't go through the front," Song Yuxi whispered. "But there's a drainage tunnel near the eastern wall. It leads to the lower gardens, and from there, a hidden passage to the Sect Head's Pavilion."

"The elders will have guards everywhere," Lin said.

"Not in the passage. It's ancient—built before the sect claimed this land. Only the sect head and his closest confidants know of it." She paused. "I only discovered it because I followed my master after that meeting."

They found the tunnel entrance hidden beneath a collapsed shrine, half-submerged in murky water. Lin activated [Beast Resonance: Partial Merge] with the shadow panther, letting its dark vision overlay his own. The tunnel stretched for what felt like miles, its walls covered in faded murals depicting beasts that no longer existed—creatures of legend, their forms blending into one another.

*"This place is old,"* the shadow panther murmured. *"Older than the valley itself. I can feel it in the stone."*

*"What do you mean?"* Lin thought back.

*"The murals... they're not just art. They're instructions. A guide to something greater than simple bonding."*

Lin stopped, examining the wall more closely. The panther was right—the beasts' forms shifted as he watched, their movements creating patterns that resonated with something deep in his cultivation. He felt his [Beast Resonance] skill pulse, responding to the ancient energy.

*[System Alert: Hidden technique detected. Analyzing...]*

*[Analysis complete: 'Primordial Convergence' - A forbidden bonding method that allows simultaneous merging with multiple beasts without cultivation penalties. Requirements: [Beast Resonance] skill, minimum three bonded beasts, and a willing conduit beast.]*

Lin's breath caught. A technique that let him merge without drawbacks? That would change everything. His current merges drained his stamina rapidly, limiting how long he could maintain them. But this—

"We're here," Song Yuxi whispered, pulling him from his thoughts.

The passage ended at a stone door, thin cracks allowing slivers of torchlight to filter through. Lin pressed his ear to the surface and heard voices—low, conspiratorial, dripping with authority.

"—the outer valley is almost ready. Another week of corruption, and the weak will be eliminated. Only the strong will remain for the Legacy."

Lin recognized that voice. It was Elder Shen, the Azure Cloud Sect's beast taming master. A man who had once visited Lin's village to assess potential disciples—and had laughed at Lin's crippled bond.

"The Shadow King grows impatient," another voice said. This one was unfamiliar, cold and echoing as if coming from a great distance. "He demands the ritual be completed before the next lunar eclipse. Fail him, and your sects will be purged along with the outer valley."

A third voice, gruff and angry: "We've held up our end of the bargain. The corruption has spread to every corner of the valley. The beast wave will come as promised."

"And when it does," the cold voice replied, "the Shadow King will claim the Legacy. Your reward will be control over the valley's remnants. A generous offer, considering your incompetence."

Lin's hands clenched into fists. They were planning a beast wave—a massive corruption event that would drive every beast in the valley into a frenzy. Thousands would die. And the elders were orchestrating it all.

"How do you want to play this?" Song Yuxi asked, her voice barely audible.

Lin thought for a moment. Rushing in would get them killed. But if he could record their conversation, broadcast it to the sect—if he could find proof—

*"Let me handle this,"* the shadow panther said. *"I can slip through the shadows, record their words, and return before they sense me."*

*"Too dangerous. If they have detection arrays—"*

*"I am a Shadowmire Stalker, little tamer. Detection arrays are my prey."*

Before Lin could argue, the panther dissolved into darkness, seeping through the stone door like smoke. Lin held his breath, counting the seconds. One. Ten. Thirty.

Then the cold voice spoke again, this time with amusement: "It seems we have an eavesdropper."

The door exploded inward.

Lin rolled, barely avoiding the blast of corrupted qi that shredded the stone around him. He came up in a combat stance, his beasts forming a protective circle around him.

The four sect elders stood before him, their faces twisted with fury. Behind them, a figure wreathed in shadow—tall, skeletal, with eyes that burned like twin suns. The Shadow King.

"So," the Shadow King said, his voice dripping with malice. "The boy with the broken snake. The one who awakened the ancient temple. How... convenient."

Elder Shen stepped forward. "Kill him. Now."

But the Shadow King raised a hand, halting the elder. "No. The boy interests me." He tilted his head, studying Lin like a specimen. "You carry the Resonance. I haven't seen that gift in a thousand years." His smile widened, revealing teeth that were too sharp, too numerous. "I wonder... will you break like the others? Or will you provide some entertainment?"

Lin felt his beasts surge with protective fury. The lightning hawk's feathers crackled. The wind-snake's venom dripped. The shadow panther, having escaped the blast, circled behind the elders, waiting for his command.

*"We can take them,"* the panther said.

*"Maybe,"* Lin replied. *"But not all of them. Not him."*

The Shadow King laughed, a sound like grinding bones. "I see your beasts are eager. Good. This was getting boring." He extended his hand, and black qi swirled around his fingers, forming into a spear of pure corruption. "Let me show you what true power looks like."

Lin's mind raced. He couldn't win this fight. But he didn't need to win—he needed to

title: The Corruption's True Face
content:
Lin's fist connected with the corrupted beast's spine, and the impact sent shockwaves through his entire body. Shadows wrapped around his arm as the panther's power amplified the strike, lightning crackled from his fingertips, and the wind-snake's venom seeped into the creature's wounds.

The beast howled. Its twisted body convulsed, black qi exploding outward in a wave of malevolent energy. Lin leaped back, his three beasts moving as extensions of his will—the shadow panther faded into the darkness, the lightning hawk climbed higher, and the wind-snake coiled around his shoulders, ready to strike.

"Get back!" Lin shouted at the pinned disciples. "Form a defensive circle!"

The disciples scrambled to comply, their own beasts—a fire fox, an iron-shelled tortoise, and a wind-finch—forming a protective ring around them. But their eyes were fixed on Lin, disbelief written across their faces.

"Three beasts," one of them whispered. "He's bonded three beasts."

"Impossible," another said. "No one bonds three beasts before the Beast Master Realm."

Lin ignored them. His attention was locked on the corrupted beast as it turned to face him, its red eyes burning with intelligence that shouldn't exist in a mindless creature. Something was wrong. He'd fought corrupted beasts before—they were savage, driven by instinct, their minds broken by the corruption.

This one was watching him. Studying him.

"You shouldn't exist," the beast spoke, and the words weren't its own. They came from somewhere deeper, layered with a dozen voices speaking in unison. "A tamer with three bonds. The resonance signature... we've been looking for you."

Lin's blood ran cold. "Who are you?"

The corrupted beast laughed—a horrible, grinding sound. "We are what's coming. What's already here. The valley thinks this is a plague, a disease. They're wrong. This is a harvest."

The creature lunged, faster than before. Lin barely had time to react, throwing himself sideways as claws the size of swords slashed through the space where he'd been standing. The shadow panther struck from behind, its claws raking across the beast's legs, but the creature didn't even flinch.

[Beast Resonance: Shadow Bind]

Lin extended his hand, and the panther's power flowed through him. Tendrils of darkness erupted from the ground, wrapping around the corrupted beast's limbs. The creature strained against them, black qi hissing where it touched the shadows.

"You can't hold us forever, little tamer."

"Don't need forever." Lin's eyes flashed. "Hawk! Now!"

The lightning hawk descended like a bolt from heaven, its body wreated in electrical energy. It struck the corrupted beast's head, and a thousand volts surged through the creature's body. The black qi screamed, recoiling from the pure lightning.

And in that moment of weakness, Lin saw it.

Inside the corrupted beast's chest, buried in layers of twisted flesh and broken bone, was a core. But it wasn't corrupted. It was pure—pulsing with golden light, trying to break free.

"What the—"

The beast roared, tearing free of the shadow bindings. It swung wildly, forcing Lin to dodge. The lightning hawk retreated, its energy spent. But Lin couldn't stop staring at what he'd seen.

That core. It wasn't from the corrupted beast. It was inside the corrupted beast.
