*"Someone who has been waiting for a long time."* The corrupted beast took a step forward, then stopped. Its body began to convulse, the black qi surging violently. *"We will meet soon, child of the Valley. And when we do, I will show you what true power looks like."*

The creature's body exploded.

Black qi erupted in every direction, a wave of pure corruption that sent Lin flying backward. He slammed into a tree, the air driven from his lungs. His beasts screamed in his mind, the bond straining under the sudden assault.

When the darkness cleared, the corrupted beast was gone. All that remained was a crater, the ground itself blackened and dead, as if the corruption had devoured the soil's very soul.

The disciples were coughing, struggling to their feet. The young woman approached Lin, her face pale.

"What was that?" she whispered.

Lin didn't answer. His gaze was fixed on the crater, his mind racing. Something had spoken through that beast. Something ancient. Something that knew about his [Beast Resonance] before he had even fully understood it himself.

*"We need to leave,"* the shadow panther said, its form solidifying beside him. *"That presence. I have felt it before. In the deep places of the valley, where even beasts fear to tread."*

"You know what it is?"

*"No. But I know what it wants."* The panther's yellow eyes met Lin's. *"It wants you."*

---

Lin didn't sleep that night.

He had found a dry patch of ground near a collapsed stone bridge, far enough from the corruption site to feel safe, but close enough to observe any changes in the swamp's behavior. The disciples had gone their separate way, thanking him profusely before vanishing into the mist. They had offered to take him to their sect, promised rewards, protection. Lin had declined.

He couldn't afford to be tied down. Not now.

The wind-snake coiled in his lap, its scales warm against his skin. The lightning hawk perched on a broken pillar, its sharp eyes scanning the darkness. The shadow panther was nowhere to be seen, but Lin felt its presence like a second heartbeat, always watching, always waiting.

*"You're thinking too loud,"* the panther's voice echoed.

"Can you blame me?" Lin stared into the fire he had built, watching the flames dance. "Something out there knows about my ability. Something old. Powerful. And it's been waiting for me."

*"Your ability is not unique."*

Lin's head snapped up. "What?"

The panther materialized from the shadows, sitting across from him. Its eyes reflected the firelight, turning them into pools of molten gold. *"Beast Resonance. I did not understand the name until today, but I have heard of it before. In the old stories, passed down through generations of shadow beasts."*

"Tell me."

*"Long ago, before the sects, before the Valley was tamed, there were beast speakers. Humans who could hear the voices of all creatures, who could merge their souls with beasts and become something greater. They were the first tamers, the ones who taught our ancestors to bond."*

Lin leaned forward. "What happened to them?"

*"They were hunted. Killed. Their bloodlines scattered to the winds."* The panther's voice grew dark. *"The ancient beast, the one that spoke through the corrupted creature today—it was one of them. A beast speaker who turned against his own kind, who used his power to enslave rather than bond. The old stories call him the Devourer."*

"Devourer," Lin repeated, the word tasting like ash on his tongue.

*"He consumes beasts. Absorbs their essence, their abilities, their souls. He is not a tamer—he is a parasite. And he has been dormant for centuries, buried deep beneath the Valley, feeding on the corruption that seeps through the land."*

A cold realization crept into Lin's mind. "The corruption. The plague spreading through the Valley. He's causing it."

*"He is the source. The corruption is his breath, seeping through the cracks in his prison. Every corrupted beast you kill, every bit of black qi you destroy—it only weakens his cage further. You are not stopping him. You are helping him escape."*

Lin's hands trembled. He looked down at the wind-snake, at the lightning hawk, felt the shadow panther's presence. Three beasts. Three bonds. He had been so proud, so certain of his growing power.

"Then what do I do?" he asked, his voice barely a whisper. "If fighting the corruption only makes it worse, what am I supposed to do?"

The panther was silent for a long moment.

*"You find the other beast speakers."*

Lin blinked. "There are others?"

*"If the Devourer survived, others must have as well. Scattered. Hidden. They carry the same Resonance you do. Find them. Combine your power. And together, seal the Devourer before he breaks free completely."*

Lin's mind raced. Other beast speakers. A hidden network of people like him. It was almost too much to process.

"How do I find them?"

*"The corruption is a map. It spreads from the Devourer's prison, but it also flows toward concentrations of life force. Beasts. Humans. Spirit energy. Follow the corruption to its source, and you will find pockets of resistance. Places where the corruption cannot touch."*

"Sanctuaries."

*"Yes."* The panther stood, stretching its lithe body. *"I know of one. A hidden grove, deep in the eastern marshes. It is protected by an ancient barrier, one that predates even the Devourer's imprisonment. If any beast speakers remain, they will be there."*

Lin rose, his decision made. "Then we go east."

*"It is a three-day journey through corrupted territory. The Devourer's influence grows stronger the closer we get. We will be attacked. We may not survive."*

Lin looked at his beasts—the crippled snake that had started it all, the hawk that had chosen him in the storm, the panther that had tested his worth. They had trusted him. Believed in him.

"I didn't survive the temple, the swamp, and a corrupted beast just to give up now." Lin's voice hardened. "Let's go find my people."

---

They traveled through the night, the shadow panther guiding them through paths only it could see. The swamp grew darker, the mist thicker, the air heavier with each step. Corrupted beasts prowled in the distance, their roars echoing through the trees, but none approached.

It was almost as if they were being allowed to pass.

Lin didn't like it.

As dawn broke, painting the sky in shades of sickly green, they reached the edge of the eastern marshes. Before them lay a vast expanse of black water, dotted with islands of twisted vegetation. In the center, barely visible through the mist, was a faint golden glow.

"The grove," the panther said.

Lin took a step forward.

A voice, cold and amused, echoed from behind him.

*"Going somewhere, little speaker?"*

Lin spun around.

Standing twenty feet away, atop a mound of corrupted earth, was a figure. Humanoid, but wrong. Its body was composed entirely of black qi, shifting and writhing like living smoke. Two points of red light burned where its eyes should be.

The Devourer's avatar.

*"I've waited so long for someone like you to appear,"* the figure said, its voice dripping with hunger. *"A pure Resonance. Untainted. Perfect for consumption."*

Lin's beasts moved into formation around him, growling, hissing, shrieking.

"Get behind me," Lin ordered, his hands already beginning to glow with the light of [Beast Resonance].

The figure laughed, a sound like grinding bone.

*"You think your little tricks will work on me? I invented Resonance, boy. I perfected it. Every technique you know, every ability you've bonded—they all came from me."*

It raised a hand, and the black qi surged forward, forming tendrils that shot toward Lin like spears.

Lin activated the merge.

*[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge]*

Shadows wrapped

title: The Price of Power
content:
The corrupted beast's roar shook the swamp as Lin's merged form struck like living lightning. His shadow-wreathed fist punched through the creature's ribcage, black qi spraying like arterial blood. The beast stumbled, its twisted body struggling to maintain cohesion.

"Keep pressure on its flanks!" Lin shouted, his voice carrying three distinct tones—human, hawk, serpent. The hunting party scrambled to obey, launching ranged attacks from behind their rock formation.

The shadow panther's voice whispered through his mind. *"The corruption runs deep. This is no natural beast. It was made."*

Lin dodged a sweeping claw, the wind-snake's instincts guiding his movements. "Made by who?"

Before the panther could answer, the corrupted beast's chest cavity split open. A tendril of pure black qi lashed out, catching Lin across the ribs. He felt the corruption trying to burrow into his meridians, and for a terrifying moment, his merged form wavered.

Then the lightning hawk's energy flared, burning the corruption away. Lin gasped, scrambling backward.

"Everyone, fall back!" he commanded. "This thing is—"

The corrupted beast's head exploded.

Not from an attack—it simply detonated, black qi erupting like a volcanic geyser. The hunting party screamed, diving for cover. Lin raised his arms, shadows and lightning weaving together into a barrier.

When the blast cleared, the beast was gone. In its place stood a figure—humanoid, but wrong. Its body was composed entirely of solidified black qi, veins of red corruption pulsing beneath its surface. It wore the tattered remains of a cultivator's robe, the symbol on its chest half-obscured by decay.

But Lin recognized it anyway.

The Emberheart Sect. One of the three great sects that ruled the valley.

"You," the figure rasped, its voice sounding like gravel being ground between stones. "Boy with three beasts. I can feel you. The resonance... it sings."

Lin's blood ran cold. "What are you?"

The figure laughed—a horrible, wet sound. "A messenger. An invitation. The Lord of Corruption has noticed you, little tamer. He finds your power... interesting."

The shadow panther materialized beside Lin, hackles raised. The lightning hawk circled overhead, sparks crackling warningly. Even the wind-snake uncoiled, ready to strike.

"I don't know who this Lord of Corruption is," Lin said, his voice steady despite the fear clawing at his chest. "But you can tell him I'm not interested."

"Interest is not a choice." The figure raised a hand, and black qi swirled around its fingers. "The corruption spreads. It consumes. And those who resist become part of the feast. You have three days to present yourself at the Crimson Maw—the corrupted zone at the valley's heart. Refuse, and your village burns. Your family. Everyone you've ever known."

Lin's vision went red. "Touch them and I'll—"

"You'll what?" The figure tilted its head, mocking. "Kill me? I'm already dead, boy. I've been dead for months. This is just a shell, puppeted by a will far greater than yours."

The black qi construct began to dissolve, corruption bleeding back into the swamp.

"Three days," the voice echoed, fading. "The Crimson Maw. Come alone, or watch your world burn."

Then it was gone, leaving only silence and the stench of decay.

Lin dropped his merged form, exhaustion crashing over him like a wave. He fell to his knees, gasping. The shadow panther pressed against his side, offering support.

"Lord of Corruption," the panther said, its voice grim. "I have heard whispers of this name. He is ancient. A being from before the valley was formed, sealed away by the first beast tamers."

"And now he's breaking free," Lin finished. "The corrupted beasts... the spreading plague. It's all him."

A hand touched his shoulder. Lin looked up to see one of the hunting party disciples—a young woman with dirt-streaked face and terrified eyes.

"Thank you," she said. "You saved us. I'm Zhao Mei, of the Jade Serpent Sect. If there's anything we can do to repay you—"

"Stay away from the Crimson Maw," Lin said, getting to his feet. "Tell your sect. Tell everyone. That place is a death trap."

Zhao Mei's face paled. "But... our elders have been sending teams to investigate the corruption. They think it's a natural phenomenon."

"It's not. It's a prison, and the occupant is waking up." Lin turned to leave, then stopped. "Three days. I need to prepare."

He walked into the mist, his three beasts falling into formation around him.

---

Back at Lin's hidden cave—a small shelter he'd carved into a cliff face weeks ago—he sat cross-legged, his beasts surrounding him in a protective circle. He'd spent the last hour searching his memories, trying to find any information about the Lord of Corruption.

Nothing. The name wasn't recorded in any of the village's texts.

But the shadow panther had knowledge. Ancient knowledge, passed down through generations of shadow beasts.

"The Lord of Corruption was once a beast tamer," the panther said, its voice resonating in Lin's mind. "The greatest of his age. He discovered a forbidden technique—a way to merge with beasts permanently, absorbing their power until he became something beyond human."

Lin's eyes widened. "That sounds like..."

"Beast Resonance? No. What you do is harmonious—a temporary union of equals. What he did was consumption. He devoured his beasts, taking their power for himself. The more he consumed, the more powerful he became. And the more corrupted."

The lightning hawk shrieked, agitated. *"A monster."*

"Yes. A monster." The panther continued. "The first beast tamers realized they couldn't defeat him. So they sealed him instead, binding him to the valley's heart. But seals weaken over time. And it seems his influence is spreading."

Lin stared at his hands. Three beasts. Three separate powers flowing through him. He'd thought he was special, chosen by fate.

Now he wasn't so sure.

"What if... what if Beast Resonance is connected to him?" Lin asked, voicing the fear that had been gnawing at him. "What if the only reason I can merge with beasts is because his corruption is already inside me?"

The wind-snake hissed, coiling protectively around his neck. *"No. You are pure. We would feel corruption in your soul."*

"But the Lord of Corruption—he started as a beast tamer too, didn't he? What if I'm walking the same path?"

Silence. None of his beasts could answer that.

Lin took a deep breath, forcing himself to calm. "Okay. Panicking won't help. I have three days. I need to get stronger. Strong enough to face this Lord, or at least survive long enough to find another solution."

He turned to the shadow panther. "You said you'd test me. What test?"

The panther's eyes gleamed. "The shadow trial. A journey through the reflection realm—a space where shadows become solid, and darkness reveals truth. Survive it, and I will teach you my deepest techniques. Fail, and your soul becomes part of the shadow."

Lin didn't hesitate. "Let's do it."

"Are you certain? The trial will push you beyond your limits. Many beasts have attempted it and failed."

"I don't have a choice." Lin stood, his body aching but his resolve firm. "If I can't get stronger, everyone I care about dies. So yes. I'm certain."

The panther studied him for a long moment, then nodded. "Very well. Prepare yourself. The trial begins at midnight."

---

Lin spent the remaining hours before midnight meditating, cycling his qi through his meridians. The power from his three beasts flowed through him in distinct streams—the wind-snake's flexibility, the lightning hawk's speed, the shadow panther's stealth. Each one felt different, but together, they formed a symphony of energy.

He thought about his village. About his mother, who'd never doubted him even when everyone else had. About the elders who'd called him worthless. About the other beast tamers who'd laughed at his crippled wind-snake.

They'd all be dead in three days if he failed.

"Worthless," he muttered, a bitter smile crossing his lips. "That's what they called me. Maybe they were right. Maybe I am just a freak with a broken talent."

*"No."*

The wind-snake's voice was soft but firm. It uncoiled from his neck and slithered onto his lap, meeting his eyes with its own.

*"You are not broken. You are different. There is a distinction."*

Lin blinked. "You've never talked this much before."

*"Because I never needed to. But now you need to hear the truth."* The snake's tongue flickered. *"I was born with a broken core. The elders said I would never grow, never evolve, never be anything but a cripple. They were right about my body. But they were wrong about my spirit."*

Lin felt tears prick at his eyes. "Xiao... I never knew."

*"Because it didn't matter. What mattered was that you saw me, not my brokenness. You bonded with me when no one else would. And because of you, I am more than I ever could have been alone."* The snake pressed its head against his cheek. *"You are not walking the path of corruption. You are walking the path of connection. There is a difference. Never forget that."*

Lin wrapped his hands around the snake, holding it close. "Thank you, Xiao. I needed that."

The shadow panther's voice broke the moment. "It's time."

Lin looked up. The moon hung directly overhead, casting the world in silver light. The shadows around them had begun to move, writhing like living creatures.

"The reflection realm is opening," the panther said. "Once you enter, you will face your greatest fears. Your deepest doubts. Everything you've ever hidden from yourself."

Lin stood, the wind-snake coiling around his arm. "I'm ready."

"Are you?" The panther stepped forward, its form beginning to dissolve into shadow. "Then follow me, little tamer. And pray that the darkness doesn't consume you."

The shadows surged, swallowing Lin whole.

---

He fell.

For what felt like hours, he fell through an endless void. No light. No sound. No sensation except the terror of infinite descent.

Then he landed.

He was in his village, but wrong. The buildings were twisted, the sky blood-red. And in the center of the square stood a figure—a version of himself, but corrupted. Black qi leaked from its eyes, its mouth, its very pores.

"Hello, Lin Xiao," the corrupted version said, smiling with too many teeth. "I've been waiting for you."

Lin's beasts were gone. His power was gone. He was just a boy, alone and afraid.

"I'm not you," Lin said, his voice trembling despite his best efforts.

"Oh but you are." The corrupted version stepped closer. "You have the same talent. The same hunger. The same desire to be more than what you are. The only difference is time."

"I'll never become you."

"Will you?" The corrupted version laughed. "You're already bonding with beasts faster than any tamer in history. You're already merging with them in ways that shouldn't be possible. How long before you want more? How long before one beast isn't enough? How long before you start consuming?"

Lin clenched his fists. "I

title: Chapter 8
content:
CHAPTER 8: The Price of Resonance

The corrupted beast's charge carried it through Lin's afterimage, its massive body crashing into the rock formation behind him. Stone exploded outward, and the disciples screamed, scrambling for cover.

Lin didn't watch. He was already moving, his body responding to instincts that weren't entirely human. The shadow panther's predatory grace guided his footwork. The lightning hawk's spatial awareness painted the battlefield in his mind—every pebble, every breath, every twitch of corrupted muscle. The wind-snake's cold calculation filtered through it all, finding angles of attack a human mind would never see.

*"Behind its left shoulder,"* the snake's voice whispered. *"The corruption is thinnest there."*

Lin pivoted, shadows wrapping around his legs like living cloaks. The corrupted beast swung a massive arm, but he was already beneath it, sliding through the mud with panther-born fluidity. His right hand extended, lightning gathering between his fingers.

*[Beast Resonance: Lightning Strike]*

A bolt of concentrated electricity slammed into the creature's shoulder joint. Black ichor exploded from the wound, sizzling against the rain-soaked ground. The beast howled, a sound that vibrated through Lin's bones and made his teeth ache.

But it didn't fall.

Instead, it turned. Slowly, deliberately. Its burning red eyes fixed on Lin with something that looked almost like recognition.

"You," it rasped.

The voice was wrong. It shouldn't have been able to speak. Corrupted beasts were mindless, driven only by the need to consume and spread their taint. But this one formed words, broken and wet, like rocks grinding together in a river of blood.

"Who sent you?" Lin asked, buying time. His mind raced through every possibility—a rogue beast tamer, a failed experiment, a natural disaster given form and fury.

The creature laughed. It was a horrible sound, full of cracks and gaps where its throat had been torn open. "Who *sent* me? No one sent me, little tamer. I am what happens when your kind plays with forces you don't understand."

Its body shifted, the twisted flesh flowing like wax. New limbs pushed through the corrupted skin—dozens of them, each ending in razor-sharp claws. The disciples screamed again, pressing themselves against the rock face.

Lin's blood ran cold.

*"That's not natural corruption,"* the shadow panther said, its voice tight with something Lin had never heard before. Fear. *"That's a failed fusion. A beast tamer tried to merge with a corrupted beast and lost control."*

"No," Lin breathed. "That's impossible. Corruption destroys the bond. Everyone knows that—"

*"Everyone knows that mountain snow is cold and that the eastern winds bring rain,"* the wind-snake interrupted, its tone sharp. *"But knowing and understanding are different things. Someone has been experimenting."*

The corrupted beast lunged.

Lin was fast, but the creature was faster now. The new limbs moved with sickening coordination, each one striking from a different angle. He dodged two, three, four—but the fifth caught him across the chest, sending him spinning through the air.

He hit the ground hard, mud filling his mouth and eyes. Pain exploded through his ribs, and he tasted copper. The triple resonance flickered, the connection between his beasts wavering.

*"Stay with us!"* the lightning hawk screamed in his mind.

Lin forced himself to focus. The bond strengthened again, but something felt different. Wrong. The shadow panther's presence had grown cold, and the wind-snake's thoughts were becoming harder to separate from his own.

*[Beast Resonance: Sustained Merge - Warning: Mental Synchronization at 67%]*

The interface blinked in his vision, and Lin's heart nearly stopped. He'd never seen that warning before. What did it mean? What was the threshold?

The corrupted beast loomed over him, its ragged form blocking out the moonlight. "I can smell them on you," it hissed. "Three bonds. Three souls, tangled with yours. You think you're special, little tamer? You think you've discovered something new?"

Lin struggled to his feet, his body screaming in protest. "I think you're about to find out what happens when you try to kill my beasts."

He raised his hand, and all three of his bonded companions answered.

The shadow panther erupted from the ground beneath the creature, its claws wreathed in darkness that seemed to drink the light. The lightning hawk dove from above, its body becoming a spear of pure electricity. The wind-snake coiled around Lin's arm, its venom dripping onto the corrupted flesh below.

*[Beast Resonance: Triple Strike]*

The attack hit simultaneously. Shadow, lightning, and venom converged on the corrupted beast's chest, boring through its defenses like a drill through wet paper. The creature screamed, a sound that echoed through the swamp and shook the very air.

For a moment, Lin thought it was over.

Then the corrupted beast stopped screaming. Stopped moving. Its head tilted at an unnatural angle, and when it spoke again, the voice had changed.

*"So you found one."*

The voice was smooth. Cultured. Completely out of place coming from the twisted, broken face of the corrupted beast.

Lin's blood turned to ice. "Who are you?"

*"Someone who has been watching you, Lin Xiao. Someone who knows exactly what you are."* The creature's mouth stretched into a smile that split its cheeks. *"Beast Resonance. The ability to merge with your bonded. A talent that hasn't appeared in a thousand years."*

"How do you know that name?"

*"Because I was the last one to have it."*

The corrupted beast's body began to collapse, the flesh sloughing off in great chunks. But the voice continued, echoing from everywhere and nowhere at once.

*"I know the truth of the resonance, Lin Xiao. I know why it disappeared. I know the price it demands."* A pause, heavy with meaning. *"And I know how to use it to destroy everything."*

The creature dissolved into a pool of black ichor, spreading across the ground like dark water. The red light in its eyes faded, and the oppressive presence lifted.

But the voice remained, a whisper on the wind.

*"Find me, little tamer. Before the resonance devours you from within."*

Lin collapsed to his knees, gasping. The triple merge shattered, and he felt the separation like a physical wound. His beasts retreated into their bonds, exhausted and shaken.

"What was that?" one of the disciples asked, her voice trembling. "What did it mean?"

Lin didn't answer. He was staring at his hands, where faint black lines were spreading across his skin like veins of corruption.

*[Mental Synchronization: 71%]*

The number was still climbing.

The shadow panther appeared beside him, its form flickering with exhaustion. *"Little tamer..."*

"I know," Lin said quietly. "I know."

He looked up at the moon, his mind racing. Someone was out there—someone who knew his secret, someone who had the same power he did. And they were hunting him.

But that wasn't what scared him most.

What scared him was the creeping darkness in his own soul, slowly spreading as his beasts' minds merged with his own. The resonance was supposed to make him stronger. But what if it was also destroying him?

What if the price of power was his own humanity?

A hand touched his shoulder. The female disciple had approached, her face pale but determined. "I'm Wei Qing, from the Azure Lotus Sect. We owe you our lives." She hesitated. "But I have to ask—what *are* you?"

Lin looked at her, and for the first time, he saw the fear in her eyes. Not fear of the corrupted beast. Fear of *him*.

"Just a beast tamer," he said, forcing a smile that felt like a lie. "Nothing special."

Behind him, the black lines on his skin continued to spread, invisible in the darkness but growing with every heartbeat.

And somewhere in the depths of Thousand Beast Valley, the voice laughed.

title: The Eighth Seal
content:
The corrupted beast's claws tore through empty air where Lin had stood a heartbeat before. He rematerialized twenty feet to the left, shadows peeling off his body like shed skin. The lightning hawk's cry split the night as it dove again, talons raking across the creature's flank.

Black ichor sprayed. The beast howled.

"Keep its attention split!" Lin shouted at the pinned disciples. "Don't let it focus on one target!"

Three of them scrambled to obey, launching spirit techniques that splashed harmlessly against the creature's hide. Useless. Their cultivation was too low. But the distraction bought Lin precious seconds.

*[Wind-Snake: Venom Strike]*

He felt the snake's essence surge through his arm as he thrust his palm forward. A stream of corrosive green energy shot out, striking the beast's leg. The corrupted flesh sizzled and hissed, eating away at the bone beneath.

The creature stumbled.

*[Shadow Panther: Phase Strike]*

Lin vanished and reappeared directly above the beast, both hands wreathed in darkness. He plunged them down like daggers, piercing through the creature's skull. Black qi erupted from the wound, and the beast's red eyes flickered.

It crashed to the ground, sending tremors through the swamp.

Silence. Heavy breathing.

Lin landed on his feet, panting. His body ached. The triple merge was powerful, but it drained him faster than he'd expected. His meridians burned like someone had poured molten iron through them.

"I—" one of the disciples started, stepping forward. "Thank you. We were on a hunting expedition and—"

"Save it." Lin cut him off, his eyes fixed on the corpse. Something was wrong. The corrupted beast's body was... dissolving. Not decomposing. Dissolving. Into black smoke that rose and twisted before being sucked into the ground.

"That's not normal," the shadow panther growled inside his mind.

"No," Lin agreed. "It's not."

He walked toward where the smoke had disappeared. The ground was marked with strange symbols—carved into the earth, hidden beneath a thin layer of mud. He hadn't noticed them before. They glowed faintly red, pulsing like a heartbeat.

"What are those?" one of the disciples asked, pointing.

Lin knelt, tracing one of the symbols with his finger. A jolt of energy shot up his arm. Familiar energy. *Corruption.* But structured. Intentional.

"This isn't natural," he said, his voice cold. "Someone placed these here."

The shadow panther materialized beside him, its eyes scanning the symbols. *"I've seen these before. In the old temple. Before it fell to rot."*

Lin's blood ran cold. "The temple? The one that's been sealed for a hundred years?"

*"The same."*

A disciple—the one who'd spoken first—stepped forward. He was young, maybe sixteen, with the blue robes of the Azure Peak Sect. His face was pale. "I've heard stories about that temple. They say the beast masters who entered never came back. That something inside devoured them."

"Stories are often rooted in truth," Lin said, standing. He looked at the symbols again. Dozens of them, arranged in concentric circles around the clearing. This wasn't a random attack. This was a ritual. "How long have you been hunting here?"

"Three days," the disciple answered. "We came to hunt corrupted beasts for their cores. They've been appearing more frequently in this region."

"More frequently," Lin repeated. "And you didn't think that was strange?"

The disciple's face reddened. "We... we thought it was just the plague spreading."

"It is the plague spreading." Lin turned to face the group fully. "But plagues don't arrange themselves in geometric patterns. Someone is guiding the corruption. Feeding it. Directing it."

He looked back at the dissolving corpse. The black smoke was still pouring into the ground, but now he could see where it was going—a central point in the clearing, where the symbols converged. A stone pillar, half-buried in mud, covered in ancient script.

Lin walked toward it, the disciples following at a cautious distance. The pillar was cold to the touch. The writing was worn, barely legible, but he could make out fragments.

*...and when the eighth seal breaks...*

*...the beast of a thousand forms shall rise...*

*...and the valley shall know only hunger...*

"Eighth seal," Lin muttered. "How many have already broken?"

The pillar didn't answer. But something else did.

A crack.

Deep beneath the ground. Followed by a rumbling that shook the swamp. The disciples cried out, stumbling. Birds erupted from the trees. The lightning hawk shrieked in alarm, circling overhead.

*"Something is waking,"* the shadow panther said, its voice tense.

Lin's heart pounded. The symbols on the ground flared bright red, then died. The pillar's script began to glow, pulsing in time with his heartbeat.

*Thump. Thump. Thump.*

Then a voice. Ancient. Cracked. Speaking directly into his mind.

*"You carry the beast within you, child. But do you carry the will to master it?"*

Lin's vision swam. The world tilted. He saw—*no, felt*—something stirring beneath the swamp. Something vast. Something that had been sleeping for centuries.

And it was hungry.

"Who are you?" Lin demanded, gripping his head.

*"I am what the seals hold back. I am what the valley fears. I am the hunger that devoured your predecessors."* The voice laughed, and it sounded like grinding bones. *"But you... you are different. You carry three beasts within you. I have not tasted such a soul in ages."*

The ground split open.

A chasm ripped through the clearing, swallowing the disciples' camp, their supplies, their weapons. They screamed, scrambling back. Lin stood at the edge, looking down into darkness so absolute it seemed to drink the light.

*"Come to me, child of beasts. The eighth seal weakens. And I am so very hungry."*

The chasm pulsed. Black tendrils of corruption lashed out, wrapping around the disciple who had spoken to Lin. He screamed, thrashing, as the corruption burrowed into his skin, turning his veins black.

"Let him go!" Lin shouted, reaching out.

But the tendrils were faster. They yanked the disciple into the chasm, and his scream cut off abruptly.

Silence.

The remaining disciples stared in horror. One of them, a girl with tears streaming down her face, looked at Lin. "What... what do we do?"

Lin's mind raced. The temple. The seals. The voice. This was bigger than a single corrupted beast. This was the source. And it had just invited him inside.

He looked at the chasm. The darkness stared back.

*"Tick-tock, little tamer."*

Lin's fists clenched. His three beasts stirred within him, sensing his resolve. The wind-snake hissed. The lightning hawk screeched. The shadow panther snarled.

*"We go in,"* Lin said, his voice steady despite the fear crawling up his spine.

"That's suicide!" one of the disciples protested.

"Staying here is suicide." Lin turned to them. "Go back to the valley. Warn the sect elders. Tell them the eighth seal is breaking and something ancient is waking."

"And you?"

Lin looked at the chasm. "I'm going to buy you time."

Before anyone could stop him, he jumped.

The darkness swallowed him whole.

---

He fell for a long time.

Or maybe it was only seconds. Time had no meaning here. The corruption pressed against him from all sides, trying to burrow into his skin, his soul, his mind. But his beasts fought back. The wind-snake's venom purified the air. The lightning hawk's electricity burned away the taint. The shadow panther's darkness shielded his thoughts.

He landed hard, his knees buckling. Stone. Cold, damp stone. He was in a cavern, lit by a faint red glow. Pillars stretched into the darkness above, covered in the same script as the one on the surface. And in the center of the cavern—

A door.

Massive. Black. Carved with images of beasts—thousands of them, all twisted and screaming. The door was cracked, hairline fractures spiderwebbing across its surface. Black smoke seeped through the cracks, pooling on the ground like blood.

And standing before the door, waiting for him, was a figure.

Human. Dressed in robes so old they had turned to rags. His face was gaunt, his eyes hollow, his skin stretched tight over bones. He smiled, revealing teeth filed to points.

"I was wondering when someone would answer," the figure said, his voice the same grinding-bones sound from before. "I've been calling for so long."

Lin's beasts bristled. "You're the corrupted beast?"

The figure laughed. "Corrupted? No, child. I am not corrupted. I am the one who does the corrupting." He spread his arms, and the black smoke swirled around him like a cloak. "I am what lives beyond the eighth seal. And you have freed me."

"I haven't freed anything."

"No?" The figure stepped closer. "You shattered the seventh seal when you bonded with your first beast. A crippled wind-snake, broken in core and spirit. But you healed it. You gave it purpose. You made it whole." He grinned. "That act of creation cracked the seal."

Lin's blood ran cold. "That's impossible."

"Is it? The seals are maintained by balance—life against death, creation against destruction. Every bond you form, every beast you heal, you tip the scales toward life. And the seals..." He gestured at the cracked door. "...they hunger for death."

The door groaned. Another crack spiderwebbed across its surface.

"The eighth seal is breaking, child. And when it does, I will feast. On your beasts. On your soul. On this entire valley." The figure's eyes gleamed. "Unless you stop me."

Lin's hand went to his chest, feeling the bonds of his three beasts. "How?"

The figure laughed again, long and loud. "Simple, child. You must kill them. All of them. Every beast you have ever bonded with, every beast you will ever bond with. Feed their death into the seals. Rebalance the scales."

Lin stared at him in horror.

"That is the only way." The figure's smile widened. "So. What will it be? Your beasts? Or the entire valley?"

The door cracked again.

And deep within Lin's soul, the wind-snake coiled tighter, the lightning hawk's wings trembled, and the shadow panther's growl faded into silence. They were waiting for his answer.

The chasm above began to close, sealing them in.

Tick-tock.

title: The Ancient's Awakening
content:
The corrupted beast's charge tore through the mist like a boulder through silk. Lin's merged form flickered—shadow stepping left, lightning surging right, serpentine grace coiling beneath. The creature's massive claw passed through his afterimage, tearing a gash in the earth where he'd stood a heartbeat before.

*"It's faster than it looks,"* the shadow panther's voice warned.

*"And angrier,"* the lightning hawk added.

*"Focus,"* Lin growled, his own voice layered with three distinct tones. He could feel them—his beasts, their instincts bleeding into his own. The panther wanted to stalk, to wait for the perfect moment. The hawk wanted to strike from above, to harry and weaken. The wind-snake wanted to poison, to slow the beast's movements until it collapsed.

Three strategies. One mind.

He let all three flow through him.

Lin shot forward, shadows wrapping his feet, lightning propelling his movements. He slid beneath the corrupted beast's next swing, his hand pressing against its exposed belly. Venom seeped from his palm, black and viscous, eating into the corrupted flesh.

The beast howled, staggering back.

"Keep it pinned!" the lead disciple shouted, raising his hands. A golden chain formed between his palms—a binding technique. The other disciples followed suit, chains of light lashing out to wrap around the creature's limbs.

*"Now!"* Lin commanded.

The shadow panther materialized on the beast's back, claws digging deep into its spine. The lightning hawk dove, striking the creature's eyes with bolts of concentrated electricity. The wind-snake struck at its throat, fangs sinking deep.

Lin felt the corruption resist—a sickening presence that tried to push back, to infect his beasts through the connection. He gritted his teeth and pushed harder, his [Beast Resonance] flaring bright.

The corrupted beast collapsed, its body dissolving into black mist.

Silence fell over the swamp.

Lin's merged form separated, his beasts returning to their positions around him. He swayed, exhaustion hitting him like a wave. Three beasts. Three simultaneous merges. His meridians burned with the strain.

"You're... you're Lin Xiao," the lead disciple said, recognition dawning in his eyes. "The cripple with the broken snake."

"I prefer 'beast tamer with potential,'" Lin said, forcing a smile.

The disciple's face paled. "We need to get back to the valley. Now. The corruption is spreading faster than anyone anticipated. The elders called an emergency assembly this morning."

Lin's smile faded. "What happened?"

"They found something," another disciple whispered, her voice trembling. "In the ruins beneath the Valley of Eternal Mist. An ancient beast core. Still pulsing with power." She swallowed. "They think it's the source of the corruption."

*"That's not possible,"* the wind-snake hissed in Lin's mind. *"Beast cores dissolve when the beast dies. They don't persist."*

*"Unless the beast never truly died,"* the shadow panther said, its voice heavy with dread.

Lin felt cold seep into his bones. "Take me to the assembly."

---

The journey back to Thousand Beast Valley's central hall took three hours through treacherous terrain. Lin moved at the front of the group, his beasts scanning for threats. Twice more, they encountered corrupted creatures—smaller this time, dispatched quickly.

But each battle left Lin more drained. The [Beast Resonance] was powerful, but it demanded more from him with each beast he bonded. Three was already pushing his limits. Four would require a breakthrough to the fourth level of the Beast Melding Realm.

The central hall loomed ahead, carved into the side of Mount Ten Thousand Souls. Ancient stone pillars rose a hundred feet into the air, covered in carvings of beasts long extinct. Elders in flowing robes surrounded a central platform, their faces grim.

Lin's arrival drew stares. Whispers rippled through the crowd.

"Is that him? The one who bonded the shadow panther?"

"Three beasts? Impossible for someone at his level."

"He's the one who cleansed the corrupted beast in the swamp."

Lin ignored them, pushing through the crowd until he reached the front. Elder Feng stood on the platform, his face ashen. Beside him, a crystalline box sat on a pedestal, containing what looked like a beating heart made of pure darkness.

"The ancient core," Elder Feng announced, his voice carrying across the hall. "Unearthed three days ago from the depths of the Valley of Eternal Mist. Initial assessments suggest it belonged to a Primordial Beast."

Gasps echoed through the hall. Primordial Beasts were legends—creatures that existed before the current era, when beasts and humans were one species. They were the ancestors of all spirit beasts.

*"That's not a Primordial Beast core,"* the shadow panther said, its voice sharp. *"I've seen one before. This is different. This is... wrong."*

Lin stepped forward. "Elder Feng, may I examine it?"

Feng's eyes narrowed. "You? Why?"

"Because I've fought the corruption firsthand. I've felt its touch through my bond. There's something the experts might miss."

More whispers. A young man stepped forward—Wei Chen, Lin's old rival, his golden-winged eagle perched on his shoulder. "You think you're qualified to examine an artifact from the ancient era? You can barely control three beasts without collapsing."

Lin met his gaze. "I'm the one who cleansed a Rank 4 corrupted beast while your sect was running for help."

Wei Chen's face reddened. "That's—"

"Enough," Elder Feng snapped. He studied Lin for a long moment, then nodded. "Approach. But if you touch it without permission, the protective formation will incinerate you."

Lin walked to the platform, his heart pounding. Up close, the core was mesmerizing—pulsing with a rhythm that seemed to match his heartbeat. Tendrils of black mist curled from its surface, reaching for the edges of the crystal box.

He extended his hand, palm open.

*"What are you doing?"* the lightning hawk shrieked.

*"Trust me,"* Lin said.

He activated [Beast Resonance]—not merging with his beasts, but reaching toward the core. His perception extended like a thread of light, touching the pulsating darkness.

The world vanished.

Lin found himself in a void, surrounded by stars. A figure stood before him—tall, ethereal, with eyes that held the weight of millennia.

*"You can see me,"* the figure said, its voice echoing across dimensions. *"Finally. Someone who can see me."*

"Who are you?" Lin asked.

*"I am what remains of the last Primordial Beast. Trapped in this core for ten thousand years while your kind picked at my bones."* The figure stepped closer, and Lin felt its power—immense, ancient, terrifying. *"You have the gift of Resonance. The ability to merge with beasts. Do you know what that means?"*

Lin shook his head.

*"It means you're the first human since the Primordial Era capable of bonding with me."* The figure smiled, and it was not a pleasant expression. *"I can give you power beyond imagination. I can make you a god among beast tamers."*

"And in exchange?"

*"In exchange? Simple."* The figure's eyes flared with darkness. *"You let me out of this cage. Let me consume the corruption that's spreading through your valley. And together, we remake this world in our image."*

Lin felt the offer pressing against his soul, tempting him. The power was real—he could feel it, vast and intoxicating.

Then he felt something else. His beasts. The wind-snake, coiled around his heart. The lightning hawk, perched in his thoughts. The shadow panther, a darkness within his own.

They were afraid.

*"Don't trust it,"* the wind-snake whispered. *"I've felt hunger before. This is different. This is starvation."*

Lin looked at the Primordial Beast, truly looked. Beneath the grandeur, beneath the promises, he saw it.

Desperation.

"You're not offering me power," Lin said slowly. "You're offering me a leash."

The figure's smile froze.

"You're trapped in that core. Dying. The corruption isn't coming from you—it's feeding on you. Eating away at what's left of your existence." Lin stepped forward. "You don't want a partner. You want a vessel."

The void shattered.

Lin gasped, stumbling back from the platform. The crystal box shook violently, cracks spiderwebbing across its surface.

"Seal it!" Elder Feng shouted. "Now!"

Formations flared around the core, layers upon layers of binding arrays. The core pulsed once, twice—then stilled.

But everyone in the hall had seen Lin's reaction. Had felt the surge of power.

Wei Chen's eyes gleamed with malice. "The cripple tampered with the artifact! He's trying to steal its power!"

The crowd's murmurs turned hostile. Elders exchanged dark glances.

And in Lin's mind, the Primordial Beast's voice whispered: *"They'll turn on you, you know. They always do. When you're ready to accept my offer, I'll be waiting. No matter how deep they bury me."*

Lin looked at his hands. His beasts stirred, ready to protect him.

But he knew the truth.

He'd just made an enemy of the most dangerous thing in Thousand Beast Valley.

And it was offering him a deal he might not be able to refuse.

title: Chapter 11
content:
CHAPTER 11: The Fifth Seal

Lin Xiao stood in the center of the carnage, his triple-merged form still crackling with residual energy. The corrupted beast lay in pieces around him, black qi dissolving into the morning mist. The disciples he'd saved were staring at him like he was a demon.

He couldn't blame them.

Two months ago, he'd been the weakest beast tamer in Thousand Beast Valley. Now he stood over a Rank 4 corrupted beast, his body flickering between three different beast forms, his cultivation hovering at the peak of the third level of the Beast Melding Realm.

The lead disciple—a young woman with a jade token marking her as a member of the Azure Sky Sect—stepped forward. Her hands were trembling, but her voice held steady. "Who are you?"

"Lin Xiao. Valley's edge territory."

"The crippled one?" The words escaped before she could stop them, and her face flushed red. "I mean—I heard stories—"

"Everyone has," Lin said, releasing his merge. The shadow panther materialized beside him, its golden eyes watching the disciples with predatory interest. The lightning hawk landed on his shoulder, feathers still sparking. The wind-snake coiled around his waist, its broken core pulsing with borrowed power.

The disciples took a collective step backward.

"Relax," Lin said. "I don't bite. Usually."

The woman composed herself. "I am Mei Ling, junior disciple of the Azure Sky Sect. We were tracking this corrupted beast for three days. It killed two of my senior brothers." Her voice cracked. "If you hadn't arrived—"

"I know." Lin's eyes softened. "I felt your desperation through the bond. That's why I came."

Mei Ling's eyes widened. "You sensed us? From how far?"

"A mile or so. The shadow panther's domain sense extends that far."

She exchanged glances with her companions. "That's not possible. Domain sense at your cultivation level should be limited to a few hundred feet at most."

Lin shrugged. He was getting tired of explaining how his abilities worked. The truth was simple: [Beast Resonance] didn't just merge beast powers—it merged their senses, their instincts, their very souls. With three beasts bonded, his perception had expanded exponentially.

"We need to report this," one of the male disciples said. "The sect master needs to know there's a cultivator in the valley who can solo a Rank 4 corrupted beast."

"No." Mei Ling held up her hand. "We don't report this."

"What? Mei Ling, he saved our lives—"

"And if we report him, what happens?" Her voice dropped to a whisper. "The elders will try to recruit him. Or worse, contain him. You know how they treat anomalies."

Lin's interest sharpened. "Anomalies?"

Mei Ling turned to face him fully. "There are others like you. Cultivators who don't fit the normal progression. The sects call them anomalies. Some are recruited. Some..." She trailed off.

"Some what?"

"Disappear."

The word hung in the air like a blade. Lin's shadow panther growled low in its throat, sensing his sudden tension.

"Why are you telling me this?"

"Because you saved my life." Mei Ling reached into her robe and pulled out a small jade slip, cracked and ancient. "And because I think you need to see this."

She tossed it to him. Lin caught it, and the moment his fingers touched the jade, a flood of information poured into his mind.

*A map. Not of the valley as he knew it, but of something deeper. A network of underground chambers, connected by tunnels that spiderwebbed beneath the entire Thousand Beast Valley. At the center of the map, marked with a symbol he didn't recognize, was a massive chamber. The words "Fifth Seal" pulsed in crimson light.*

"What is this?" Lin breathed.

"The reason the corruption is spreading." Mei Ling's face was pale. "The elders know about it. They've known for years. The corruption isn't natural—it's being released. Deliberately. Someone is breaking the seals beneath the valley, and the sects are letting it happen."

"That's insane. Why would they—"

"To cull the weak."

The words hit Lin like a physical blow.

"The valley produces too many beast tamers," Mei Ling continued, her voice bitter. "Too many mouths to feed, too many cultivators competing for limited resources. The elders decided that only the strong should survive. So they opened the seals, let the corruption seep up from the depths. The weak die, the strong rise. It's been happening for three generations."

Lin's hands clenched into fists. The wind-snake hissed, sensing his rage. "You're telling me the entire valley has been a slaughterhouse this whole time?"

"Not the entire valley. Just the outer regions. The places where the poor train, where the crippled bonds are formed." She looked at him with something like pity. "Places like where you grew up."

The lightning hawk screeched, and Lin felt its fury echo through their bond. He understood now. The strange attacks, the escalating corruption, the way the major sects never seemed to suffer losses—it all made terrible, perfect sense.

"Why are you telling me this?" Lin asked again. "You're Azure Sky Sect. You benefit from this system."

"Because my sister was culled." Mei Ling's eyes glistened. "She bonded with a crippled beast, like you. They sent her into the deep marshes during a corruption surge. Said it was a training exercise. She never came back."

Lin studied her face, searching for deception. He found none. Just grief, old and deep, polished smooth by years of suppression.

"If what you say is true," he said slowly, "then stopping the corruption means finding whoever's breaking the seals."

"There are seven seals total. Five have already been broken. The sixth is under attack as we speak." Mei Ling pointed to the jade slip. "That map shows the entrance to the seal network. It's hidden beneath the Executioner's Ridge."

"Executioner's Ridge?" Lin's blood ran cold. That place was a death trap. The corrupted beasts there were said to be Rank 5 or higher, creatures that even sect elders avoided.

"I know it's dangerous." Mei Ling met his eyes. "But you're an anomaly. You can do things other cultivators can't. If anyone can reach the sixth seal before it's broken, it's you."

"And if I go there, what do I do?"

"Stop whoever's breaking the seals. Kill them if you have to." Her voice hardened. "Then seal the breach yourself. The method is in the jade slip."

Lin looked down at the ancient jade in his hands. Three beasts bonded. A conspiracy that reached the highest levels of power. A mission that would almost certainly kill him.

He smiled.

"Alright. I'll do it."

Mei Ling blinked. "Just like that?"

"Just like that." Lin activated [Beast Resonance], and the shadow panther melted into his form, followed by the lightning hawk, followed by the wind-snake. His body began to shift, taking on aspects of all three creatures. "I didn't bond with three beasts to sit around and watch people die. If the elders want a culling, they're going to get one—but not the way they expect."

He turned to leave, but Mei Ling called out. "Wait. There's something else you should know."

Lin paused.

"The person breaking the seals." Mei Ling's voice was barely audible. "Three years ago, my sister sent me a message before she died. She said she saw the destroyer's face. She described him perfectly."

"And?"

"It was a beast tamer. A powerful one." Mei Ling swallowed hard. "He had bonded with a crippled wind-snake. Just like you."

The world tilted.

Lin's merge destabilized, and the beasts separated from his body, landing around him in a defensive formation. His mind raced. A beast tamer with a crippled wind-snake? That was impossible. He was the only one who had ever bonded with such a beast. Everyone said so.

"You're lying."

"I wish I was." Mei Ling's eyes held nothing but truth. "My sister said the destroyer called himself the First Anomaly. He escaped the valley ten years ago. He's been breaking the seals ever since, trying to release something from the deepest chamber. Something called the Primordial Beast."

The Primordial Beast. The legend that every child in the valley grew up hearing. The first spirit beast, the one from which all others were descended. Imprisoned beneath the valley a thousand years ago by the original beast tamers.

If it was released—

"The map," Lin said, his voice urgent. "Can it show me where the First Anomaly is now?"

Mei Ling nodded. "Focus on the jade. It will—"

A scream tore through the swamp. Not human. Something deeper, more primal. It came from the direction of Executioner's Ridge, and it shook the ground beneath their feet.

"The sixth seal," Mei Ling whispered. "It's already broken."

Lin grabbed the jade slip and ran. His beasts followed, merging with him mid-stride. The shadow panther's speed, the lightning hawk's agility, the wind-snake's flexibility—all of it combined as he blurred through the swamp, leaving Mei Ling and her disciples behind.

The trees whipped past. The ground shook again, harder this time. Cracks appeared in the earth, black qi seeping out like blood from a wound.

*"Little tamer,"* the shadow panther's voice echoed in his mind, urgent and afraid. *"Something is waking up. Something old."*

*"I know."*

*"It will kill you."*

*"Probably."*

Lin pushed harder, his body straining against the limits of his cultivation. The jade slip pulsed with heat, the map growing clearer in his mind. He was close. Just a few more miles.

Then the sky turned black.

Not night-black. Something deeper. A darkness that swallowed light itself. The sun vanished, replaced by an endless void. Lin stumbled, his shadow panther senses screaming warnings from every direction.

A voice spoke. Not from the air. From inside his mind.

*"Little brother. I've been waiting for you."*

Lin froze. The voice was familiar. Too familiar. It was his own voice, but older, colder, touched by something ancient and terrible.

*"You found my map. You followed my trail. Just as I knew you would."*

"You're the First Anomaly," Lin said, his voice shaking despite his efforts to control it.

*"I am what you will become. Given time. Given power. Given the truth."*

"I will never become a monster."

*"Monster?"* The voice laughed, and the sound shattered the remaining light. *"Brother, you don't understand. I'm not the monster. I'm the cure. The Primordial Beast isn't a prisoner. It's a guardian. And the seals aren't meant to hold it in."*

A pause. The darkness pressed closer.

*"They're meant to hold something else out."*

Lin's blood ran cold. "What are you talking about?"

The voice didn't answer. Instead, the ground beneath Lin's feet opened, and he fell into darkness.

---

He landed hard, the impact driving the breath from his lungs. When he opened his eyes, he was in a vast underground chamber. Ancient runes covered every surface, pulsing with fading light. And in the center of the chamber stood a figure.

A man. His back to Lin. A crippled wind-snake coiled around his waist—identical to Lin's own beast.

The man turned.

Lin's heart stopped.

The face staring back at him was his own

title: Chapter 12
content:
CHAPTER 12: The Corrupted Heart

Lin's body sang with power as he moved through the corrupted beast's attack. The triple merge had transformed him into something else entirely—a creature of shadow and lightning and venom, all wrapped in human form.

The beast's claw passed through where he'd been standing a moment before, and Lin appeared at its flank, driving a lightning-coated fist into its ribs. The impact sent cracks spiderwebbing through the corrupted flesh, but the creature barely flinched.

*"It doesn't feel pain,"* the shadow panther's voice whispered in his mind. *"The corruption has burned away its nerves."*

*"Then we'll have to destroy it completely."*

Lin leaped backward as the beast spun, its tail—a mass of bone spikes fused together—whistling through the air where his head had been. The disciples screamed and scrambled further up the rock formation, their own beasts cowering behind them.

"Get to higher ground!" Lin shouted. "Don't let it corner you!"

The lightning hawk screeched overhead, diving in a blaze of electrical fury. Its talons raked across the beast's face, carving deep furrows that oozed black pus. The creature howled, but the sound was wrong—garbled, like a dying animal trying to imitate a roar.

*"The corruption has reached its vocal cords,"* the wind-snake observed, its voice calm despite the chaos. *"This creature has been suffering for a long time."*

Lin's chest tightened. He'd heard about corruption spreading through the valley, but this was the first time he'd seen it up close. The beast before him wasn't evil—it was a victim. A powerful beast that had been twisted into a mindless weapon.

"End it quickly," Lin muttered, more to himself than his beasts. "Give it peace."

He pushed more power into the merge. The shadow panther's essence wrapped around him like a second skin, the lightning hawk's electricity crackled through his veins, and the wind-snake's venom pooled in his palms. He became a blur of motion, striking again and again—each hit precise, each movement calculated.

The corrupted beast couldn't keep up. Its movements grew slower, more erratic. Black qi leaked from its wounds in thick streams, poisoning the ground beneath it. The swamp water hissed and boiled where the corruption touched it.

Lin saw his opening.

He dove forward, sliding between the beast's legs, and drove both hands upward into its chest. Venom and lightning exploded outward, punching through the corrupted flesh and out the beast's back. The creature froze, its red eyes flickering once, twice—

And then it collapsed, the ground shaking with the impact.

Lin stood over the fallen beast, breathing hard. The merge began to dissolve, his three beasts separating from him and materializing in their physical forms. The shadow panther landed silently beside him, the lightning hawk perched on a nearby tree branch, and the wind-snake coiled around his waist.

"Are you all right?" one of the disciples called down, her voice trembling.

Lin nodded, but his eyes were fixed on the corpse. Something was wrong. The black qi that had been leaking from the beast's wounds wasn't dissipating—it was gathering, coalescing into a single point above the body.

"Get back!" Lin shouted, grabbing the wind-snake and throwing himself to the side.

The black qi condensed into a sphere, then shot upward into the sky like a beacon. It hung there for a moment, pulsing with malevolent light, before streaking toward the heart of the valley.

"What was that?" the disciple asked, her face pale.

Lin's blood ran cold. He'd seen that before. In the temple. In the murals depicting the valley's history.

"That was a signal," he said slowly. "The corruption isn't random. It's being controlled."

The disciples exchanged terrified glances. "Controlled by what?"

Before Lin could answer, the ground began to tremble. Deep rumbles echoed from beneath the swamp, and the water around them started to churn. Bubbles rose to the surface, popping with foul-smelling gas.

"Something's coming," the shadow panther growled, its fur bristling.

The tremors grew stronger. The rock formation behind the disciples cracked, sending them scrambling for stable ground. Lin grabbed two of them and pulled them clear just as the entire formation collapsed into the churning water.

Then the swamp split open.

A massive pit appeared where the rock formation had been, revealing a network of tunnels carved into the earth below. From those tunnels came a sound that made Lin's soul shudder—a chorus of screams, both human and beast, twisted together in agony.

"Corrupted," the wind-snake hissed. "Hundreds of them."

Lin peered into the darkness and felt his heart stop. The tunnels were filled with glowing red eyes. Dozens. Hundreds. All of them staring upward, waiting.

"No," he whispered. "This can't be..."

A figure emerged from the largest tunnel. It walked on two legs, but its body was a nightmare of fused flesh—beast parts stitched together with black qi, forming something that was almost human, but not quite. Its face was a mask of agony, and its eyes burned with intelligence.

"Lin Xiao," the figure said, its voice a thousand screams layered together. "I've been waiting for you."

Lin's blood turned to ice. "Who are you?"

The figure smiled, its mouth splitting open too wide, revealing rows of jagged teeth. "I am what happens when beast tamers play god. I am the consequence of their arrogance." It spread its arms, gesturing to the army of corrupted beasts behind it. "And I am here to claim what is mine."

"I don't understand," Lin said, positioning himself between the figure and the disciples. "What do you want?"

"You." The figure's smile widened. "Or more specifically, the power inside you. [Beast Resonance] is not a gift, Lin Xiao. It's a key. A key to unlock the prison that holds the true master of this valley."

The shadow panther stepped forward, growling. *"Lies. The temple showed us the truth."*

"The temple showed you what it wanted you to see," the figure replied. "Did you never wonder why the valley produces so many spirit beasts? Why the qi here is so rich? Why the corruption started spreading just as you began to awaken your power?"

Lin's mind raced. The timing. The spread of corruption. His sudden ability to bond with multiple beasts. It couldn't be a coincidence.

"You're saying I caused this?"

"Not caused. Enabled." The figure took a step closer, and Lin saw that its feet weren't feet at all—they were roots, burrowing into the earth. "The prison has been weakening for centuries. But your resonance—your ability to merge beast souls—it resonates with the prison itself. Every time you merge, you send ripples through the barrier. And now..." It laughed, a horrible, wet sound. "Now, it's ready to break."

Lin's hands trembled. He looked at his three beasts—the panther, the hawk, the snake. They were staring at him with trust, with loyalty. They believed in him.

But what if they shouldn't?

"I can stop," Lin said. "I won't merge again. I'll seal my power."

"Too late." The figure raised its hand, and the army of corrupted beasts began to climb. "The process has already begun. In three days, the prison will shatter. And when it does, the true ruler of Thousand Beast Valley will be reborn."

It turned, disappearing back into the tunnel. The corrupted beasts surged forward, swarming out of the pit like a tide of nightmares.

Lin didn't have time to think. He grabbed the disciples, pulling them into a run. "Move! Now!"

They fled through the swamp, the corrupted army at their heels. Lin's beasts fought to cover their retreat, but there were too many. The lightning hawk unleashed arcs of electricity that felled dozens, but more just kept coming. The shadow panther moved like a ghost, cutting down corrupted after corrupted, but they didn't stop. The wind-snake's venom slowed them, but it couldn't stop them.

They reached a clearing, and Lin saw it—a formation of stone pillars, ancient and weathered, arranged in a circle. The same symbol he'd seen in the temple.

"The prison," he breathed.

The corrupted army halted at the edge of the clearing, as if afraid to approach. Lin turned, and saw the figure standing at the back of the army, watching him with those terrible, intelligent eyes.

"Three days," the figure called out. "Find a way to stop it, Lin Xiao. Or watch everything you love be consumed."

The army retreated, sinking back into the swamp like shadows at dawn. The figure lingered a moment longer, then bowed mockingly and vanished.

Lin collapsed to his knees, his body shaking. The disciples gathered around him, their faces a mix of fear and hope.

"What do we do?" one of them asked.

Lin looked at his hands. The hands that had bonded with beasts. The hands that held the power of resonance. The hands that might have doomed them all.

"I don't know," he admitted.

The shadow panther pressed its head against his shoulder. *"Then we find out. Together."*

Lin closed his eyes, feeling the bond with his beasts pulse like a second heartbeat. He had three days. Three days to find a way to stop a prison from breaking. Three days to save the valley.

Or three days to become the villain of his own story.

A new voice echoed from the edge of the clearing—an old man's voice, cracked with age but carrying authority. "So you've finally arrived, boy."

Lin looked up. An ancient beast tamer stood at the base of one of the stone pillars, his robes tattered, his eyes milky white with blindness. But his presence... it radiated power unlike anything Lin had ever felt.

"You've been expecting me?" Lin asked.

The old man smiled, revealing gaps where teeth should have been. "I've been waiting for you for three hundred years. The prison's keeper. The last survivor of the sect that built this valley." He extended a gnarled hand. "And the only one who knows how to stop what's coming."

Lin rose to his feet, his beasts flanking him. "Then teach me."

The old man's smile faded. "First, you need to understand the truth. The real truth. About the valley, about the beasts, and about the power that sleeps beneath our feet."

He turned and began walking toward the stone pillars. "Come. We have much to discuss, and very little time."

Lin followed, his three beasts close behind. Behind them, the swamp fell silent, the corrupted army gone as if it had never been.

But in the distance, deep beneath the earth, something stirred. Something ancient. Something hungry.

And in three days, it would break free.

title: Chapter 13
content:
CHAPTER 13: The Price of Resonance

The corrupted beast's head hit the mud with a wet thud, and Lin staggered back, gasping. The triple merge's power faded from his limbs like receding tide, leaving behind a hollow ache that went deeper than muscle.

"You saved us," one of the disciples whispered, her robes torn and stained with swamp water. She couldn't have been more than sixteen, her face pale beneath the grime. "That thing... it killed three of my brothers before you came."

Lin forced a nod, his throat tight. *Three disciples. Dead.* The corruption was spreading faster than anyone in the valley wanted to admit.

"We need to move," the shadow panther's voice cut through his thoughts. Its form rippled beside him, solidifying from darkness. *"The corruption attracts more corruption. Where one fell, others will come."*

The hunting party scrambled to gather their dead. Lin watched them, his new senses extended far beyond what should have been possible. He could feel the swamp breathing around him—the pulse of insects, the whisper of roots, the distant, thrumming heartbeat of something vast and ancient stirring beneath the earth.

*What is that?*

He pushed his perception deeper, riding the bond of his three beasts. The wind-snake's sensitivity to air currents, the lightning hawk's awareness of electromagnetic fields, the shadow panther's connection to the spaces between light—all of it merged into a single, overwhelming tapestry of sensation.

And there, at the very edge of his awareness, he felt it.

A pulse. Rhythmic. Deliberate.

Like a heartbeat coming from the center of the valley.

"Little tamer," the shadow panther growled, its ears flattening. *"I feel it too. Something is wrong."*

---

They made camp on a rocky outcropping an hour later, far from the corruption site. The surviving disciples—five in total—huddled together, their eyes darting at every sound. Lin sat apart, the lightning hawk perched on his shoulder, the wind-snake coiled around his wrist.

"You're that Lin Xiao," one of the disciples said. He was older, maybe twenty, with the bearing of someone used to command. His robe marked him as a core disciple of the Jade Serpent Sect. "The cripple who bonded a broken snake."

"Was," Lin said quietly. "I'm not that anymore."

The disciple's eyes flicked to the shadow panther, still half-visible in the darkness. "So I see. Three beasts. That's... rare."

"Rare" was an understatement. Most beast tamers never bonded more than one beast in their lifetime. Two was exceptional. Three was the stuff of legends.

"I'm Yu Ming," the disciple said, extending his hand. "Thank you. Truly. We would have died back there."

Lin took his hand, but before he could respond, the ground trembled.

Everyone froze.

The tremor lasted three seconds, then stopped. Silence descended, heavy and oppressive. Even the insects had gone quiet.

"What was that?" one of the younger disciples whispered.

Lin's beasts stirred uneasily. The wind-snake hissed, its tongue flickering. The lightning hawk's feathers crackled with nervous energy. The shadow panther had vanished entirely, its presence a tense coil in Lin's mind.

*"Something is coming,"* the panther said. *"From below."*

The ground exploded.

---

Lin threw himself sideways, rolling as a massive clawed hand burst through the stone where he'd been standing. The creature that emerged was like nothing he'd ever seen—humanoid, but twisted, its skin a patchwork of scales, fur, and cracked chitin. Its eyes were pits of molten gold, and its mouth split into a grin that showed too many teeth.

Not corrupted. Something else.

*"A chimera,"* the shadow panther snarled, materializing behind the creature. *"Someone has been playing god."*

Chimeras. Beasts fused together through forbidden alchemy, their souls crushed into a single, unstable vessel. The practice had been outlawed for centuries, punished by death in every sect across the continent.

But here it was, standing in front of Lin, reeking of blood and rage.

"Run!" Lin shouted at the disciples. "Get to the valley's edge. Don't stop!"

Yu Ming hesitated, but one look at the chimera's golden eyes sent him scrambling. The disciples fled, leaving Lin alone with the monster.

*"Smart,"* the chimera spoke. Its voice was a chorus of discordant tones, the echoes of a dozen dying beasts screaming in unison. *"They know when to retreat. You should learn from them."*

Lin didn't answer. He reached deep into his core, feeling the three bonds thrum with readiness. The wind-snake coiled tighter. The lightning hawk spread its wings. The shadow panther circled, waiting for an opening.

*[Beast Resonance: Triple Merge]*

Power flooded his veins, and he moved.

His fist connected with the chimera's chest, lightning and shadow exploding on impact. The creature staggered back, a gash opening across its torso, but instead of blood, black smoke poured from the wound. It laughed, the sound like grinding bones.

"Impressive," it said. "But you're using borrowed power. Do you even know what that costs?"

Lin struck again, faster this time, his movements blurring between the panther's stealth and the hawk's speed. He landed three hits, four, five—each one leaving deeper wounds. The chimera swayed, its laughter fading.

And then it spoke a single word that made Lin's blood run cold.

"Mother."

The ground split open.

From the chasm rose something vast. Something ancient. Something that had been sleeping beneath the valley for longer than human memory.

A titan. A beast so old that its body had become part of the earth itself, roots and stone woven into its flesh. Its eyes opened—three of them, arranged in a triangle across its face—and they fixed on Lin with terrible recognition.

*"Son of Resonance,"* the titan rumbled. Its voice shook the air, rattled Lin's bones, echoed through his very soul. *"We have been waiting for you."*

The chimera bowed, its golden eyes gleaming with triumph. "The prophecy was true. The one who can merge with beasts has finally appeared."

Lin's mind raced. Prophecy? Waiting for him? None of this made sense. He was a cripple from a backwater valley. He was nobody.

"You're wrong," he said, his voice steady despite the fear clawing at his chest. "I'm not anyone's answer."

The titan's three eyes narrowed. *"You are the key, Lin Xiao. The key to unlocking what has been sealed. The key to awakening the true heart of Thousand Beast Valley."*

"I don't know what you're talking about."

*"No,"* the titan agreed. *"But you will."*

It reached for him, and despite the triple merge's power, despite the strength of three beasts flowing through his veins, Lin couldn't move. The titan's presence was absolute, pressing down on him like a mountain, crushing his will, his resistance, his hope.

*"The resonance within you is not a gift,"* the titan said, its massive hand closing around Lin. *"It is a debt. One that must be repaid."*

Darkness claimed him.

---

Lin woke to the sound of dripping water.

He was lying on cold stone, his body aching from the triple merge's backlash. The bonds with his beasts felt distant, muted, as if something was blocking them. He forced his eyes open and found himself in a cavern lit by pale blue crystals embedded in the walls.

"You're awake."

The voice came from everywhere and nowhere. Lin sat up, his hand instinctively reaching for his beasts. The wind-snake stirred weakly at his wrist. The lightning hawk was somewhere above, its presence faint but alive. The shadow panther was gone—no, not gone, but suppressed, forced into a corner of his consciousness.

*"She's blocking us,"* the panther growled, its mental voice strained. *"The titan. She's powerful, little tamer. More powerful than anything I've ever felt."*

"Who are you?" Lin called out. "What do you want?"

A figure emerged from the shadows. A woman, tall and regal, her skin the color of moonlight, her hair a cascade of silver that seemed to flow like water. Her eyes were the same molten gold as the chimera's.

"I am what remains of the valley's first beast tamer," she said. "And you, Lin Xiao, carry the resonance that was stolen from me three thousand years ago."

Lin's breath caught. Three thousand years. That was older than any recorded history in the continent.

"I don't understand."

The woman smiled, and it was a cold, terrible thing. "You will. But first, I need to show you what happens when a beast tamer refuses to pay the price of their power."

She raised her hand, and the cavern walls dissolved into images. Lin saw a city—no, a sect—burning. He saw beast tamers falling, their bonded beasts turning on them, fangs and claws tearing through flesh. He saw a mountain of corpses, human and beast alike, piled so high it blotted out the sun.

"This," the woman said, "is what your future looks like. Unless you accept what you truly are."

"And what is that?" Lin whispered.

Her golden eyes met his, and in them, he saw something ancient and ravenous. Something that had been waiting for him since before he was born.

"A vessel," she said. "For the one who created the resonance in the first place. For me."

The ground shook. The crystals on the walls flared with blinding light. And Lin felt something ancient stirring in the deepest part of his soul—something that had always been there, waiting, watching.

*No,* he thought, struggling against it. *I am my own person. I chose my beasts. I earned my power.*

*But did you?* a voice whispered back—a voice that was not his own, not the panther's, not the hawk's, not the snake's. *Or was it all given to you so I could take it back?*

The woman laughed, and the sound was the death rattle of a thousand souls.

"Welcome home, Lin Xiao," she said. "The resonance has finally returned to its true master."

The bonds with his beasts shattered.

title: The Price of Fusion
content:
Lin Xiao's fist connected with the corrupted beast's flank, and the impact sent shockwaves through his entire body. Shadows wrapped around his arm, amplifying the strike, but something felt *wrong*. The beast's flesh didn't yield like normal corrupted tissue—it *pulsed*, alive with a rhythm that matched his own heartbeat.

*Since when do corrupted beasts have heartbeats?*

The creature spun, its massive claw swiping through the air where Lin had been standing a second ago. He was already gone, melting into the shadows cast by the rock formation, but the question lingered like poison in his mind.

"Keep it pinned!" one of the disciples shouted—a girl with a fire-wolf at her side, its flames flickering weakly in the swamp mist.

Lin reappeared atop a boulder, assessing the situation. Three disciples, all from the Azure Flame Sect. Their robes were torn, their beasts exhausted. The fire-wolf had a deep gash along its flank. A stone-armored tortoise lay on its back, legs twitching. The third disciple held a broken staff, his spirit beast—a wind-sparrow—lying dead at his feet.

*They're not going to survive this.*

The corrupted beast turned its burning gaze toward Lin. Black qi dripped from its maw, sizzling where it hit the damp ground. But it was the creature's *eyes* that made Lin's blood run cold. They weren't mindless. They were *calculating*.

"You," the panther's voice whispered in his mind, sharp with warning. "This one is different. It *remembers*."

"Remembers what?" Lin asked, but the panther didn't answer.

The beast charged.

Lin raised his hand, calling on the lightning hawk. Electricity surged through the air, arcing toward the creature—but it *dodged*. Not a clumsy dodge, but a precise, practiced movement, as if it had fought lightning hawks before.

*That's impossible.*

The wind-snake lashed out, venom spraying, but the beast's corrupted hide absorbed the poison like water into sand. It swung its tail, catching Lin across the chest and sending him crashing into the rock formation. Stone cracked. Blood filled his mouth.

"Brother Lin!" the fire-wolf disciple screamed.

Lin pushed himself up, his ribs screaming in protest. The shadow panther materialized beside him, its form flickering with agitation.
